records.source_text: परमेव ब्रह्मेह मनोमयत्वादिभिर्धर्मैरुपास्यम् । कुतः ? सर्वत्र प्रसिद्धोपदेशात् । यत्सर्वेषु वेदान्तेषु प्रसिद्धं ब्रह्मशब्दस्यालम्बनं जगत्कारणम् , इह च ‘ सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म ’ इति वाक्योपक्रमे श्रुतम् , तदेव मनोमयत्वादिधर्मैर्विशिष्टमुपदिश्यत इति युक्तम् । एवं च सति प्रकृतहानाप्रकृतप्रक्रिये न भविष्यतः । ननु वाक्योपक्रमे शमविधिविवक्षया ब्रह्म निर्दिष्टं न स्वविवक्षयेत्युक्तम् ; अत्रोच्यते — यद्यपि शमविधिविवक्षया ब्रह्म निर्दिष्टम् , तथापि मनोमयत्वादिषूपदिश्यमानेषु तदेव ब्रह्म सन्निहितं भवति , जीवस्तु न सन्निहितः , न च स्वशब्देनोपात्त इति वैषम्यम् ॥ १ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: parameva brahmeha manomayatvādibhirdharmairupāsyam | kutaḥ ? sarvatra prasiddhopadeśāt | yatsarveṣu vedānteṣu prasiddhaṃ brahmaśabdasyālambanaṃ jagatkāraṇam , iha ca ‘ sarvaṃ khalvidaṃ brahma ’ iti vākyopakrame śrutam , tadeva manomayatvādidharmairviśiṣṭamupadiśyata iti yuktam | evaṃ ca sati prakṛtahānāprakṛtaprakriye na bhaviṣyataḥ | nanu vākyopakrame śamavidhivivakṣayā brahma nirdiṣṭaṃ na svavivakṣayetyuktam ; atrocyate — yadyapi śamavidhivivakṣayā brahma nirdiṣṭam , tathāpi manomayatvādiṣūpadiśyamāneṣu tadeva brahma sannihitaṃ bhavati , jīvastu na sannihitaḥ , na ca svaśabdenopātta iti vaiṣamyam || 1 ||
records.transliteration: parameva brahmeha manomayatvādibhirdharmairupāsyam | kutaḥ ? sarvatra prasiddhopadeśāt | yatsarveṣu vedānteṣu prasiddhaṃ brahmaśabdasyālambanaṃ jagatkāraṇam , iha ca ‘ sarvaṃ khalvidaṃ brahma ’ iti vākyopakrame śrutam , tadeva manomayatvādidharmairviśiṣṭamupadiśyata iti yuktam | evaṃ ca sati prakṛtahānāprakṛtaprakriye na bhaviṣyataḥ | nanu vākyopakrame śamavidhivivakṣayā brahma nirdiṣṭaṃ na svavivakṣayetyuktam ; atrocyate — yadyapi śamavidhivivakṣayā brahma nirdiṣṭam , tathāpi manomayatvādiṣūpadiśyamāneṣu tadeva brahma sannihitaṃ bhavati , jīvastu na sannihitaḥ , na ca svaśabdenopātta iti vaiṣamyam || 1 ||
records.literal_translation: It is the supreme Brahman alone that is to be worshiped here through attributes such as mind-formedness. Why? Because of instruction of what is universally known. That which in all Vedanta is known as the referent of the word Brahman, the cause of the world, is heard here at the beginning of the passage as 'all this indeed is Brahman'; that very thing is taught as qualified by mind-formedness and the rest - this is proper. Thus there is no abandonment of the topic and no shift to an untopical procedure. If one says Brahman was indicated only with intent to enjoin tranquility at the opening and not for its own sake, we reply: even if indicated for shama-vidhi, still when mind-formedness etc. are taught, that very nearby Brahman is available; jiva is neither nearby nor directly introduced by its own term.
records.technical_translation: Siddhanta: the upasya is para-brahman, not the embodied individual. The hetu is sarvatra-prasiddhopadesa: the referent already established across Vedanta as brahma-sabda-vacya and jagat-karana is explicitly introduced at passage opening ('sarvam khalv idam brahma'). Therefore subsequent qualifiers (mano-maya etc.) are to be read as vishesana of that same prakrta Brahman, avoiding prakrta-hana and aprakrta-prakriya (topic break and forced shift). Even granting that the opening sentence functionally supports shama-vidhi, textual proximity and explicit naming still privilege Brahman as the continuing referent; jiva lacks equivalent immediate textual establishment.

records.source_text: वक्तुमिष्टा विवक्षिताः । यद्यप्यपौरुषेये वेदे वक्तुरभावात् नेच्छार्थः सम्भवति , तथाप्युपादानेन फलेनोपचर्यते । लोके हि यच्छब्दाभिहितमुपादेयं भवति तद्विवक्षितमित्युच्यते , यदनुपादेयं तदविवक्षितमिति । तद्वद्वेदेऽप्युपादेयत्वेनाभिहितं विवक्षितं भवति , इतरदविवक्षितम् । उपादानानुपादाने तु वेदवाक्यतात्पर्यातात्पर्याभ्यामवगम्येते । तदिह ये विवक्षिता गुणा उपासनायामुपादेयत्वेनोपदिष्टाः सत्यसङ्कल्पप्रभृतयः , ते परस्मिन्ब्रह्मण्युपपद्यन्ते । सत्यसङ्कल्पत्वं हि सृष्टिस्थितिसंहारेष्वप्रतिबद्धशक्तित्वात्परमात्मन एवावकल्पते । परमात्मगुणत्वेन च ‘ य आत्मापहतपाप्मा ’ (छा. उ. ८ । ७ । १) इत्यत्र ‘ सत्यकामः सत्यसङ्कल्पः ’ इति श्रुतम् , ‘ आकाशात्मा ’ इति च आकाशवदात्मा अस्येत्यर्थः । सर्वगतत्वादिभिर्धर्मैः सम्भवत्याकाशेन साम्यं ब्रह्मणः । ‘ ज्यायान्पृथिव्याः ’ इत्यादिना चैतदेव दर्शयति । यदापि आकाश आत्मा यस्येति व्याख्यायते , तदापि सम्भवति सर्वजगत्कारणस्य सर्वात्मनो ब्रह्मण आकाशात्मत्वम् । अत एव ‘ सर्वकर्मा ’ इत्यादि । एवमिहोपास्यतया विवक्षिता गुणा ब्रह्मण्युपपद्यन्ते । यत्तूक्तम् — ‘ मनोमयः प्राणशरीरः ’ इति जीवलिङ्गम् , न तद्ब्रह्मण्युपपद्यत इति ; तदपि ब्रह्मण्युपपद्यत इति ब्रूमः । सर्वात्मत्वाद्धि ब्रह्मणो जीवसम्बन्धीनि मनोमयत्वादीनि ब्रह्मसम्बन्धीनि भवन्ति । तथा च ब्रह्मविषये श्रुतिस्मृती भवतः — ‘ त्वं स्त्री त्वं पुमानसि त्वं कुमार उत वा कुमारी । त्वं जीर्णो दण्डेन वञ्चसि त्वं जातो भवसि विश्वतोमुखः ’ (श्वे. उ. ४ । ३) इति ; ‘ सर्वतःपाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् । सर्वतःश्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ’ (भ. गी. १३ । १३) इति च । ‘ अप्राणो ह्यमनाः शुभ्रः ’ इति श्रुतिः शुद्धब्रह्मविषया , इयं तु श्रुतिः ‘ मनोमयः प्राणशरीरः ’ इति सगुणब्रह्मविषयेति विशेषः । अतो विवक्षितगुणोपपत्तेः परमेव ब्रह्म इहोपास्यत्वेनोपदिष्टमिति गम्यते ॥ २ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: vaktumiṣṭā vivakṣitāḥ | yadyapyapauruṣeye vede vakturabhāvāt necchārthaḥ sambhavati , tathāpyupādānena phalenopacaryate | loke hi yacchabdābhihitamupādeyaṃ bhavati tadvivakṣitamityucyate , yadanupādeyaṃ tadavivakṣitamiti | tadvadvede'pyupādeyatvenābhihitaṃ vivakṣitaṃ bhavati , itaradavivakṣitam | upādānānupādāne tu vedavākyatātparyātātparyābhyāmavagamyete | tadiha ye vivakṣitā guṇā upāsanāyāmupādeyatvenopadiṣṭāḥ satyasaṅkalpaprabhṛtayaḥ , te parasminbrahmaṇyupapadyante | satyasaṅkalpatvaṃ hi sṛṣṭisthitisaṃhāreṣvapratibaddhaśaktitvātparamātmana evāvakalpate | paramātmaguṇatvena ca ‘ ya ātmāpahatapāpmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 7 | 1) ityatra ‘ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṅkalpaḥ ’ iti śrutam , ‘ ākāśātmā ’ iti ca ākāśavadātmā asyetyarthaḥ | sarvagatatvādibhirdharmaiḥ sambhavatyākāśena sāmyaṃ brahmaṇaḥ | ‘ jyāyānpṛthivyāḥ ’ ityādinā caitadeva darśayati | yadāpi ākāśa ātmā yasyeti vyākhyāyate , tadāpi sambhavati sarvajagatkāraṇasya sarvātmano brahmaṇa ākāśātmatvam | ata eva ‘ sarvakarmā ’ ityādi | evamihopāsyatayā vivakṣitā guṇā brahmaṇyupapadyante | yattūktam — ‘ manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīraḥ ’ iti jīvaliṅgam , na tadbrahmaṇyupapadyata iti ; tadapi brahmaṇyupapadyata iti brūmaḥ | sarvātmatvāddhi brahmaṇo jīvasambandhīni manomayatvādīni brahmasambandhīni bhavanti | tathā ca brahmaviṣaye śrutismṛtī bhavataḥ — ‘ tvaṃ strī tvaṃ pumānasi tvaṃ kumāra uta vā kumārī | tvaṃ jīrṇo daṇḍena vañcasi tvaṃ jāto bhavasi viśvatomukhaḥ ’ (śve. u. 4 | 3) iti ; ‘ sarvataḥpāṇipādaṃ tatsarvato'kṣiśiromukham | sarvataḥśrutimalloke sarvamāvṛtya tiṣṭhati ’ (bha. gī. 13 | 13) iti ca | ‘ aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhraḥ ’ iti śrutiḥ śuddhabrahmaviṣayā , iyaṃ tu śrutiḥ ‘ manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīraḥ ’ iti saguṇabrahmaviṣayeti viśeṣaḥ | ato vivakṣitaguṇopapatteḥ parameva brahma ihopāsyatvenopadiṣṭamiti gamyate || 2 ||

records.literal_translation: 'Intended' means intended to be conveyed. Though in the authorless Veda no speaker-intent is possible in the ordinary sense, the term is used figuratively based on what is to be accepted as result. In common usage, what a word-expression presents as to-be-adopted is called intended; what is not to be adopted is unintended. So also in Veda: what is stated as adoptable is intended, the rest unintended. And adoption/non-adoption are known by the purport/non-purport of the Vedic sentence. Here, the attributes intended as upasana-relevant, such as true resolve, fit in the supreme Brahman. True-resolve belongs only to the supreme self, whose power in creation, maintenance, and dissolution is unobstructed. And as qualities of the supreme self these are heard elsewhere: 'that self free from evil ... whose desires are true, whose resolves are true,' and 'whose self is space.' 'Whose self is space' means whose nature is like space; likeness to space by all-pervasiveness etc. is possible for Brahman. Even if interpreted as 'for whom space is self,' that too fits Brahman as cause and self of all. Hence also 'doer of all actions' etc. Thus the qualities intended here for meditation fit Brahman. As for the objection that 'mind-formed, with prana as body' is a jiva-sign and does not fit Brahman, we say it too fits Brahman: because Brahman is the self of all, jiva-related predicates can belong to Brahman. So scripture and smriti say of Brahman: 'You are woman, you are man ...' and 'with hands and feet everywhere...' The text 'without prana, without mind, pure' concerns pure Brahman, while this text concerns qualified Brahman; that is the distinction. Therefore, because intended qualities are fitting, it is the supreme Brahman that is taught here as the object of worship. (2)
records.technical_translation: This block defines vivaksa functionally through upadeyata (what the passage normatively asks the meditator to take up), not through psychological author-intention. Hermeneutic criterion: vedavakya-tatparya identifies which predicates are operative in upasana. Predicates such as satya-sankalpatva, akasa-atmatva, and sarva-karmatva are shown to be coherent for saguna-brahman, especially given jagat-karanatva and sarvatmatva. Apparent jiva-lingas (mano-maya, prana-sarira) are re-read under total-self logic: predicates of individuated loci can be secondarily predicated of Brahman as universal self. Scriptural differentiation resolves conflict: aprana/amanas statements target nirguna-shuddha-brahman, while this context is saguna-upasana. Hence the sutra-ground is established: vivaksita-guna-upapatti confirms Brahman-reference, not jiva-reference.

records.source_text: पूर्वेण सूत्रेण ब्रह्मणि विवक्षितानां गुणानामुपपत्तिरुक्ता । अनेन शारीरे तेषामनुपपत्तिरुच्यते । तुशब्दोऽवधारणार्थः । ब्रह्मैवोक्तेन न्यायेन मनोमयत्वादिगुणम् ; न तु शारीरो जीवो मनोमयत्वादिगुणः ; यत्कारणम् — ‘ सत्यसङ्कल्पः ’ ‘ आकाशात्मा ’ ‘ अवाकी ’ ‘ अनादरः ’ ‘ ज्यायान्पृथिव्याः ’ इति चैवंजातीयका गुणा न शारीरे आञ्जस्येनोपपद्यन्ते । शारीर इति शरीरे भव इत्यर्थः । नन्वीश्वरोऽपि शरीरे भवति । सत्यम् , शरीरे भवति ; न तु शरीर एव भवति ; ‘ ज्यायान्पृथिव्या ज्यायानन्तरिक्षात् ’ ‘ आकाशवत्सर्वगतश्च नित्यः ’ (शत. ब्रा. १० । ६ । ३ । २) इति च व्यापित्वश्रवणात् । जीवस्तु शरीर एव भवति , तस्य भोगाधिष्ठानाच्छरीरादन्यत्र वृत्त्यभावात् ॥ ३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: pūrveṇa sūtreṇa brahmaṇi vivakṣitānāṃ guṇānāmupapattiruktā | anena śārīre teṣāmanupapattirucyate | tuśabdo'vadhāraṇārthaḥ | brahmaivoktena nyāyena manomayatvādiguṇam ; na tu śārīro jīvo manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yatkāraṇam — ‘ satyasaṅkalpaḥ ’ ‘ ākāśātmā ’ ‘ avākī ’ ‘ anādaraḥ ’ ‘ jyāyānpṛthivyāḥ ’ iti caivaṃjātīyakā guṇā na śārīre āñjasyenopapadyante | śārīra iti śarīre bhava ityarthaḥ | nanvīśvaro'pi śarīre bhavati | satyam , śarīre bhavati ; na tu śarīra eva bhavati ; ‘ jyāyānpṛthivyā jyāyānantarikṣāt ’ ‘ ākāśavatsarvagataśca nityaḥ ’ (śata. brā. 10 | 6 | 3 | 2) iti ca vyāpitvaśravaṇāt | jīvastu śarīra eva bhavati , tasya bhogādhiṣṭhānāccharīrādanyatra vṛttyabhāvāt || 3 ||
records.transliteration: pūrveṇa sūtreṇa brahmaṇi vivakṣitānāṃ guṇānāmupapattiruktā | anena śārīre teṣāmanupapattirucyate | tuśabdo'vadhāraṇārthaḥ | brahmaivoktena nyāyena manomayatvādiguṇam ; na tu śārīro jīvo manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yatkāraṇam — ‘ satyasaṅkalpaḥ ’ ‘ ākāśātmā ’ ‘ avākī ’ ‘ anādaraḥ ’ ‘ jyāyānpṛthivyāḥ ’ iti caivaṃjātīyakā guṇā na śārīre āñjasyenopapadyante | śārīra iti śarīre bhava ityarthaḥ | nanvīśvaro'pi śarīre bhavati | satyam , śarīre bhavati ; na tu śarīra eva bhavati ; ‘ jyāyānpṛthivyā jyāyānantarikṣāt ’ ‘ ākāśavatsarvagataśca nityaḥ ’ (śata. brā. 10 | 6 | 3 | 2) iti ca vyāpitvaśravaṇāt | jīvastu śarīra eva bhavati , tasya bhogādhiṣṭhānāccharīrādanyatra vṛttyabhāvāt || 3 ||
records.literal_translation: The previous sutra established that the intended attributes fit Brahman. This sutra states their non-fit in the embodied individual. The particle tu is restrictive. By the prior reasoning, mind-made and related attributes belong to Brahman alone, not to the embodied jiva. Why? Because attributes such as 'whose will is true,' 'whose self is space,' 'speechless,' 'without dependence,' 'greater than earth,' and similar do not straightforwardly fit the embodied self. 'Embodied' means situated in body. Objection: Isvara too is in body. True, He is in body, but He is not body-bound as body itself, since scripture teaches His pervasiveness ('greater than earth, greater than mid-space,' 'all-pervading and eternal like space'). The jiva, however, is body-bound for experience and does not function apart from body in that mode. (3)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.3 denies sharira-jiva as referent of manomaya predicates; the listed mahat-gunas fit only the all-pervasive Brahman, not body-delimited experiencer-consciousness.

records.source_text: इतश्च न शारीरो मनोमयत्वादिगुणः ; यस्मात्कर्मकर्तृव्यपदेशो भवति — ‘ एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसम्भवितास्मि ’ (छा. उ. ३ । १४ । ४) इति । एतमिति प्रकृतं मनोमयत्वादिगुणमुपास्यमात्मानं कर्मत्वेन प्राप्यत्वेन व्यपदिशति ; अभिसम्भवितास्मीति शारीरमुपासकं कर्तृत्वेन प्रापकत्वेन । अभिसम्भवितास्मीति प्राप्तास्मीत्यर्थः । न च सत्यां गतावेकस्य कर्मकर्तृव्यपदेशो युक्तः । तथोपा स्यो पासकभावोऽपि भेदाधिष्ठान एव । तस्मादपि न शारीरो मनोमयत्वादिविशिष्टः ॥ ४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca na śārīro manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yasmātkarmakartṛvyapadeśo bhavati — ‘ etamitaḥ pretyābhisambhavitāsmi ’ (chā. u. 3 | 14 | 4) iti | etamiti prakṛtaṃ manomayatvādiguṇamupāsyamātmānaṃ karmatvena prāpyatvena vyapadiśati ; abhisambhavitāsmīti śārīramupāsakaṃ kartṛtvena prāpakatvena | abhisambhavitāsmīti prāptāsmītyarthaḥ | na ca satyāṃ gatāvekasya karmakartṛvyapadeśo yuktaḥ | tathopā syo pāsakabhāvo'pi bhedādhiṣṭhāna eva | tasmādapi na śārīro manomayatvādiviśiṣṭaḥ || 4 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca na śārīro manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yasmātkarmakartṛvyapadeśo bhavati — ‘ etamitaḥ pretyābhisambhavitāsmi ’ (chā. u. 3 | 14 | 4) iti | etamiti prakṛtaṃ manomayatvādiguṇamupāsyamātmānaṃ karmatvena prāpyatvena vyapadiśati ; abhisambhavitāsmīti śārīramupāsakaṃ kartṛtvena prāpakatvena | abhisambhavitāsmīti prāptāsmītyarthaḥ | na ca satyāṃ gatāvekasya karmakartṛvyapadeśo yuktaḥ | tathopā syo pāsakabhāvo'pi bhedādhiṣṭhāna eva | tasmādapi na śārīro manomayatvādiviśiṣṭaḥ || 4 ||
records.literal_translation: For this reason too, the mind-made qualifier and related attributes do not belong to the embodied self: there is designation of object and agent in the statement 'having departed from here, I shall attain Him.' Here 'Him' designates the previously discussed object of meditation with mind-made and related qualities as object/goal; 'I shall attain' designates the embodied meditator as agent/attainer. Thus 'I shall attain' means 'I shall reach.' In real movement, one and the same entity cannot coherently be both object and agent in this way. Likewise, the meditated and meditator roles presuppose difference. Therefore, the one qualified by mind-made attributes is not the embodied self. (4)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.4: karmakartṛ-vyapadesha (goal/attainer distinction) and upasya/upasaka structure require referential difference, excluding jiva self-reference.

records.source_text: इतश्च शारीरादन्यो मनोमयत्वादिगुणः ; यस्माच्छब्दविशेषो भवति समानप्रकरणे श्रुत्यन्तरे — ‘ यथा व्रीहिर्वा यवो वा श्यामाको वा श्यामाकतण्डुलो वैवमयमन्तरात्मन्पुरुषो हिरण्मयः ’ (श. ब्रा. १० । ६ । ३ । २) इति । शारीरस्यात्मनो यः शब्दोऽभिधायकः सप्तम्यन्तः — अन्तरात्मन्निति ; तस्माद्विशिष्टोऽन्यः प्रथमान्तः पुरुषशब्दो मनोमयत्वादिविशिष्टस्यात्मनोऽभिधायकः । तस्मात्तयोर्भेदोऽधिगम्यते ॥ ५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca śārīrādanyo manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yasmācchabdaviśeṣo bhavati samānaprakaraṇe śrutyantare — ‘ yathā vrīhirvā yavo vā śyāmāko vā śyāmākataṇḍulo vaivamayamantarātmanpuruṣo hiraṇmayaḥ ’ (śa. brā. 10 | 6 | 3 | 2) iti | śārīrasyātmano yaḥ śabdo'bhidhāyakaḥ saptamyantaḥ — antarātmanniti ; tasmādviśiṣṭo'nyaḥ prathamāntaḥ puruṣaśabdo manomayatvādiviśiṣṭasyātmano'bhidhāyakaḥ | tasmāttayorbhedo'dhigamyate || 5 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca śārīrādanyo manomayatvādiguṇaḥ ; yasmācchabdaviśeṣo bhavati samānaprakaraṇe śrutyantare — ‘ yathā vrīhirvā yavo vā śyāmāko vā śyāmākataṇḍulo vaivamayamantarātmanpuruṣo hiraṇmayaḥ ’ (śa. brā. 10 | 6 | 3 | 2) iti | śārīrasyātmano yaḥ śabdo'bhidhāyakaḥ saptamyantaḥ — antarātmanniti ; tasmādviśiṣṭo'nyaḥ prathamāntaḥ puruṣaśabdo manomayatvādiviśiṣṭasyātmano'bhidhāyakaḥ | tasmāttayorbhedo'dhigamyate || 5 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the one with mind-made and related attributes is other than the embodied self: there is a specific wording in a parallel passage of the same topic - 'as a grain of rice or barley... this golden person in the inner self.' The word denoting the embodied self appears in locative form ('in the inner self'), while a distinct nominative word 'person' denotes another self qualified by mind-made and related attributes. Thus, difference between the two is understood. (5)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.5 uses grammatical distinction (saptami vs prathama reference) in parallel shruti to infer bheda between sharira-jiva locus and worshipped purusha.

records.source_text: स्मृतिश्च शारीरपरमात्मनोर्भेदं दर्शयति — ‘ ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति । भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ’ (भ. गी. १८ । ६१) इत्याद्या ।
records.transliteration_iast: smṛtiśca śārīraparamātmanorbhedaṃ darśayati — ‘ īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe'rjuna tiṣṭhati | bhrāmayansarvabhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā ’ (bha. gī. 18 | 61) ityādyā |
records.transliteration: smṛtiśca śārīraparamātmanorbhedaṃ darśayati — ‘ īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe'rjuna tiṣṭhati | bhrāmayansarvabhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā ’ (bha. gī. 18 | 61) ityādyā |
records.literal_translation: And smriti too shows difference between embodied self and Supreme Self, in passages such as: 'The Lord dwells in the heart-region of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings to revolve, mounted on machine-like structures, by maya.'
records.technical_translation: Bhashya support line: smriti corroborates jiva-Ishvara distinction relevant to the adhikarana conclusion.

records.source_text: अत्राह — कः पुनरयं शारीरो नाम परमात्मनोऽन्यः , यः प्रतिषिध्यते — ‘ अनुपपत्तेस्तु न शारीरः ’ इत्यादिना ? श्रुतिस्तु ‘ नान्योऽतोऽस्ति द्रष्टा नान्योऽतोऽस्ति श्रोता ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । २३) इत्येवंजातीयका परमात्मनोऽन्यमात्मानं वारयति । तथा स्मृतिरपि ‘ क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत ’ (भ. गी. १३ । २) इत्येवंजातीयकेति । अत्रोच्यते — सत्य मे वैतत् , पर एवात्मा देहेन्द्रियमनोबुद्ध्युपाधिभिः परिच्छिद्यमानो बालैः शारीर इत्युपचर्यते । यथा घटकरकाद्युपाधिवशादपरिच्छिन्नमपि नभः परिच्छिन्नवदवभासते , तद्वत् । तदपेक्षया च कर्मत्वकर्तृत्वादिभेदव्यवहारो न विरुध्यते प्राक् ‘ तत्त्वमसि ’ इत्यात्मैकत्वोपदेशग्रहणात् । गृहीते त्वात्मैकत्वे बन्धमोक्षादिसर्वव्यवहारपरिसमाप्तिरेव स्यात् ॥ ६ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: atrāha — kaḥ punarayaṃ śārīro nāma paramātmano'nyaḥ , yaḥ pratiṣidhyate — ‘ anupapattestu na śārīraḥ ’ ityādinā ? śrutistu ‘ nānyo'to'sti draṣṭā nānyo'to'sti śrotā ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 23) ityevaṃjātīyakā paramātmano'nyamātmānaṃ vārayati | tathā smṛtirapi ‘ kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata ’ (bha. gī. 13 | 2) ityevaṃjātīyaketi | atrocyate — satya me vaitat , para evātmā dehendriyamanobuddhyupādhibhiḥ paricchidyamāno bālaiḥ śārīra ityupacaryate | yathā ghaṭakarakādyupādhivaśādaparicchinnamapi nabhaḥ paricchinnavadavabhāsate , tadvat | tadapekṣayā ca karmatvakartṛtvādibhedavyavahāro na virudhyate prāk ‘ tattvamasi ’ ityātmaikatvopadeśagrahaṇāt | gṛhīte tvātmaikatve bandhamokṣādisarvavyavahāraparisamāptireva syāt || 6 ||
records.transliteration: atrāha — kaḥ punarayaṃ śārīro nāma paramātmano'nyaḥ , yaḥ pratiṣidhyate — ‘ anupapattestu na śārīraḥ ’ ityādinā ? śrutistu ‘ nānyo'to'sti draṣṭā nānyo'to'sti śrotā ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 23) ityevaṃjātīyakā paramātmano'nyamātmānaṃ vārayati | tathā smṛtirapi ‘ kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata ’ (bha. gī. 13 | 2) ityevaṃjātīyaketi | atrocyate — satya me vaitat , para evātmā dehendriyamanobuddhyupādhibhiḥ paricchidyamāno bālaiḥ śārīra ityupacaryate | yathā ghaṭakarakādyupādhivaśādaparicchinnamapi nabhaḥ paricchinnavadavabhāsate , tadvat | tadapekṣayā ca karmatvakartṛtvādibhedavyavahāro na virudhyate prāk ‘ tattvamasi ’ ityātmaikatvopadeśagrahaṇāt | gṛhīte tvātmaikatve bandhamokṣādisarvavyavahāraparisamāptireva syāt || 6 ||
records.literal_translation: Here one may ask: who is this so-called embodied self, other than the Supreme, that is being denied by 'not the embodied one because of non-applicability' and so forth? Yet shruti statements such as 'there is no other seer, no other hearer than this' reject any self apart from the Supreme; and smriti says similarly, 'know Me as the knower of the field in all fields.' Reply: this is true. The Supreme Self alone, when seemingly delimited by body-sense-mind-intellect adjuncts, is conventionally spoken of by the untrained as 'embodied,' just as undelimited space appears delimited through pot and jar adjuncts. Relative to that appearance, transactional distinctions such as objecthood and agency are not contradictory prior to assimilating the non-dual teaching 'That thou art.' But once Self-identity is realized, all transactions concerning bondage and liberation and similar distinctions come to completion. (6)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.6 reconciles provisional jiva-language with non-dual finality via upadhi-based superimposition and pedagogical two-level discourse.

records.source_text: अर्भकमल्पम् ओको नीडम् , ‘ एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये ’ इति परिच्छिन्नायतनत्वात् , स्वशब्देन च ‘ अणीयान्व्रीहेर्वा यवाद्वा ’ इत्यणीयस्त्वव्यपदेशात् , शारीर एवाराग्रमात्रो जीव इहोपदिश्यते , न सर्वगतः परमात्मा — इति यदुक्तं तत्परिहर्तव्यम् । अत्रोच्यते — नायं दोषः । न ताव त्परिच्छिन्नदेशस्य सर्वगतत्वव्यपदेशः कथम प्युपपद्यते । सर्वगतस्य तु सर्वदेशेषु विद्यमानत्वात्परिच्छिन्नदेशव्यपदेशोऽपि कयाचिदपेक्षया सम्भवति । यथा सम स्तवसुधाधिपतिरपि हि सन् अयोध्याधिपतिरिति व्यपदिश्यते । कया पुनरपेक्षया सर्वगतः सन्नीश्वरोऽर्भकौका अणीयांश्च व्यपदिश्यत इति । निचाय्यत्वादेवमिति ब्रूमः । एवम् अणीयस्त्वादिगुणगणोपेत ईश्वरः , तत्र हृदयपुण्डरीके निचाय्यो द्रष्टव्य उपदिश्यते ; यथा सालग्रामे हरिः । तत्रास्य बुद्धिविज्ञानं ग्राहकम् । सर्वगतोऽपीश्वरस्तत्रोपास्यमानः प्रसीदति । व्योमवच्चैतद्द्रष्टव्यम् । यथा सर्वगतमपि सद्व्योम सूचीपाशाद्यपेक्षयार्भकौकोऽणीयश्च व्यपदिश्यते , एवं ब्रह्मापि । तदेवं निचाय्यत्वापेक्षं ब्रह्मणोऽर्भकौकस्त्वमणीयस्त्वं च , न पारमार्थिकम् । तत्र यदाशङ्क्यते — हृदयायतनत्वाद्ब्रह्मणो हृदयानां च प्रतिशरीरं भिन्नत्वाद्भिन्नायतनानां च शुकादीनामनेकत्वसावयवत्वानित्यत्वादिदोषदर्शनाद्ब्रह्मणोऽपि तत्प्रसङ्ग इति , तदपि परिहृतं भवति ॥ ७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: arbhakamalpam oko nīḍam , ‘ eṣa ma ātmāntarhṛdaye ’ iti paricchinnāyatanatvāt , svaśabdena ca ‘ aṇīyānvrīhervā yavādvā ’ ityaṇīyastvavyapadeśāt , śārīra evārāgramātro jīva ihopadiśyate , na sarvagataḥ paramātmā — iti yaduktaṃ tatparihartavyam | atrocyate — nāyaṃ doṣaḥ | na tāva tparicchinnadeśasya sarvagatatvavyapadeśaḥ kathama pyupapadyate | sarvagatasya tu sarvadeśeṣu vidyamānatvātparicchinnadeśavyapadeśo'pi kayācidapekṣayā sambhavati | yathā sama stavasudhādhipatirapi hi san ayodhyādhipatiriti vyapadiśyate | kayā punarapekṣayā sarvagataḥ sannīśvaro'rbhakaukā aṇīyāṃśca vyapadiśyata iti | nicāyyatvādevamiti brūmaḥ | evam aṇīyastvādiguṇagaṇopeta īśvaraḥ , tatra hṛdayapuṇḍarīke nicāyyo draṣṭavya upadiśyate ; yathā sālagrāme hariḥ | tatrāsya buddhivijñānaṃ grāhakam | sarvagato'pīśvarastatropāsyamānaḥ prasīdati | vyomavaccaitaddraṣṭavyam | yathā sarvagatamapi sadvyoma sūcīpāśādyapekṣayārbhakauko'ṇīyaśca vyapadiśyate , evaṃ brahmāpi | tadevaṃ nicāyyatvāpekṣaṃ brahmaṇo'rbhakaukastvamaṇīyastvaṃ ca , na pāramārthikam | tatra yadāśaṅkyate — hṛdayāyatanatvādbrahmaṇo hṛdayānāṃ ca pratiśarīraṃ bhinnatvādbhinnāyatanānāṃ ca śukādīnāmanekatvasāvayavatvānityatvādidoṣadarśanādbrahmaṇo'pi tatprasaṅga iti , tadapi parihṛtaṃ bhavati || 7 ||

records.literal_translation: The objection that should be dismissed is this: because of restricted locus ('this self within the heart') and because of smallness-expressions ('smaller than rice or barley'), it must be the embodied jiva, minute like a point, that is taught here, not the all-pervasive Supreme. Reply: this is no defect. For a truly spatially delimited thing, all-pervasiveness cannot fit; but for what is all-pervasive, designation in a limited place is possible under a specific standpoint, since it is present everywhere. Just as one who is lord of the whole earth is still called lord of Ayodhya under a perspective, so too. Under what perspective is the all-pervasive Lord called small-housed and minute? We say: because He is to be specifically focused upon. The Lord endowed with such qualities is taught as to be contemplated in the lotus of the heart, as Hari in a Salagrama. Here intellect-cognition functions as the grasping medium; and even though all-pervasive, when worshipped there the Lord becomes gracious. This should be understood like space: though all-pervasive, space is called tiny or minute with reference to a needle-eye or loop. Likewise Brahman. Thus Brahman's small-housedness and minuteness are relative to contemplative focusing, not ultimate. Therefore the further worry is also answered: if Brahman has heart as locus, and hearts differ per body, then like pots (many loci) Brahman would become many, composite, non-eternal, etc. That consequence does not follow.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.7 defuses alpatva-hrdaya objections by introducing nicayya-upasana relativity: local specification is pedagogical focus-mode, not ontological delimitation of Brahman.

records.source_text: अर्भकौकस्त्वात्तद्व्यपदेशाच्च नेति चेन्न निचाय्यत्वादेवं व्योमवच्च ॥ ७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: arbhakaukastvāttadvyapadeśācca neti cenna nicāyyatvādevaṃ vyomavacca || 7 ||
records.transliteration: arbhakaukastvāttadvyapadeśācca neti cenna nicāyyatvādevaṃ vyomavacca || 7 ||
records.literal_translation: If one says 'not so, because of small abode and such designation,' no; because this is due to focused contemplation - like space. (7)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.7: arbhakaukas/minuteness language does not force jiva-reference; it is upasana-conditioned, analogous to localized speech about all-pervasive space.

records.source_text: व्योमव त्सर्वगतस्य ब्रह्मणः सर्वप्राणिहृदयसम्बन्धात् , चिद्रूपतया च शारीरादविशिष्टत्वात् , सुखदुःखादिसम्भोगोऽप्यविशिष्टः प्रसज्येत । एकत्वाच्च ; न हि परस्मादात्मनोऽन्यः कश्चिदात्मा संसारी विद्यते , ‘ नान्योऽतोऽस्ति विज्ञाता ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । २३) इत्यादिश्रुतिभ्यः । तस्मात्परस्यैव ब्रह्मणः सम्भोगप्राप्तिरिति चेत् , न ; वैशेष्यात् । न तावत्सर्वप्राणिहृदयसम्बन्धात् शारीरवद्ब्रह्मणः सम्भोगप्रसङ्गः , वैशेष्यात् । विशेषो हि भवति शारीरपरमेश्वरयोः । एकः कर्ता भोक्ता धर्माधर्मादिसाधनः सुखदुःखादिमांश्च । एकस्तद्विपरीतोऽपहतपाप्मत्वादिगुणः । एतस्मादनयोर्विशेषादेकस्य भोगः , नेतरस्य । यदि च सन्निधानमात्रेण वस्तुशक्तिमनाश्रित्य कार्यसम्बन्धोऽभ्युपगम्येत , आकाशादीनामपि दाहादिप्रसङ्गः । सर्वग ता नेकात्मवादिनामपि समावेतौ चोद्यपरिहारौ । यदप्येकत्वाद्ब्रह्मण आत्मान्तराभावाच्छारीरस्य भोगेन ब्रह्मणो भोगप्रसङ्ग इति , अत्र वदामः — इदं तावद्देवानांप्रियः प्रष्टव्यः ; कथमयं त्वयात्मान्तराभावोऽध्यवसित इति । ‘ तत्त्वमसि ’ ‘ अहं ब्रह्मास्मि ’ ‘ नान्योऽतोऽस्ति विज्ञाता ’ इत्यादिशास्त्रेभ्य इति चेत् , यथाशास्त्रं तर्हि शास्त्रीयोऽर्थः प्रतिपत्तव्यः , न तत्रार्धजरतीयं लभ्यम् । शास्त्रं च ‘ तत्त्वमसि ’ इत्यपहतपाप्मत्वादिविशेषणं ब्रह्म शारीरस्यात्मत्वेनोपदिशच्छारीरस्यैव तावदुपभोक्तृत्वं वारयति । कुतस्तदुपभोगेन ब्रह्मण उपभोगप्रसङ्गः । अथागृहीतं शारीरस्य ब्रह्मणैकत्वम् , तदा मिथ्याज्ञाननिमित्तः शारीरस्योपभोगः । न तेन परमार्थरूपस्य ब्रह्मणः संस्पर्शः । न हि बालैस्तलमलिनतादिभिर्व्योम्नि विकल्प्यमाने तलमलिनतादिविशिष्टमेव परमार्थतो व्योम भवति । तदाह — न वैशेष्यादिति नैकत्वेऽपि शारीरस्योपभोगेन ब्रह्मण उपभोगप्रसङ्गः , वैशेष्यात् । विशेषो हि भवति मिथ्याज्ञानसम्यग्ज्ञानयोः । मिथ्याज्ञानकल्पित उपभोगः , सम्यग्ज्ञानदृष्टमेकत्वम् । न च मिथ्याज्ञानकल्पितेनोपभोगेन सम्यग्ज्ञानदृष्टं वस्तु संस्पृश्यते । तस्मान्नोपभोगगन्धोऽपि शक्य ईश्वरस्य कल्पयितुम् ॥ ८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: vyomava tsarvagatasya brahmaṇaḥ sarvaprāṇihṛdayasambandhāt , cidrūpatayā ca śārīrādaviśiṣṭatvāt , sukhaduḥkhādisambhogo'pyaviśiṣṭaḥ prasajyeta | ekatvācca ; na hi parasmādātmano'nyaḥ kaścidātmā saṃsārī vidyate , ‘ nānyo'to'sti vijñātā ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 23) ityādiśrutibhyaḥ | tasmātparasyaiva brahmaṇaḥ sambhogaprāptiriti cet , na ; vaiśeṣyāt | na tāvatsarvaprāṇihṛdayasambandhāt śārīravadbrahmaṇaḥ sambhogaprasaṅgaḥ , vaiśeṣyāt | viśeṣo hi bhavati śārīraparameśvarayoḥ | ekaḥ kartā bhoktā dharmādharmādisādhanaḥ sukhaduḥkhādimāṃśca | ekastadviparīto'pahatapāpmatvādiguṇaḥ | etasmādanayorviśeṣādekasya bhogaḥ , netarasya | yadi ca sannidhānamātreṇa vastuśaktimanāśritya kāryasambandho'bhyupagamyeta , ākāśādīnāmapi dāhādiprasaṅgaḥ | sarvaga tā nekātmavādināmapi samāvetau codyaparihārau | yadapyekatvādbrahmaṇa ātmāntarābhāvācchārīrasya bhogena brahmaṇo bhogaprasaṅga iti , atra vadāmaḥ — idaṃ tāvaddevānāṃpriyaḥ praṣṭavyaḥ ; kathamayaṃ tvayātmāntarābhāvo'dhyavasita iti | ‘ tattvamasi ’ ‘ ahaṃ brahmāsmi ’ ‘ nānyo'to'sti vijñātā ’ ityādiśāstrebhya iti cet , yathāśāstraṃ tarhi śāstrīyo'rthaḥ pratipattavyaḥ , na tatrārdhajaratīyaṃ labhyam | śāstraṃ ca ‘ tattvamasi ’ ityapahatapāpmatvādiviśeṣaṇaṃ brahma śārīrasyātmatvenopadiśacchārīrasyaiva tāvadupabhoktṛtvaṃ vārayati | kutastadupabhogena brahmaṇa upabhogaprasaṅgaḥ | athāgṛhītaṃ śārīrasya brahmaṇaikatvam , tadā mithyājñānanimittaḥ śārīrasyopabhogaḥ | na tena paramārtharūpasya brahmaṇaḥ saṃsparśaḥ | na hi bālaistalamalinatādibhirvyomni vikalpyamāne talamalinatādiviśiṣṭameva paramārthato vyoma bhavati | tadāha — na vaiśeṣyāditi naikatve'pi śārīrasyopabhogena brahmaṇa upabhogaprasaṅgaḥ , vaiśeṣyāt | viśeṣo hi bhavati mithyājñānasamyagjñānayoḥ | mithyājñānakalpita upabhogaḥ , samyagjñānadṛṣṭamekatvam | na ca mithyājñānakalpitenopabhogena samyagjñānadṛṣṭaṃ vastu saṃspṛśyate | tasmānnopabhogagandho'pi śakya īśvarasya kalpayitum || 8 ||

records.literal_translation: If Brahman is all-pervasive like space, then because it is related to all hearts and not different from embodied self as consciousness, enjoyment of pleasure and pain would also become non-differentiated. And by non-duality there is no other transmigrating self apart from the Supreme ('there is no other knower than this'). So it would follow that Brahman itself undergoes experience. Reply: no, because of distinction. Mere relation to all hearts does not entail embodied-style enjoyment for Brahman; there is a specific difference between embodied self and Supreme Lord: one is doer/enjoyer, conditioned by dharma-adharma and subject to pleasure/pain; the other is opposite, qualified by freedom from evil and related attributes. Because of this difference, experience belongs to one, not the other. If mere spatial proximity, without regard to entity-nature, were enough for functional transfer, then space too would burn when near fire. The same objection and reply also apply against theorists of many all-pervasive selves. As for the non-duality argument - since no other self exists, experience of the embodied would become Brahman's - first ask: how have you settled this non-otherness? If from scriptures like 'That thou art,' 'I am Brahman,' 'there is no other knower,' then scriptural meaning must be received as taught, not half-parsed. Scripture, teaching Brahman with qualifiers like freedom from evil as the self of the embodied, first negates enjoyership as ultimate for the embodied itself; why then impose enjoyership on Brahman through that relation? If identity has not yet been realized, embodied enjoyment is due to ignorance and does not touch Brahman in its reality - just as imagined dirt at the edge of sky does not make real sky dirty. Thus, even with non-duality, embodied enjoyment does not transfer to Brahman, because there is distinction between ignorance-cognition and true knowledge: enjoyment is ignorance-imagined; identity is known in true knowledge; the ignorance-imagined cannot contaminate what true knowledge reveals. Therefore not even a trace of enjoyment can be attributed to Isvara.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.8 answers bhoga-transfer objection through vaiśeṣya (jiva-Ishvara functional distinction) plus two-level epistemology: mithyajñāna-vyavahara cannot affect samyagjñāna-established Brahman.

records.source_text: सम्भोगप्राप्तिरिति चेन्न वैशेष्यात् ॥ ८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: sambhogaprāptiriti cenna vaiśeṣyāt || 8 ||
records.transliteration: sambhogaprāptiriti cenna vaiśeṣyāt || 8 ||
records.literal_translation: If one says enjoyment would result, no - because of distinction. (8)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.8: bhoga does not accrue to Brahman despite all-pervasiveness/non-duality claims, due to decisive jiva-Ishvara distinction at relevant level.

records.source_text: कठवल्लीषु पठ्यते ‘ यस्य ब्रह्म च क्षत्रं चोभे भवत ओदनः । मृत्युर्यस्योपसेचनं क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । २५) इति । अत्र कश्चिदोदनोपसेचनसूचितोऽत्ता प्रतीयते । तत्र किमग्निरत्ता स्यात् , उत जीवः , अथवा परमात्मा , इति संशयः । विशे षा नवधारणात् । त्रयाणां चाग्निजीवपरमात्मनामस्मिन्ग्रन्थे प्रश्नोपन्यासोपलब्धेः । किं ताव त्प्राप्तम् ? अग्निरत्तेति । कुतः ? ‘ अग्निरन्नादः ’ (बृ. उ. १ । ४ । ६) इति श्रुतिप्रसिद्धिभ्याम् । जीवो वा अत्ता स्यात् ‘ तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्ति ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । १) इति दर्शनात् । न परमात्मा , ‘ अनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीति ’ इति दर्शनादित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: kaṭhavallīṣu paṭhyate ‘ yasya brahma ca kṣatraṃ cobhe bhavata odanaḥ | mṛtyuryasyopasecanaṃ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 25) iti | atra kaścidodanopasecanasūcito'ttā pratīyate | tatra kimagnirattā syāt , uta jīvaḥ , athavā paramātmā , iti saṃśayaḥ | viśe ṣā navadhāraṇāt | trayāṇāṃ cāgnijīvaparamātmanāmasmingranthe praśnopanyāsopalabdheḥ | kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? agniratteti | kutaḥ ? ‘ agnirannādaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 1 | 4 | 6) iti śrutiprasiddhibhyām | jīvo vā attā syāt ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvatti ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) iti darśanāt | na paramātmā , ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ iti darśanādityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: kaṭhavallīṣu paṭhyate ‘ yasya brahma ca kṣatraṃ cobhe bhavata odanaḥ | mṛtyuryasyopasecanaṃ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 25) iti | atra kaścidodanopasecanasūcito'ttā pratīyate | tatra kimagnirattā syāt , uta jīvaḥ , athavā paramātmā , iti saṃśayaḥ | viśe ṣā navadhāraṇāt | trayāṇāṃ cāgnijīvaparamātmanāmasmingranthe praśnopanyāsopalabdheḥ | kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? agniratteti | kutaḥ ? ‘ agnirannādaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 1 | 4 | 6) iti śrutiprasiddhibhyām | jīvo vā attā syāt ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvatti ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) iti darśanāt | na paramātmā , ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ iti darśanādityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: In the Katha section it is read: 'For whom brahmana and kshatra both are food, and death is condiment - who thus knows where He is?' Here an eater is indicated by the food-condiment imagery. The doubt is: is the eater fire, or the jiva, or the Supreme Self? There is no immediate determination by special mark, and in this textual region all three - fire, jiva, and Supreme - are encountered in question and exposition. Prima facie one may take fire as eater, from scriptural/common usage ('fire is eater of food'). Or one may take jiva, as seen in texts where one of two birds eats the sweet fruit. Not the Supreme, since another text says one (the higher) does not eat but merely witnesses. With this prima facie view, we reply -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.2.9 frames triadic candidate analysis (agni/jiva/Brahman) for the 'attā' passage with lexical and intertextual arguments.

records.source_text: अत्तात्र परमात्मा भवितुमर्हति । कुतः ? चराचरग्रहणात् । चराचरं हि स्थावरजङ्गमं मृत्यूपसेचनमिहाद्यत्वेन प्रतीयते । तादृशस्य चाद्यस्य न परमात्मनोऽन्यः कार्त्स्न्येनात्ता सम्भवति । परमात्मा तु विकारजातं संहरन्सर्वमत्तीत्युपपद्यते । नन्विह चराचरग्रहणं नोपलभ्यते , कथं सिद्धवच्चराचरग्रहणं हेतुत्वेनोपादीयते ? नैष दोषः , मृत्यूपसेचनत्वेनेहाद्यत्वेन सर्वस्य प्राणिनिकायस्य प्रतीयमानत्वात् , ब्रह्मक्षत्रयोश्च प्राधान्यात्प्रदर्शनार्थत्वोपपत्तेः । यत्तु परमात्मनोऽपि नात्तृत्वं सम्भवति ‘ अनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीति ’ इति दर्शनादिति , अत्रोच्यते — कर्मफलभोगस्य प्रतिषेधकमेतद्दर्शनम् , तस्य सन्निहितत्वात् । न विकारसंहारस्य प्रतिषेधकम् , सर्ववेदान्तेषु सृष्टिस्थितिसंहारकारणत्वेन ब्रह्मणः प्रसिद्धत्वात् । तस्मात्परमात्मैवेहात्ता भवितुमर्हति ॥ ९ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: attātra paramātmā bhavitumarhati | kutaḥ ? carācaragrahaṇāt | carācaraṃ hi sthāvarajaṅgamaṃ mṛtyūpasecanamihādyatvena pratīyate | tādṛśasya cādyasya na paramātmano'nyaḥ kārtsnyenāttā sambhavati | paramātmā tu vikārajātaṃ saṃharansarvamattītyupapadyate | nanviha carācaragrahaṇaṃ nopalabhyate , kathaṃ siddhavaccarācaragrahaṇaṃ hetutvenopādīyate ? naiṣa doṣaḥ , mṛtyūpasecanatvenehādyatvena sarvasya prāṇinikāyasya pratīyamānatvāt , brahmakṣatrayośca prādhānyātpradarśanārthatvopapatteḥ | yattu paramātmano'pi nāttṛtvaṃ sambhavati ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ iti darśanāditi , atrocyate — karmaphalabhogasya pratiṣedhakametaddarśanam , tasya sannihitatvāt | na vikārasaṃhārasya pratiṣedhakam , sarvavedānteṣu sṛṣṭisthitisaṃhārakāraṇatvena brahmaṇaḥ prasiddhatvāt | tasmātparamātmaivehāttā bhavitumarhati || 9 ||
records.transliteration: attātra paramātmā bhavitumarhati | kutaḥ ? carācaragrahaṇāt | carācaraṃ hi sthāvarajaṅgamaṃ mṛtyūpasecanamihādyatvena pratīyate | tādṛśasya cādyasya na paramātmano'nyaḥ kārtsnyenāttā sambhavati | paramātmā tu vikārajātaṃ saṃharansarvamattītyupapadyate | nanviha carācaragrahaṇaṃ nopalabhyate , kathaṃ siddhavaccarācaragrahaṇaṃ hetutvenopādīyate ? naiṣa doṣaḥ , mṛtyūpasecanatvenehādyatvena sarvasya prāṇinikāyasya pratīyamānatvāt , brahmakṣatrayośca prādhānyātpradarśanārthatvopapatteḥ | yattu paramātmano'pi nāttṛtvaṃ sambhavati ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ iti darśanāditi , atrocyate — karmaphalabhogasya pratiṣedhakametaddarśanam , tasya sannihitatvāt | na vikārasaṃhārasya pratiṣedhakam , sarvavedānteṣu sṛṣṭisthitisaṃhārakāraṇatvena brahmaṇaḥ prasiddhatvāt | tasmātparamātmaivehāttā bhavitumarhati || 9 ||
records.literal_translation: The eater here must be the Supreme Self. Why? Because what is taken as eaten is the moving and unmoving whole. The entire stationary and moving cosmos, with death as condiment, is implied as food here. For such an object, no one other than the Supreme can be total eater in full extent. The Supreme, as dissolver of all modifications, is coherently said to eat all. Objection: explicit moving-unmoving language is not directly present; how then is this reason treated as established? No fault: since death as condiment and food-status implies the total aggregate of beings, and brahmana-kshatra are shown as principal exemplars. Objection again: even for Supreme, eaterhood is impossible due to texts saying 'the other does not eat but only looks on.' Reply: that statement negates karmic-fruit enjoyment in that context, not cosmic dissolution. It cannot negate dissolution, since all Vedanta establishes Brahman as cause of creation, maintenance, and dissolution. Therefore here the eater is the Supreme Self alone. (9)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.9 determines attā as Brahman via caracara-totality and saṃhāra-function, while restricting 'non-eating witness' texts to karma-bhoga contexts.

records.source_text: इतश्च परमात्मैवेहात्ता भवितुमर्हति ; यत्कारणं प्रकरणमिदं परमात्मनः — ‘ न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चित् ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १८) इत्यादि । प्रकृतग्रहणं च न्याय्यम् । ‘ क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ’ इति च दुर्विज्ञानत्वं परमात्मलिङ्गम् ॥ १० ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca paramātmaivehāttā bhavitumarhati ; yatkāraṇaṃ prakaraṇamidaṃ paramātmanaḥ — ‘ na jāyate mriyate vā vipaścit ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 18) ityādi | prakṛtagrahaṇaṃ ca nyāyyam | ‘ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ ’ iti ca durvijñānatvaṃ paramātmaliṅgam || 10 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca paramātmaivehāttā bhavitumarhati ; yatkāraṇaṃ prakaraṇamidaṃ paramātmanaḥ — ‘ na jāyate mriyate vā vipaścit ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 18) ityādi | prakṛtagrahaṇaṃ ca nyāyyam | ‘ ka itthā veda yatra saḥ ’ iti ca durvijñānatvaṃ paramātmaliṅgam || 10 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the eater here must be the Supreme Self: this whole section concerns the Supreme ('the knower is not born, nor dies,' etc.), and taking the already-in-topic referent is methodologically proper. Also, the statement 'who indeed knows where He is?' indicates difficult knowability, which is a mark of the Supreme. (10)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.10 reinforces the atta-as-Brahman reading by prakarana continuity and durvijnyatva-linga.

records.source_text: कठवल्लीष्वेव पठ्यते — ‘ ऋतं पिबन्तौ सुकृतस्य लोके गुहां प्रविष्टौ परमे परार्धे । छायातपौ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति पञ्चाग्नयो ये च त्रिणाचिकेताः ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । १) इति । तत्र संशयः — किमिह बुद्धिजीवौ निर्दिष्टौ , उत जीवपरमात्मानाविति । यदि बुद्धिजीवौ , ततो बुद्धिप्रधानात्कार्यकरणसङ्घाताद्विलक्षणो जीवः प्रतिपादितो भवति । तदपीह प्रतिपादयितव्यम् , ‘ येयं प्रेते विचिकित्सा मनुष्येऽस्तीत्येके नायमस्तीति चैके । एतद्विद्यामनुशिष्टस्त्वयाहं वराणामेष वरस्तृतीयः ’ (क. उ. १ । १ । २०) इति पृष्टत्वात् । अथ जीवपरमात्मानौ , ततो जीवाद्विलक्षणः परमात्मा प्रतिपादितो भवति । तदपीह प्रतिपादयितव्यम् — ‘ अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मादन्यत्रास्मात्कृताकृतात् । अन्यत्र भूताच्च भव्याच्च यत्तत्पश्यसि तद्वद ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १४) इति पृष्टत्वात् । अत्रा हा क्षेप्ता — उभावप्येतौ पक्षौ न सम्भवतः । कस्मात् ? ऋतपानं कर्मफलोपभोगः , ‘ सुकृतस्य लोके ’ इति लिङ्गात् । तच्च चेतनस्य क्षेत्रज्ञस्य सम्भवति , नाचेतनाया बुद्धेः । ‘ पिबन्तौ ’ इति च द्विवचनेन द्वयोः पानं दर्शयति श्रुतिः । अतो बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञपक्षस्तावन्न सम्भवति । अत एव क्षेत्रज्ञपरमात्मपक्षोऽपि न सम्भवति । चेतनेऽपि परमात्मनि ऋतपानासम्भवात् , ‘ अनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीति ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । १) इति मन्त्रवर्णादिति । अत्रोच्यते — नैष दोषः ; छत्रिणो गच्छन्तीत्येकेनापि च्छत्रिणा बहूनामच्छत्रिणां छत्रित्वोपचारदर्शनात् । एवमेकेनापि पिबता द्वौ पिबन्तावुच्येयाताम् । यद्वा जीवस्तावत्पिबति । ईश्वरस्तु पाययति । पाययन्नपि पिबतीत्युच्यते , पाचयितर्यपि पक्तृत्वप्रसिद्धिदर्शनात् । बुद्धि क्षेत्रज्ञपरिग्रहोऽपि सम्भवति ; करणे कर्तृत्वोपचारात् । ‘ एधांसि पचन्ति ’ इति प्रयोगदर्शनात् । न चा ध्यात्माधिकारेऽन्यौ कौचिद्द्वावृतं पिबन्तौ सम्भवतः । तस्माद्बुद्धिजीवौ स्यातां जीवपरमात्मानौ वेति संशयः ॥
records.transliteration_iast: kaṭhavallīṣveva paṭhyate — ‘ ṛtaṃ pibantau sukṛtasya loke guhāṃ praviṣṭau parame parārdhe | chāyātapau brahmavido vadanti pañcāgnayo ye ca triṇāciketāḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 1) iti | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimiha buddhijīvau nirdiṣṭau , uta jīvaparamātmānāviti | yadi buddhijīvau , tato buddhipradhānātkāryakaraṇasaṅghātādvilakṣaṇo jīvaḥ pratipādito bhavati | tadapīha pratipādayitavyam , ‘ yeyaṃ prete vicikitsā manuṣye'stītyeke nāyamastīti caike | etadvidyāmanuśiṣṭastvayāhaṃ varāṇāmeṣa varastṛtīyaḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 1 | 20) iti pṛṣṭatvāt | atha jīvaparamātmānau , tato jīvādvilakṣaṇaḥ paramātmā pratipādito bhavati | tadapīha pratipādayitavyam — ‘ anyatra dharmādanyatrādharmādanyatrāsmātkṛtākṛtāt | anyatra bhūtācca bhavyācca yattatpaśyasi tadvada ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 14) iti pṛṣṭatvāt | atrā hā kṣeptā — ubhāvapyetau pakṣau na sambhavataḥ | kasmāt ? ṛtapānaṃ karmaphalopabhogaḥ , ‘ sukṛtasya loke ’ iti liṅgāt | tacca cetanasya kṣetrajñasya sambhavati , nācetanāyā buddheḥ | ‘ pibantau ’ iti ca dvivacanena dvayoḥ pānaṃ darśayati śrutiḥ | ato buddhikṣetrajñapakṣastāvanna sambhavati | ata eva kṣetrajñaparamātmapakṣo'pi na sambhavati | cetane'pi paramātmani ṛtapānāsambhavāt , ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) iti mantravarṇāditi | atrocyate — naiṣa doṣaḥ ; chatriṇo gacchantītyekenāpi cchatriṇā bahūnāmacchatriṇāṃ chatritvopacāradarśanāt | evamekenāpi pibatā dvau pibantāvucyeyātām | yadvā jīvastāvatpibati | īśvarastu pāyayati | pāyayannapi pibatītyucyate , pācayitaryapi paktṛtvaprasiddhidarśanāt | buddhi kṣetrajñaparigraho'pi sambhavati ; karaṇe kartṛtvopacārāt | ‘ edhāṃsi pacanti ’ iti prayogadarśanāt | na cā dhyātmādhikāre'nyau kauciddvāvṛtaṃ pibantau sambhavataḥ | tasmādbuddhijīvau syātāṃ jīvaparamātmānau veti saṃśayaḥ ||

records.literal_translation: In the Katha text it is read: 'The two who drink the truth-result in the world of merit, entered into the cave, in the highest beyond; shadow and light, so say knowers of Brahman, performers of five fires, and the triple-Nachiketas.' Doubt arises: are these two intellect and jiva, or jiva and Supreme Self? If intellect and jiva, then the jiva distinct from the body-mind aggregate is taught - a topic relevant here, since Nachiketas asked what persists after death. If jiva and Supreme, then a Supreme distinct from jiva is taught - also relevant, since he asked for that beyond dharma/adharma, beyond done/undone, beyond past/future. Objection: neither option works. 'Drinking truth-result' implies enjoyment of karma-fruit ('in the world of merit'), possible for conscious knower but not for inert intellect; and dual number 'the two drink' indicates both drink, so intellect-jiva fails. Therefore jiva-Supreme also fails, since texts say the higher does not eat, only witnesses. Reply: no fault. By usage, many non-umbrella persons can be referred to as umbrella-bearers because one bears an umbrella; similarly, even if one drinks, two may be spoken of as drinking. Or jiva drinks while Isvara causes-to-drink, and causative agency is often designated as the act itself (as one who has food cooked is called cook). Even intellect-jiva grouping can be figuratively argued by attributing agency to an instrument (as in 'the logs cook'). And in this inward-self context there are no other plausible two candidates besides these pairs. So the doubt stands between intellect-jiva and jiva-Supreme.
records.technical_translation: Opening of 1.2.11 maps the dvivacana ambiguity in 'pibantau': competing pairings are tested via karma-bhoga, figurative agency, and adhyatma context constraints.

records.source_text: किं ताव त्प्राप्तम् ? बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञाविति । कुतः ? ‘ गुहां प्रविष्टौ ’ इति विशेषणात् । यदि शरीरं गुहा , यदि वा हृदयम् , उभयथापि बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञौ गुहां प्रविष्टावुपपद्येते । न च सति सम्भवे सर्वगतस्य ब्रह्मणो विशिष्टदेशत्वं युक्तं कल्पयितुम् । ‘ सुकृतस्य लोके ’ इति च कर्मगोचरानतिक्रमं दर्शयति । परमात्मा तु न सुकृतस्य वा दुष्कृतस्य वा गोचरे वर्तते , ‘ न कर्मणा वर्धते नो कनीयान् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । २३) इति श्रुतेः । ‘ छायातपौ ’ इति च चेतनाचेतनयोर्निर्देश उपपद्यते , छायातपवत्परस्परविलक्षणत्वात् । तस्माद्बुद्धिक्षेत्रज्ञाविहोच्येयातामित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? buddhikṣetrajñāviti | kutaḥ ? ‘ guhāṃ praviṣṭau ’ iti viśeṣaṇāt | yadi śarīraṃ guhā , yadi vā hṛdayam , ubhayathāpi buddhikṣetrajñau guhāṃ praviṣṭāvupapadyete | na ca sati sambhave sarvagatasya brahmaṇo viśiṣṭadeśatvaṃ yuktaṃ kalpayitum | ‘ sukṛtasya loke ’ iti ca karmagocarānatikramaṃ darśayati | paramātmā tu na sukṛtasya vā duṣkṛtasya vā gocare vartate , ‘ na karmaṇā vardhate no kanīyān ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 23) iti śruteḥ | ‘ chāyātapau ’ iti ca cetanācetanayornirdeśa upapadyate , chāyātapavatparasparavilakṣaṇatvāt | tasmādbuddhikṣetrajñāvihocyeyātāmityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? buddhikṣetrajñāviti | kutaḥ ? ‘ guhāṃ praviṣṭau ’ iti viśeṣaṇāt | yadi śarīraṃ guhā , yadi vā hṛdayam , ubhayathāpi buddhikṣetrajñau guhāṃ praviṣṭāvupapadyete | na ca sati sambhave sarvagatasya brahmaṇo viśiṣṭadeśatvaṃ yuktaṃ kalpayitum | ‘ sukṛtasya loke ’ iti ca karmagocarānatikramaṃ darśayati | paramātmā tu na sukṛtasya vā duṣkṛtasya vā gocare vartate , ‘ na karmaṇā vardhate no kanīyān ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 23) iti śruteḥ | ‘ chāyātapau ’ iti ca cetanācetanayornirdeśa upapadyate , chāyātapavatparasparavilakṣaṇatvāt | tasmādbuddhikṣetrajñāvihocyeyātāmityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: What is prima facie obtained is: intellect and knower-self. Why? Because of the qualifier 'entered the cave.' Whether cave means body or heart, intellect and knower-self can coherently be said to have entered there. And where that is possible, one should not force a spatially particular location upon all-pervasive Brahman. The phrase 'in the world of merit' also indicates not crossing karma-domain; but the Supreme does not move within merit/demerit range ('neither increased by good nor diminished by bad'). Also 'shadow and light' fits a conscious-inert pair, due to mutual contrast. Therefore one may take the pair here as intellect and knower-self. To that we reply -
records.technical_translation: Purvapaksha refinement in 1.2.11: guhapravesha, sukrita-loka limitation, and chaya-tapa contrast are marshaled for buddhi-jiva pairing.

records.source_text: विज्ञानात्मपरमात्मानाविहोच्येयाताम् । कस्मात् ? आत्मानौ हि तावुभावपि चेतनौ समानस्वभावौ । संख्याश्रवणे च समानस्वभावेष्वेव लोके प्रतीतिर्दृश्यते । ‘ अस्य गोर्द्वितीयोऽन्वेष्टव्यः ’ इत्युक्ते , गौरेव द्वितीयोऽन्विष्यते , नाश्वः पुरुषो वा । तदिह ऋतपानेन लिङ्गेन निश्चिते विज्ञानात्मनि द्वितीयान्वेषणायां समानस्वभावश्चेतनः परमात्मैव प्रतीयते । ननूक्तं गुहाहितत्वदर्शनान्न परमात्मा प्रत्येतव्य इति ; गुहाहितत्वदर्शनादेव परमात्मा प्रत्येतव्य इति वदामः । गुहाहितत्वं तु श्रुतिस्मृतिष्वसकृत्परमात्मन एव दृश्यते — ‘ गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम् ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १२) ‘ यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन् ’ (तै. उ. २ । १ । १) ‘ आत्मानमन्विच्छ गुहां प्रविष्टम् ’ इत्याद्यासु । सर्वगतस्यापि ब्रह्मण उपलब्ध्यर्थो देशविशेषोपदेशो न विरुध्यत इत्येतदप्युक्तमेव । सुकृतलोकवर्तित्वं तु च्छत्रित्ववदेकस्मिन्नपि वर्तमानमुभयोरविरुद्धम् । ‘ छायातपौ ’ इत्यप्यविरुद्धम् ; छायातपवत्परस्परविलक्षणत्वात्संसारित्वासंसारित्वयोः , अविद्याकृतत्वात्संसारित्वस्य पारमार्थिकत्वाच्चासंसारित्वस्य । तस्माद्विज्ञानात्मपरमात्मानौ गुहां प्रविष्टौ गृह्येते ॥ ११ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: vijñānātmaparamātmānāvihocyeyātām | kasmāt ? ātmānau hi tāvubhāvapi cetanau samānasvabhāvau | saṃkhyāśravaṇe ca samānasvabhāveṣveva loke pratītirdṛśyate | ‘ asya gordvitīyo'nveṣṭavyaḥ ’ ityukte , gaureva dvitīyo'nviṣyate , nāśvaḥ puruṣo vā | tadiha ṛtapānena liṅgena niścite vijñānātmani dvitīyānveṣaṇāyāṃ samānasvabhāvaścetanaḥ paramātmaiva pratīyate | nanūktaṃ guhāhitatvadarśanānna paramātmā pratyetavya iti ; guhāhitatvadarśanādeva paramātmā pratyetavya iti vadāmaḥ | guhāhitatvaṃ tu śrutismṛtiṣvasakṛtparamātmana eva dṛśyate — ‘ guhāhitaṃ gahvareṣṭhaṃ purāṇam ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 12) ‘ yo veda nihitaṃ guhāyāṃ parame vyoman ’ (tai. u. 2 | 1 | 1) ‘ ātmānamanviccha guhāṃ praviṣṭam ’ ityādyāsu | sarvagatasyāpi brahmaṇa upalabdhyartho deśaviśeṣopadeśo na virudhyata ityetadapyuktameva | sukṛtalokavartitvaṃ tu cchatritvavadekasminnapi vartamānamubhayoraviruddham | ‘ chāyātapau ’ ityapyaviruddham ; chāyātapavatparasparavilakṣaṇatvātsaṃsāritvāsaṃsāritvayoḥ , avidyākṛtatvātsaṃsāritvasya pāramārthikatvāccāsaṃsāritvasya | tasmādvijñānātmaparamātmānau guhāṃ praviṣṭau gṛhyete || 11 ||

records.literal_translation: The two taught here should be the knowing self and the Supreme Self. Why? Both are selves, conscious, and same in kind; in ordinary usage, when two are mentioned, the second is expected from same class. If told 'find a second cow for this cow,' one seeks another cow, not a horse or person. Here, once one member is fixed as conscious knower by the sign of drinking, the second is naturally grasped as another conscious same-kind reality, namely the Supreme. Objection: cave-entry shows it is not Supreme. Reply: cave-reference itself supports Supreme, since shruti and smriti repeatedly describe the Supreme as cave-seated ('hidden in the cave,' 'deposited in the cave in highest ether,' 'seek the self entered into the cave'). Also, teaching a particular locus for realizing even all-pervasive Brahman is non-contradictory, as already explained. Residence in merit-world can be understood for both as in cases like umbrella-designation. 'Shadow and light' too is consistent: samsaric and non-samsaric are mutually distinct like shadow and sunlight; samsaric status is ignorance-made, while non-samsaric status is ultimate. Therefore the pair entered in the cave should be taken as knowing self and Supreme Self. (11)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.11 establishes jiva-paramatma pairing via same-class dual reference principle, guhahita scriptural support, and qualified interpretation of sukrita/chaya-tapa descriptors.

records.source_text: गुहां प्रविष्टावात्मानौ हि तद्दर्शनात् ॥ ११ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: guhāṃ praviṣṭāvātmānau hi taddarśanāt || 11 ||
records.transliteration: guhāṃ praviṣṭāvātmānau hi taddarśanāt || 11 ||
records.literal_translation: The two selves are indeed entered into the cave, because that is seen (in scripture). (11)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.11: guhapraviṣṭa-darśana supports jiva-paramatma dual reference.

records.source_text: विशेषणं च विज्ञानात्मपरमात्मनोरेव भवति । ‘ आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि शरीरं रथमेव तु ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ३) इत्यादिना परेण ग्रन्थेन रथिरथादिरूपककल्पनया विज्ञानात्मानं रथिनं संसारमोक्षयोर्गन्तारं कल्पयति । ‘ सोऽध्वनः पारमाप्नोति तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम् ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ९) इति च परमात्मानं गन्तव्यं कल्पयति । तथा ‘ तं दुर्दर्शं गूढमनुप्रविष्टं गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम् । अध्यात्मयोगाधिगमेन देवं मत्वा धीरो हर्षशोकौ जहाति ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १२) इति पूर्वस्मिन्नपि ग्रन्थे मन्तृमन्तव्यत्वेनैतावेव विशेषितौ । प्रकरणं चेदं परमात्मनः । ‘ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति ’ इति च वक्तृविशेषोपादानं परमात्मपरिग्रहे घटते । तस्मादिह जीवपरमात्मानावुच्येयाताम् । एष एव न्यायः ‘ द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । १) इत्येवमादिष्वपि । तत्रापि ह्यध्यात्माधिकारान्न प्राकृतौ सुपर्णावुच्येते । ‘ तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्ति ’ इत्यदनलिङ्गाद्विज्ञानात्मा भवति । ‘ अनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीति ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । १) इत्यनशनचेतनत्वाभ्यां परमात्मा । अनन्तरे च मन्त्रे तावेव द्रष्टृद्रष्टव्यभावेन विशिनष्टि — ‘ समाने वृक्षे पुरुषो निमग्नोऽनीशया शोचति मुह्यमानः । जुष्टं यदा पश्यत्यन्यमीशमस्य महिमानमिति वीतशोकः ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । २) इति ॥
records.transliteration_iast: viśeṣaṇaṃ ca vijñānātmaparamātmanoreva bhavati | ‘ ātmānaṃ rathinaṃ viddhi śarīraṃ rathameva tu ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 3) ityādinā pareṇa granthena rathirathādirūpakakalpanayā vijñānātmānaṃ rathinaṃ saṃsāramokṣayorgantāraṃ kalpayati | ‘ so'dhvanaḥ pāramāpnoti tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padam ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 9) iti ca paramātmānaṃ gantavyaṃ kalpayati | tathā ‘ taṃ durdarśaṃ gūḍhamanupraviṣṭaṃ guhāhitaṃ gahvareṣṭhaṃ purāṇam | adhyātmayogādhigamena devaṃ matvā dhīro harṣaśokau jahāti ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 12) iti pūrvasminnapi granthe mantṛmantavyatvenaitāveva viśeṣitau | prakaraṇaṃ cedaṃ paramātmanaḥ | ‘ brahmavido vadanti ’ iti ca vaktṛviśeṣopādānaṃ paramātmaparigrahe ghaṭate | tasmādiha jīvaparamātmānāvucyeyātām | eṣa eva nyāyaḥ ‘ dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) ityevamādiṣvapi | tatrāpi hyadhyātmādhikārānna prākṛtau suparṇāvucyete | ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvatti ’ ityadanaliṅgādvijñānātmā bhavati | ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) ityanaśanacetanatvābhyāṃ paramātmā | anantare ca mantre tāveva draṣṭṛdraṣṭavyabhāvena viśinaṣṭi — ‘ samāne vṛkṣe puruṣo nimagno'nīśayā śocati muhyamānaḥ | juṣṭaṃ yadā paśyatyanyamīśamasya mahimānamiti vītaśokaḥ ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 2) iti ||
records.transliteration: viśeṣaṇaṃ ca vijñānātmaparamātmanoreva bhavati | ‘ ātmānaṃ rathinaṃ viddhi śarīraṃ rathameva tu ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 3) ityādinā pareṇa granthena rathirathādirūpakakalpanayā vijñānātmānaṃ rathinaṃ saṃsāramokṣayorgantāraṃ kalpayati | ‘ so'dhvanaḥ pāramāpnoti tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padam ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 9) iti ca paramātmānaṃ gantavyaṃ kalpayati | tathā ‘ taṃ durdarśaṃ gūḍhamanupraviṣṭaṃ guhāhitaṃ gahvareṣṭhaṃ purāṇam | adhyātmayogādhigamena devaṃ matvā dhīro harṣaśokau jahāti ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 12) iti pūrvasminnapi granthe mantṛmantavyatvenaitāveva viśeṣitau | prakaraṇaṃ cedaṃ paramātmanaḥ | ‘ brahmavido vadanti ’ iti ca vaktṛviśeṣopādānaṃ paramātmaparigrahe ghaṭate | tasmādiha jīvaparamātmānāvucyeyātām | eṣa eva nyāyaḥ ‘ dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) ityevamādiṣvapi | tatrāpi hyadhyātmādhikārānna prākṛtau suparṇāvucyete | ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvatti ’ ityadanaliṅgādvijñānātmā bhavati | ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśīti ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 1) ityanaśanacetanatvābhyāṃ paramātmā | anantare ca mantre tāveva draṣṭṛdraṣṭavyabhāvena viśinaṣṭi — ‘ samāne vṛkṣe puruṣo nimagno'nīśayā śocati muhyamānaḥ | juṣṭaṃ yadā paśyatyanyamīśamasya mahimānamiti vītaśokaḥ ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 2) iti ||
records.literal_translation: And the qualifying context also fits only knowing self and Supreme Self. The subsequent text, with the chariot metaphor ('know the self as rider, body as chariot'), presents the knowing self as traveler through samsara and liberation; and 'he reaches the far end of the path, that highest station of Vishnu' presents the Supreme as the goal. Earlier too ('that difficult-to-see, hidden, cave-seated ancient one; knowing that deity through inward yoga, the wise one gives up joy and sorrow') these two are marked as knower and known. The overall section concerns the Supreme, and the special mention 'knowers of Brahman say' is apt when the Supreme is included. The same reasoning applies to 'two birds, united friends': there too, in an inward-self context, the two are not ordinary birds. From 'one eats the sweet fruit' the knower-self is identified; from 'the other does not eat, but looks on' the Supreme is identified by non-eating witness-consciousness. The next mantra clarifies the same two as seer and seen: on the same tree, the deluded one grieves; when he beholds the other Lord and His greatness, he becomes free of sorrow.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya continuation supports sutra 1.2.11 with extended intertextual mapping (Katha + Mundaka) of bhokta-sakshi dyad as jiva-paramatma.

records.source_text: अपर आह — ‘ द्वा सुपर्णा ’ इति नेयमृगस्याधिकरणस्य सिद्धान्तं भजते , पैङ्गिरहस्यब्राह्मणेनान्यथा व्याख्यातत्वात् — ‘ तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्तीति सत्त्वमनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीतीत्यनश्नन्नन्योऽभिपश्यति ज्ञस्तावेतौ सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञौ ’ इति । सत्त्वशब्दो जीवः क्षेत्रज्ञशब्दः परमात्मेति यदुच्यते , तन्न ; सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञशब्दयोरन्तःकरणशारीरपरतया प्रसिद्धत्वात् । तत्रैव च व्याख्यातत्वात् — ‘ तदेतत्सत्त्वं येन स्वप्नं पश्यति , अथ योऽयं शारीर उपद्रष्टा स क्षेत्रज्ञस्तावेतौ सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञौ ’ इति । ना प्य स्याधिकरणस्य पूर्वपक्षं भजते । न ह्यत्र शारीरः क्षेत्रज्ञः कर्तृत्वभोक्तृत्वादिना संसारधर्मेणोपेतो विवक्ष्यते । कथं तर्हि ? सर्वसंसारधर्मातीतो ब्रह्मस्वभावश्चैतन्यमात्रस्वरूपः ; ‘ अनश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीतीत्यनश्नन्नन्योऽभिपश्यति ज्ञः ’ इति वचनात् , ‘ तत्त्वमसि ’ ‘ क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि ’ (भ. गी. १३ । २) इत्यादिश्रुतिस्मृतिभ्यश्च । तावता च विद्योपसंहारदर्शनमेवमेवावकल्पते , ‘ तावेतौ सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञौ न ह वा एवंविदि किञ्चन रज आध्वंसते ’ इत्यादि । कथं पुनरस्मिन्पक्षे ‘ तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्तीति सत्त्वम् ’ इत्यचेतने सत्त्वे भोक्तृत्ववचनमिति , उच्यते — नेयं श्रुतिरचेतनस्य सत्त्वस्य भोक्तृत्वं वक्ष्यामीति प्रवृत्ता ; किं तर्हि ? चेतनस्य क्षेत्रज्ञस्याभोक्तृत्वं ब्रह्मस्वभावतां च वक्ष्यामीति । तदर्थं सुखादिविक्रियावति सत्त्वे भोक्तृत्वमध्यारोपयति । इदं हि कर्तृत्वं भोक्तृत्वं च सत्त्वक्षेत्रज्ञयोरितरेतरस्वभावाविवेककृतं कल्प्यते । परमार्थतस्तु नान्यतरस्यापि सम्भवति , अचेतनत्वात्सत्त्वस्य , अविक्रियत्वाच्च क्षेत्रज्ञस्य । अविद्याप्रत्युपस्थापितस्वभावत्वाच्च सत्त्वस्य सुतरां न सम्भवति । तथा च श्रुतिः — ‘ यत्र वा अन्यदिव स्यात्तत्रान्योऽन्यत्पश्येत् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ५ । १५) इत्यादिना स्वप्नदृष्टहस्त्यादिव्यवहारवदविद्याविषय एव कर्तृत्वादिव्यवहारं दर्शयति । ‘ यत्र त्वस्य सर्वमात्मैवाभूत्तत्केन कं पश्येत् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ५ । १५) इत्यादिना च विवेकिनः कर्तृत्वादिव्यवहाराभवं दर्शयति ॥ १२ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: apara āha — ‘ dvā suparṇā ’ iti neyamṛgasyādhikaraṇasya siddhāntaṃ bhajate , paiṅgirahasyabrāhmaṇenānyathā vyākhyātatvāt — ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvattīti sattvamanaśnannanyo'bhicākaśītītyanaśnannanyo'bhipaśyati jñastāvetau sattvakṣetrajñau ’ iti | sattvaśabdo jīvaḥ kṣetrajñaśabdaḥ paramātmeti yaducyate , tanna ; sattvakṣetrajñaśabdayorantaḥkaraṇaśārīraparatayā prasiddhatvāt | tatraiva ca vyākhyātatvāt — ‘ tadetatsattvaṃ yena svapnaṃ paśyati , atha yo'yaṃ śārīra upadraṣṭā sa kṣetrajñastāvetau sattvakṣetrajñau ’ iti | nā pya syādhikaraṇasya pūrvapakṣaṃ bhajate | na hyatra śārīraḥ kṣetrajñaḥ kartṛtvabhoktṛtvādinā saṃsāradharmeṇopeto vivakṣyate | kathaṃ tarhi ? sarvasaṃsāradharmātīto brahmasvabhāvaścaitanyamātrasvarūpaḥ ; ‘ anaśnannanyo'bhicākaśītītyanaśnannanyo'bhipaśyati jñaḥ ’ iti vacanāt , ‘ tattvamasi ’ ‘ kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi ’ (bha. gī. 13 | 2) ityādiśrutismṛtibhyaśca | tāvatā ca vidyopasaṃhāradarśanamevamevāvakalpate , ‘ tāvetau sattvakṣetrajñau na ha vā evaṃvidi kiñcana raja ādhvaṃsate ’ ityādi | kathaṃ punarasminpakṣe ‘ tayoranyaḥ pippalaṃ svādvattīti sattvam ’ ityacetane sattve bhoktṛtvavacanamiti , ucyate — neyaṃ śrutiracetanasya sattvasya bhoktṛtvaṃ vakṣyāmīti pravṛttā ; kiṃ tarhi ? cetanasya kṣetrajñasyābhoktṛtvaṃ brahmasvabhāvatāṃ ca vakṣyāmīti | tadarthaṃ sukhādivikriyāvati sattve bhoktṛtvamadhyāropayati | idaṃ hi kartṛtvaṃ bhoktṛtvaṃ ca sattvakṣetrajñayoritaretarasvabhāvāvivekakṛtaṃ kalpyate | paramārthatastu nānyatarasyāpi sambhavati , acetanatvātsattvasya , avikriyatvācca kṣetrajñasya | avidyāpratyupasthāpitasvabhāvatvācca sattvasya sutarāṃ na sambhavati | tathā ca śrutiḥ — ‘ yatra vā anyadiva syāttatrānyo'nyatpaśyet ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 5 | 15) ityādinā svapnadṛṣṭahastyādivyavahāravadavidyāviṣaya eva kartṛtvādivyavahāraṃ darśayati | ‘ yatra tvasya sarvamātmaivābhūttatkena kaṃ paśyet ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 5 | 15) ityādinā ca vivekinaḥ kartṛtvādivyavahārābhavaṃ darśayati || 12 ||

records.literal_translation: Another says: 'two birds' does not follow this adhikarana's siddhanta, because the Paingi Rahasya Brahmana explains it differently: one eats the fruit - that is sattva; the other, not eating, sees - the knower; these two are sattva and kshetrajna. But taking sattva as jiva and kshetrajna as Supreme is not right, since in common usage here these terms denote inner instrument and embodied knower, and that very text explains so ('that sattva by which one sees dream; and this embodied overseer is kshetrajna'). Yet this does not establish the present adhikarana's purvapaksha either: the kshetrajna here is not intended as embodied self qualified by doership/enjoyership samsaric traits. Rather, as shown by 'the other not eating sees, as knower,' and by texts like 'That thou art' and 'know Me as kshetrajna in all fields,' the intended meaning is consciousness alone, beyond all samsaric traits, of Brahman-nature. Then the vidya-conclusion passages also fit ('for one who knows thus, no dust adheres,' etc.). Objection: in this view why is enjoyership spoken in inert sattva ('one of them eats fruit')? Reply: this shruti does not aim to establish actual enjoyership of inert sattva; rather it aims to teach non-enjoyership and Brahman-nature of conscious kshetrajna. For that purpose it superimposes enjoyership on sattva, which is subject to pleasure/pain modifications. Doership and enjoyership are projected through non-discrimination between sattva and kshetrajna. In truth they belong to neither: not to sattva (inert), not to kshetrajna (non-modifying). Since sattva's presented nature is itself ignorance-conditioned, attribution is even less real. Shruti confirms this: where duality appears, transactional seeing etc. occurs like dream-elephant dealings within ignorance-domain; where all has become Self, who would see what by what? - for the discerning, doership and related transactions cease.
records.technical_translation: Alternative hermeneutic note: even when sattva-kshetrajna terminology is retained, ultimate intent is non-dual kshetrajna beyond bhoktrtva; bhoga language is adhyaropa within avidya-vyavahara.

records.source_text: कुतश्च विज्ञानात्मपरमात्मानौ गृह्येते ? —
records.transliteration_iast: kutaśca vijñānātmaparamātmānau gṛhyete ? —
records.transliteration: kutaśca vijñānātmaparamātmānau gṛhyete ? —
records.literal_translation: And on what further grounds are the knowing self and the Supreme Self taken (as the pair)?
records.technical_translation: Leading transition that asks for additional inferential grounds in the two-in-the-cave discussion.

records.transliteration_iast: viśeṣaṇācca || 12 ||

records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.12: specific qualifiers in the passage confirm the intended pair as jiva and Supreme Self.

records.source_text: ‘ य एषोऽक्षिणि पुरुषो दृश्यत एष आत्मेति होवाचैतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्मेति । तद्यद्यप्यस्मिन्सर्पिर्वोदकं वा सिञ्चति वर्त्मनी एव गच्छति ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । १) इत्यादि श्रूयते । तत्र संशयः — किमयं प्रतिबिम्बात्माक्ष्यधिकरणो निर्दिश्यते , अथ विज्ञानात्मा , उत देवतात्मेन्द्रियस्याधिष्ठाता , अथवेश्वर इति । किं तावत्प्राप्तम् ? छायात्मा पुरुषप्रतिरूप इति । कुतः ? तस्य दृश्यमानत्वप्रसिद्धेः , ‘ य एषोऽक्षिणि पुरुषो दृश्यते ’ इति च प्रसिद्धव दु पदेशात् । विज्ञानात्मनो वायं निर्देश इति युक्तम् । स हि चक्षुषा रूपं पश्यंश्चक्षुषि सन्निहितो भवति । आत्मशब्दश्चास्मिन्पक्षेऽनुकूलो भवति । आदित्यपुरुषो वा चक्षुषोऽनुग्राहकः प्रतीयते — ‘ रश्मिभिरेषोऽस्मिन्प्रतिष्ठितः ’ (बृ. उ. ५ । ५ । २) इति श्रुतेः , अमृतत्वादीनां च देवतात्मन्यपि कथञ्चित्सम्भवात् । नेश्वरः , स्थानविशेषनिर्देशात् — इत्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ ya eṣo'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyata eṣa ātmeti hovācaitadamṛtamabhayametadbrahmeti | tadyadyapyasminsarpirvodakaṃ vā siñcati vartmanī eva gacchati ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 1) ityādi śrūyate | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimayaṃ pratibimbātmākṣyadhikaraṇo nirdiśyate , atha vijñānātmā , uta devatātmendriyasyādhiṣṭhātā , athaveśvara iti | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? chāyātmā puruṣapratirūpa iti | kutaḥ ? tasya dṛśyamānatvaprasiddheḥ , ‘ ya eṣo'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyate ’ iti ca prasiddhava du padeśāt | vijñānātmano vāyaṃ nirdeśa iti yuktam | sa hi cakṣuṣā rūpaṃ paśyaṃścakṣuṣi sannihito bhavati | ātmaśabdaścāsminpakṣe'nukūlo bhavati | ādityapuruṣo vā cakṣuṣo'nugrāhakaḥ pratīyate — ‘ raśmibhireṣo'sminpratiṣṭhitaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 5 | 5 | 2) iti śruteḥ , amṛtatvādīnāṃ ca devatātmanyapi kathañcitsambhavāt | neśvaraḥ , sthānaviśeṣanirdeśāt — ityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: ‘ ya eṣo'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyata eṣa ātmeti hovācaitadamṛtamabhayametadbrahmeti | tadyadyapyasminsarpirvodakaṃ vā siñcati vartmanī eva gacchati ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 1) ityādi śrūyate | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimayaṃ pratibimbātmākṣyadhikaraṇo nirdiśyate , atha vijñānātmā , uta devatātmendriyasyādhiṣṭhātā , athaveśvara iti | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? chāyātmā puruṣapratirūpa iti | kutaḥ ? tasya dṛśyamānatvaprasiddheḥ , ‘ ya eṣo'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyate ’ iti ca prasiddhava du padeśāt | vijñānātmano vāyaṃ nirdeśa iti yuktam | sa hi cakṣuṣā rūpaṃ paśyaṃścakṣuṣi sannihito bhavati | ātmaśabdaścāsminpakṣe'nukūlo bhavati | ādityapuruṣo vā cakṣuṣo'nugrāhakaḥ pratīyate — ‘ raśmibhireṣo'sminpratiṣṭhitaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 5 | 5 | 2) iti śruteḥ , amṛtatvādīnāṃ ca devatātmanyapi kathañcitsambhavāt | neśvaraḥ , sthānaviśeṣanirdeśāt — ityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: It is heard: 'The person seen in the eye - this is the Self, this is immortal, fearless, this is Brahman; and even if ghee or water is poured into this eye, it goes only to the eyelids.' Doubt arises: is this the reflected self located in the eye, or the knowing self, or the presiding deity of the sense-organ, or the Lord? Prima facie, one may take it as reflected image, since that is visibly seen and the wording seems to teach something familiar as though established. Or it may be the knowing self, since seeing forms through the eye, it is associated with the eye; the word atma also seems favorable there. Or it may be the solar deity aiding vision, as scripture links rays to the eye, and immortality-like predicates may in some manner fit a deity-self. Not the Lord, due to the specification of a particular location. To this we reply -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.2.13 enumerates four candidates for the akshini-purusha (reflection, jiva, devata, Ishvara) with lexical and contextual support.

records.source_text: परमेश्वर ए वा क्षिण्यभ्यन्तरः पुरुष इहोपदिष्ट इति । कस्मात् ? उपपत्तेः । उपपद्यते हि परमेश्वरे गुणजातमिहोपदिश्यमानम् । आत्मत्वं तावन्मुख्यया वृत्त्या परमेश्वरे उपपद्यते , ‘ स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि ’ इति श्रुतेः । अमृतत्वाभयत्वे च तस्मिन्नसकृच्छ्रुतौ श्रूयेते । तथा परमेश्वरानुरूपमेतदक्षिस्थानम् । यथा हि परमेश्वरः सर्वदोषैरलिप्तः , अपहतपाप्मत्वादिश्रवणात् ; तथाक्षिस्थानं सर्वलेपरहितमुपदिष्टम् ‘ तद्यद्यप्यस्मिन्सर्पिर्वोदकं वा सिञ्चति , वर्त्मनी एव गच्छति ’ इति श्रुतेः । संयद्वामत्वादिगुणोपदेशश्च तस्मिन्नवकल्पते । ‘ एतं संयद्वाम इत्याचक्षते । एतं हि सर्वाणि वामान्यभिसंयन्ति’ , (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । २) ‘ एष उ एव वामनी रे ष हि सर्वाणि वामानि नयति ।’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । ३) ‘ एष उ एव भामनी रे ष हि सर्वेषु लोकेषु भाति ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । ४) इति च । अत उपपत्तेरन्तरः परमेश्वरः ॥ १३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: parameśvara e vā kṣiṇyabhyantaraḥ puruṣa ihopadiṣṭa iti | kasmāt ? upapatteḥ | upapadyate hi parameśvare guṇajātamihopadiśyamānam | ātmatvaṃ tāvanmukhyayā vṛttyā parameśvare upapadyate , ‘ sa ātmā tattvamasi ’ iti śruteḥ | amṛtatvābhayatve ca tasminnasakṛcchrutau śrūyete | tathā parameśvarānurūpametadakṣisthānam | yathā hi parameśvaraḥ sarvadoṣairaliptaḥ , apahatapāpmatvādiśravaṇāt ; tathākṣisthānaṃ sarvaleparahitamupadiṣṭam ‘ tadyadyapyasminsarpirvodakaṃ vā siñcati , vartmanī eva gacchati ’ iti śruteḥ | saṃyadvāmatvādiguṇopadeśaśca tasminnavakalpate | ‘ etaṃ saṃyadvāma ityācakṣate | etaṃ hi sarvāṇi vāmānyabhisaṃyanti’ , (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 2) ‘ eṣa u eva vāmanī re ṣa hi sarvāṇi vāmāni nayati |’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 3) ‘ eṣa u eva bhāmanī re ṣa hi sarveṣu lokeṣu bhāti ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 4) iti ca | ata upapatterantaraḥ parameśvaraḥ || 13 ||
records.transliteration: parameśvara e vā kṣiṇyabhyantaraḥ puruṣa ihopadiṣṭa iti | kasmāt ? upapatteḥ | upapadyate hi parameśvare guṇajātamihopadiśyamānam | ātmatvaṃ tāvanmukhyayā vṛttyā parameśvare upapadyate , ‘ sa ātmā tattvamasi ’ iti śruteḥ | amṛtatvābhayatve ca tasminnasakṛcchrutau śrūyete | tathā parameśvarānurūpametadakṣisthānam | yathā hi parameśvaraḥ sarvadoṣairaliptaḥ , apahatapāpmatvādiśravaṇāt ; tathākṣisthānaṃ sarvaleparahitamupadiṣṭam ‘ tadyadyapyasminsarpirvodakaṃ vā siñcati , vartmanī eva gacchati ’ iti śruteḥ | saṃyadvāmatvādiguṇopadeśaśca tasminnavakalpate | ‘ etaṃ saṃyadvāma ityācakṣate | etaṃ hi sarvāṇi vāmānyabhisaṃyanti’ , (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 2) ‘ eṣa u eva vāmanī re ṣa hi sarvāṇi vāmāni nayati |’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 3) ‘ eṣa u eva bhāmanī re ṣa hi sarveṣu lokeṣu bhāti ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 4) iti ca | ata upapatterantaraḥ parameśvaraḥ || 13 ||
records.literal_translation: The person taught as inside the eye is the Supreme Lord alone. Why? Because the taught cluster of attributes fits Him. Selfhood in the primary sense fits the Supreme by texts like 'that Self, thou art.' Immortality and fearlessness are repeatedly heard of Him. This eye-location description also matches Him: as He is untouched by all defects (freedom from evil etc.), so the eye-location is taught as unstained ('even if ghee or water is poured, it goes only to the eyelids'). The taught qualities such as samyadvama and related terms also coherently apply to Him: all desirable things converge there, He leads all desirable things, He shines in all worlds. Therefore, from this fitness, the inner one is the Supreme Lord. (13)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.13 resolves akshini-purusha as Ishvara by upapatti of key predicates: atmatva, amrtatva/abhayatva, aliptatva, and samyadvama-vamani-bhamani functions.

records.transliteration: antara upapatteḥ || 13 ||
records.literal_translation: The inner one (is Brahman), because of applicability. (13)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.13: antar-purusha in the eye is Brahman/Ishvara by attribute-fit.

records.source_text: कथं पुनराकाशवत्सर्वगतस्य ब्रह्मणोऽक्ष्यल्पं स्थानमुपपद्यत इति , अत्रोच्यते — भवेदेषानवकॢप्तिः , यद्येतदेवैकं स्थानमस्य निर्दिष्टं भवेत् । सन्ति ह्यन्यान्यपि पृथिव्यादीनि स्थानान्यस्य निर्दिष्टानि — ‘ यः पृथिव्यां तिष्ठन् ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । ३) इत्यादिना । तेषु हि चक्षुरपि निर्दिष्टम् ‘ यश्चक्षुषि तिष्ठन् ’ इति । स्थानादिव्यपदेशादित्यादिग्रहणेनैतद्दर्शयति — न केवलं स्थानमेवैकमनुचितं ब्रह्मणो निर्दिश्यमानं दृश्यते । किं तर्हि ? नाम रूपमित्येवंजातीयकमप्यनामरूपस्य ब्रह्मणोऽनुचितं निर्दिश्यमानं दृश्यते — ‘ तस्योदिति नाम ’ (छा. उ. १ । ६ । ७) ‘ हिरण्यश्मश्रुः ’ इत्यादि । निर्गुणमपि सद्ब्रह्म नामरूपगतैर्गुणैः सगुणमुपासनार्थं तत्र तत्रोपदिश्यत इत्येतदप्युक्तमेव । सर्वगतस्यापि ब्रह्मण उपलब्ध्यर्थं स्थानविशेषो न विरुध्यते , सालग्राम इव विष्णोरित्येतदप्युक्तमेव ॥ १४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: kathaṃ punarākāśavatsarvagatasya brahmaṇo'kṣyalpaṃ sthānamupapadyata iti , atrocyate — bhavedeṣānavakḷptiḥ , yadyetadevaikaṃ sthānamasya nirdiṣṭaṃ bhavet | santi hyanyānyapi pṛthivyādīni sthānānyasya nirdiṣṭāni — ‘ yaḥ pṛthivyāṃ tiṣṭhan ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 3) ityādinā | teṣu hi cakṣurapi nirdiṣṭam ‘ yaścakṣuṣi tiṣṭhan ’ iti | sthānādivyapadeśādityādigrahaṇenaitaddarśayati — na kevalaṃ sthānamevaikamanucitaṃ brahmaṇo nirdiśyamānaṃ dṛśyate | kiṃ tarhi ? nāma rūpamityevaṃjātīyakamapyanāmarūpasya brahmaṇo'nucitaṃ nirdiśyamānaṃ dṛśyate — ‘ tasyoditi nāma ’ (chā. u. 1 | 6 | 7) ‘ hiraṇyaśmaśruḥ ’ ityādi | nirguṇamapi sadbrahma nāmarūpagatairguṇaiḥ saguṇamupāsanārthaṃ tatra tatropadiśyata ityetadapyuktameva | sarvagatasyāpi brahmaṇa upalabdhyarthaṃ sthānaviśeṣo na virudhyate , sālagrāma iva viṣṇorityetadapyuktameva || 14 ||
records.transliteration: kathaṃ punarākāśavatsarvagatasya brahmaṇo'kṣyalpaṃ sthānamupapadyata iti , atrocyate — bhavedeṣānavakḷptiḥ , yadyetadevaikaṃ sthānamasya nirdiṣṭaṃ bhavet | santi hyanyānyapi pṛthivyādīni sthānānyasya nirdiṣṭāni — ‘ yaḥ pṛthivyāṃ tiṣṭhan ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 3) ityādinā | teṣu hi cakṣurapi nirdiṣṭam ‘ yaścakṣuṣi tiṣṭhan ’ iti | sthānādivyapadeśādityādigrahaṇenaitaddarśayati — na kevalaṃ sthānamevaikamanucitaṃ brahmaṇo nirdiśyamānaṃ dṛśyate | kiṃ tarhi ? nāma rūpamityevaṃjātīyakamapyanāmarūpasya brahmaṇo'nucitaṃ nirdiśyamānaṃ dṛśyate — ‘ tasyoditi nāma ’ (chā. u. 1 | 6 | 7) ‘ hiraṇyaśmaśruḥ ’ ityādi | nirguṇamapi sadbrahma nāmarūpagatairguṇaiḥ saguṇamupāsanārthaṃ tatra tatropadiśyata ityetadapyuktameva | sarvagatasyāpi brahmaṇa upalabdhyarthaṃ sthānaviśeṣo na virudhyate , sālagrāma iva viṣṇorityetadapyuktameva || 14 ||
records.literal_translation: How can a very small eye-location be fitting for Brahman who is all-pervasive like space? Reply: this objection would stand if that alone were the only location taught. But many other locations are taught too, such as earth and so forth ('who stands in earth...'), and among them eye is also taught ('who stands in the eye'). By saying 'because of designation of place and the like,' the intent is this: it is not only one seemingly unsuitable location that is taught for Brahman. Rather, other seemingly unsuitable designations are also given for the nameless-formless Brahman - names/forms such as 'its name is Uditi,' 'golden-bearded,' etc. The attributeless Brahman is taught in many places with name-form predicates for the sake of upasana; this has already been explained. And even for all-pervasive Brahman, teaching a specific location for realization is not contradictory, like Vishnu in a Salagrama; this too has already been explained. (14)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.14 generalizes the hermeneutic: multiple location/name-form designations are upasana-upaya conventions, not ontological limitations on nirguna Brahman.

records.source_text: अपि च नैवात्र विवदितव्यम् — किं ब्रह्मास्मिन्वाक्येऽभिधीयते , न वेति । सुखविशिष्टाभिधानादेव ब्रह्मत्वं सिद्धम् । सुखविशिष्टं हि ब्रह्म यद्वाक्योपक्रमे प्रक्रान्तम् ‘ प्राणो ब्रह्म कं ब्रह्म खं ब्रह्म ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १० । ४) इति , तदेवेहाभिहितम् ; प्रकृतपरिग्रहस्य न्याय्यत्वात् , ‘ आचार्यस्तु ते गतिं वक्ता ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १४ । १) इति च गतिमात्राभिधानप्रतिज्ञानात् । कथं पुनर्वाक्योपक्रमे सुखविशिष्टं ब्रह्म विज्ञायत इति , उच्यते — ‘ प्राणो ब्रह्म कं ब्रह्म खं ब्रह्म ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १० । ४) इत्येतदग्नीनां वचनं श्रुत्वोपकोसल उवाच — ‘ विजानाम्यहं यत्प्राणो ब्रह्म , कं च तु खं च न विजानामि ’ इति । तत्रेदं प्रतिवचनम् — ‘ यद्वाव कं तदेव खं यदेव खं तदेव कम् ’ इति । तत्र खं शब्दो भूताकाशे निरूढो लोके । यदि तस्य विशेषणत्वेन कंशब्दः सुखवाची नोपादीयेत , तथा सति केवले भूताकाशे ब्रह्मशब्दो नामादिष्विव प्रतीकाभिप्रायेण प्रयुक्त इति प्रतीतिः स्यात् । तथा कंशब्दस्य विषयेन्द्रियसम्पर्कजनिते सामये सुखे प्रसिद्धत्वात् , यदि तस्य खंशब्दो विशेषणत्वेन नोपादीयेत ; लौकिकं सुखं ब्रह्मेति प्रतीतिः स्यात् । इतरेतर विशेषितौ तु कंखंशब्दौ सुखात्मकं ब्रह्म गमयतः । तत्र द्वितीये ब्रह्मशब्देऽनुपादीयमाने ‘ कं खं ब्रह्म ’ इत्येवोच्यमाने कंशब्दस्य विशेषणत्वेनैवोपयुक्तत्वात्सुखस्य गुणस्याध्येयत्वं स्यात् । तन्मा भूत् — इत्युभयोः कंखंशब्दयोर्ब्रह्मशब्दशिरस्त्वम् — ‘ कं ब्रह्म खं ब्रह्म ’ इति । इष्टं हि सुखस्यापि गुणस्य गुणिवद्ध्येयत्वम् । तदेवं वाक्योपक्रमे सुखविशिष्टं ब्रह्मोपदिष्टम् । प्रत्येकं च गार्हपत्यादयोऽग्नयः स्वं स्वं महिमानमुपदिश्य ‘ एषा सोम्य तेऽस्मद्विद्यात्मविद्या च ’ इत्युपसंहरन्तः पूर्वत्र ब्रह्म निर्दिष्टमिति ज्ञापयन्ति । ‘ आचार्यस्तु ते गतिं वक्ता ’ इति च गतिमात्राभिधानप्रतिज्ञानमर्थान्तरविवक्षां वारयति । ‘ यथा पुष्करपलाश आपो न श्लिष्यन्त एवमेवंविदि पापं कर्म न श्लिष्यते ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १४ । ३) इति चाक्षिस्थानं पुरुषं विजानतः पापेनानुपघातं ब्रुवन्नक्षिस्थानस्य पुरुषस्य ब्रह्मत्वं दर्शयति । तस्मात्प्रकृतस्यैव ब्रह्मणोऽक्षिस्थानतां संयद्वामत्वादिगुणतां चोक्त्वा अर्चिरादिकां तद्विदो गतिं वक्ष्यामीत्युपक्रमते — ‘ य एषोऽक्षिणि पुरुषो दृश्यत एष आत्मेति होवाच ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । १) इति ॥ १५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: api ca naivātra vivaditavyam — kiṃ brahmāsminvākye'bhidhīyate , na veti | sukhaviśiṣṭābhidhānādeva brahmatvaṃ siddham | sukhaviśiṣṭaṃ hi brahma yadvākyopakrame prakrāntam ‘ prāṇo brahma kaṃ brahma khaṃ brahma ’ (chā. u. 4 | 10 | 4) iti , tadevehābhihitam ; prakṛtaparigrahasya nyāyyatvāt , ‘ ācāryastu te gatiṃ vaktā ’ (chā. u. 4 | 14 | 1) iti ca gatimātrābhidhānapratijñānāt | kathaṃ punarvākyopakrame sukhaviśiṣṭaṃ brahma vijñāyata iti , ucyate — ‘ prāṇo brahma kaṃ brahma khaṃ brahma ’ (chā. u. 4 | 10 | 4) ityetadagnīnāṃ vacanaṃ śrutvopakosala uvāca — ‘ vijānāmyahaṃ yatprāṇo brahma , kaṃ ca tu khaṃ ca na vijānāmi ’ iti | tatredaṃ prativacanam — ‘ yadvāva kaṃ tadeva khaṃ yadeva khaṃ tadeva kam ’ iti | tatra khaṃ śabdo bhūtākāśe nirūḍho loke | yadi tasya viśeṣaṇatvena kaṃśabdaḥ sukhavācī nopādīyeta , tathā sati kevale bhūtākāśe brahmaśabdo nāmādiṣviva pratīkābhiprāyeṇa prayukta iti pratītiḥ syāt | tathā kaṃśabdasya viṣayendriyasamparkajanite sāmaye sukhe prasiddhatvāt , yadi tasya khaṃśabdo viśeṣaṇatvena nopādīyeta ; laukikaṃ sukhaṃ brahmeti pratītiḥ syāt | itaretara viśeṣitau tu kaṃkhaṃśabdau sukhātmakaṃ brahma gamayataḥ | tatra dvitīye brahmaśabde'nupādīyamāne ‘ kaṃ khaṃ brahma ’ ityevocyamāne kaṃśabdasya viśeṣaṇatvenaivopayuktatvātsukhasya guṇasyādhyeyatvaṃ syāt | tanmā bhūt — ityubhayoḥ kaṃkhaṃśabdayorbrahmaśabdaśirastvam — ‘ kaṃ brahma khaṃ brahma ’ iti | iṣṭaṃ hi sukhasyāpi guṇasya guṇivaddhyeyatvam | tadevaṃ vākyopakrame sukhaviśiṣṭaṃ brahmopadiṣṭam | pratyekaṃ ca gārhapatyādayo'gnayaḥ svaṃ svaṃ mahimānamupadiśya ‘ eṣā somya te'smadvidyātmavidyā ca ’ ityupasaṃharantaḥ pūrvatra brahma nirdiṣṭamiti jñāpayanti | ‘ ācāryastu te gatiṃ vaktā ’ iti ca gatimātrābhidhānapratijñānamarthāntaravivakṣāṃ vārayati | ‘ yathā puṣkarapalāśa āpo na śliṣyanta evamevaṃvidi pāpaṃ karma na śliṣyate ’ (chā. u. 4 | 14 | 3) iti cākṣisthānaṃ puruṣaṃ vijānataḥ pāpenānupaghātaṃ bruvannakṣisthānasya puruṣasya brahmatvaṃ darśayati | tasmātprakṛtasyaiva brahmaṇo'kṣisthānatāṃ saṃyadvāmatvādiguṇatāṃ coktvā arcirādikāṃ tadvido gatiṃ vakṣyāmītyupakramate — ‘ ya eṣo'kṣiṇi puruṣo dṛśyata eṣa ātmeti hovāca ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 1) iti || 15 ||
