records.literal_translation: Further, there should be no dispute here over whether Brahman is taught in this sentence or not. Brahmanhood is established simply from being taught as qualified by bliss. The bliss-qualified Brahman introduced earlier ('prana is Brahman; ka is Brahman; kha is Brahman') is what is taught here, since taking the topic already in progress is methodologically proper, and because the pledge 'your teacher will tell you the path' indicates only path-exposition, not a new topic. How is bliss-qualified Brahman known there? After hearing the fires say 'prana is Brahman, ka is Brahman, kha is Brahman,' Upakosala says: I know prana as Brahman, but I do not know ka and kha. The reply: what is ka is indeed kha; what is kha is indeed ka. In common usage kha is fixed for physical space. If ka (bliss-signifying) were not taken as qualifier, one might think only physical space is intended and Brahman-word is used symbolically like in name-symbol meditations. Conversely, ka is also known for sensory pleasure; if not qualified by kha, one may think ordinary pleasure is Brahman. But mutually qualifying ka and kha indicate bliss-natured Brahman. If the second Brahman-word were omitted ('ka kha Brahman'), ka would function merely as qualifier and bliss as a quality alone might become the meditated object; to avoid that, both ka and kha are each crowned with Brahman ('ka Brahman, kha Brahman'). It is desirable that even a quality such as bliss be meditated as inseparable from its possessor. Thus at the section-opening bliss-qualified Brahman is taught. And each sacred fire, after teaching its own glory, concludes 'this, dear one, is our knowledge and the Self-knowledge,' indicating that Brahman was already taught earlier. The statement 'the teacher will tell you the path' also blocks intention of a different subject. And saying that for one who knows the person in the eye sin does not cling establishes that the person in the eye is Brahman. Therefore, after stating eye-location and samyadvama-like qualities of the already introduced Brahman, the text proceeds to teach the path of one who knows that Brahman, beginning: 'the person seen in the eye - this is the Self.' (15)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.15 secures continuity of the Upakosala section: ka/kha reinterpretation yields sukha-visista Brahman, and eye-person plus path teaching are read as downstream unfoldings of that single Brahman topic.

records.source_text: सुखविशिष्टाभिधानादेव च ॥ १५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: sukhaviśiṣṭābhidhānādeva ca || 15 ||
records.transliteration: sukhaviśiṣṭābhidhānādeva ca || 15 ||
records.literal_translation: And indeed from being taught as qualified by bliss. (15)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.15: Brahman-reference is fixed because the prior topic is explicitly sukha-visista Brahman.

records.source_text: इत श्चा क्षिस्थानः पुरुषः परमेश्वरः , यस्माच्छ्रुतोपनिषत्कस्य श्रुतरहस्यविज्ञानस्य ब्रह्मविदो या गतिर्देवयानाख्या प्रसिद्धा श्रुतौ — ‘ अ थो त्तरेण तपसा ब्रह्मचर्येण श्रद्धया विद्ययात्मानमन्विष्यादित्यमभिजयन्ते । एतद्वै प्राणानामायतनमेतदमृतमभयमेतत्परायणमेतस्मान्न पुनरावर्तन्ते ’ (प्र. उ. १ । १०) इति , स्मृतावपि — ‘ अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् । तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ’ (भ. गी. ८ । २४) इति , सैवेहाक्षिपुरुषविदोऽभिधीयमाना दृश्यते । ‘ अथ यदु चैवास्मिञ्छव्यं कुर्वन्ति यदि च नार्चिषमेवाभिसम्भवन्ति ’ इत्युपक्रम्य ‘ आदित्याच्चन्द्रमसं चन्द्रमसो विद्युतं तत्पुरुषोऽमानवः स एनान्ब्रह्म गमयत्येष देवपथो ब्रह्मपथ एतेन प्रतिपद्यमाना इमं मानवमावर्तं नावर्तन्ते ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । ५) इति । तदिह ब्रह्मविद्विषयया प्रसिद्धया गत्या अक्षिस्थानस्य ब्रह्मत्वं निश्चीयते ॥ १६ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ita ścā kṣisthānaḥ puruṣaḥ parameśvaraḥ , yasmācchrutopaniṣatkasya śrutarahasyavijñānasya brahmavido yā gatirdevayānākhyā prasiddhā śrutau — ‘ a tho ttareṇa tapasā brahmacaryeṇa śraddhayā vidyayātmānamanviṣyādityamabhijayante | etadvai prāṇānāmāyatanametadamṛtamabhayametatparāyaṇametasmānna punarāvartante ’ (pra. u. 1 | 10) iti , smṛtāvapi — ‘ agnirjyotirahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇmāsā uttarāyaṇam | tatra prayātā gacchanti brahma brahmavido janāḥ ’ (bha. gī. 8 | 24) iti , saivehākṣipuruṣavido'bhidhīyamānā dṛśyate | ‘ atha yadu caivāsmiñchavyaṃ kurvanti yadi ca nārciṣamevābhisambhavanti ’ ityupakramya ‘ ādityāccandramasaṃ candramaso vidyutaṃ tatpuruṣo'mānavaḥ sa enānbrahma gamayatyeṣa devapatho brahmapatha etena pratipadyamānā imaṃ mānavamāvartaṃ nāvartante ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 5) iti | tadiha brahmavidviṣayayā prasiddhayā gatyā akṣisthānasya brahmatvaṃ niścīyate || 16 ||
records.transliteration: ita ścā kṣisthānaḥ puruṣaḥ parameśvaraḥ , yasmācchrutopaniṣatkasya śrutarahasyavijñānasya brahmavido yā gatirdevayānākhyā prasiddhā śrutau — ‘ a tho ttareṇa tapasā brahmacaryeṇa śraddhayā vidyayātmānamanviṣyādityamabhijayante | etadvai prāṇānāmāyatanametadamṛtamabhayametatparāyaṇametasmānna punarāvartante ’ (pra. u. 1 | 10) iti , smṛtāvapi — ‘ agnirjyotirahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇmāsā uttarāyaṇam | tatra prayātā gacchanti brahma brahmavido janāḥ ’ (bha. gī. 8 | 24) iti , saivehākṣipuruṣavido'bhidhīyamānā dṛśyate | ‘ atha yadu caivāsmiñchavyaṃ kurvanti yadi ca nārciṣamevābhisambhavanti ’ ityupakramya ‘ ādityāccandramasaṃ candramaso vidyutaṃ tatpuruṣo'mānavaḥ sa enānbrahma gamayatyeṣa devapatho brahmapatha etena pratipadyamānā imaṃ mānavamāvartaṃ nāvartante ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 5) iti | tadiha brahmavidviṣayayā prasiddhayā gatyā akṣisthānasya brahmatvaṃ niścīyate || 16 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the person in the eye is the Supreme Lord: the devayana path known in shruti as belonging to the knower of Brahman and one who has received the hidden teaching, and also known in smriti, is seen here as being taught for the knower of the eye-person. Beginning with mention of the archis path and continuing through sun, moon, lightning, and the non-human guide to Brahman, the text says those who proceed by this divine path do not return to the human cycle. Hence, by this well-known path associated with knowers of Brahman, the Brahmanhood of the eye-located person is decisively established. (16)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.16 uses gati-lakshana: the arciradi/devayana sequence tied to brahmavid in canonical sources confirms akshi-purusha as Brahman.

records.literal_translation: And because of mention of the path of one who has heard the Upanishadic secret. (16)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.16: shrutopanishatka-gati attribution indicates Brahman, not a lower referent.

records.source_text: यत्पुनरुक्तं छायात्मा , विज्ञानात्मा , देवतात्मा वा स्यादक्षिस्थान इति , अत्रोच्यते — न च्छायात्मादिरितर इह ग्रहणमर्हति । कस्मात् ? अनवस्थितेः । न तावच्छायात्मनश्चक्षुषि नित्यमवस्थानं सम्भवति । यदैव हि कश्चित्पुरुष श्चक्षुरासीदति , तदा चक्षुषि पुरुषच्छाया दृश्यते । अपगते तस्मिन्न दृश्यते । ‘ य एषो ऽक्षिणि पुरुषः ’ इति च श्रुतिः सन्निधानात्स्वचक्षुषि दृश्यमानं पुरुषमुपास्यत्वेनोपदिशति । न चो पासनाकाले च्छायाकरं कञ्चित्पुरुषं चक्षुःसमीपे सन्निधाप्योपास्त इति युक्तं कल्पयितुम् । ‘ अ स्यै व शरीरस्य नाशमन्वेष नश्यति ’ (छा. उ. ८ । ९ । १) इति श्रुतिश्छायात्मनोऽप्यनवस्थितत्वं दर्शयति । असम्भवाच्च तस्मिन्नमृतत्वादीनां गुणानां न च्छायात्मनि प्रतीतिः । तथा विज्ञानात्मनोऽपि साधारणे कृत्स्नशरीरेन्द्रियसम्बन्धे सति न चक्षुष्येवावस्थितत्वं शक्यं वक्तुम् । ब्रह्मणस्तु सर्वव्यापिनोऽपि दृष्ट उपलब्ध्यर्थो हृदयादिदेशविशेषसम्बन्धः । समानश्च विज्ञानात्मन्यप्यमृतत्वादीनां गुणानामसम्भवः । यद्यपि विज्ञानात्मा परमात्मनोऽनन्य एव , तथाप्यविद्याकामकर्मकृतं तस्मिन्मर्त्यत्वमध्यारोपितं भयं चेत्यमृतत्वाभयत्वे नोपपद्येते । संयद्वामत्वादयश्चैतस्मिन्ननैश्वर्यादनुपपन्ना एव । देवतात्मनस्तु ‘ रश्मिभिरेषोऽस्मिन्प्रतिष्ठितः ’ इति श्रुतेः यद्यपि चक्षुष्यवस्थानं स्यात् , तथाप्यात्मत्वं तावन्न सम्भवति , पराग्रूपत्वात् । अमृतत्वादयोऽपि न सम्भवन्ति , उत्पत्तिप्रलयश्रवणात् । अमरत्वमपि देवानां चिरकालावस्थानापेक्षम् । ऐश्वर्यमपि परमेश्वरायत्तम् , न स्वाभाविकम् ; ‘ भीषास्माद्वातः पवते । भीषोदेति सूर्यः । भीषास्मादग्निश्चेन्द्रश्च । मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ’ (तै. उ. २ । ८ । १) इति मन्त्रवर्णात् । तस्मात्परमेश्वर एवायमक्षिस्थानः प्रत्येतव्यः । अस्मिं श्च पक्षे ‘ दृश्यते ’ इति प्रसिद्धवदुपादानं शास्त्राद्यपेक्षं विद्वद्विषयं प्ररोचनार्थमिति व्याख्येयम् ॥ १७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: yatpunaruktaṃ chāyātmā , vijñānātmā , devatātmā vā syādakṣisthāna iti , atrocyate — na cchāyātmādiritara iha grahaṇamarhati | kasmāt ? anavasthiteḥ | na tāvacchāyātmanaścakṣuṣi nityamavasthānaṃ sambhavati | yadaiva hi kaścitpuruṣa ścakṣurāsīdati , tadā cakṣuṣi puruṣacchāyā dṛśyate | apagate tasminna dṛśyate | ‘ ya eṣo 'kṣiṇi puruṣaḥ ’ iti ca śrutiḥ sannidhānātsvacakṣuṣi dṛśyamānaṃ puruṣamupāsyatvenopadiśati | na co pāsanākāle cchāyākaraṃ kañcitpuruṣaṃ cakṣuḥsamīpe sannidhāpyopāsta iti yuktaṃ kalpayitum | ‘ a syai va śarīrasya nāśamanveṣa naśyati ’ (chā. u. 8 | 9 | 1) iti śrutiśchāyātmano'pyanavasthitatvaṃ darśayati | asambhavācca tasminnamṛtatvādīnāṃ guṇānāṃ na cchāyātmani pratītiḥ | tathā vijñānātmano'pi sādhāraṇe kṛtsnaśarīrendriyasambandhe sati na cakṣuṣyevāvasthitatvaṃ śakyaṃ vaktum | brahmaṇastu sarvavyāpino'pi dṛṣṭa upalabdhyartho hṛdayādideśaviśeṣasambandhaḥ | samānaśca vijñānātmanyapyamṛtatvādīnāṃ guṇānāmasambhavaḥ | yadyapi vijñānātmā paramātmano'nanya eva , tathāpyavidyākāmakarmakṛtaṃ tasminmartyatvamadhyāropitaṃ bhayaṃ cetyamṛtatvābhayatve nopapadyete | saṃyadvāmatvādayaścaitasminnanaiśvaryādanupapannā eva | devatātmanastu ‘ raśmibhireṣo'sminpratiṣṭhitaḥ ’ iti śruteḥ yadyapi cakṣuṣyavasthānaṃ syāt , tathāpyātmatvaṃ tāvanna sambhavati , parāgrūpatvāt | amṛtatvādayo'pi na sambhavanti , utpattipralayaśravaṇāt | amaratvamapi devānāṃ cirakālāvasthānāpekṣam | aiśvaryamapi parameśvarāyattam , na svābhāvikam ; ‘ bhīṣāsmādvātaḥ pavate | bhīṣodeti sūryaḥ | bhīṣāsmādagniścendraśca | mṛtyurdhāvati pañcamaḥ ’ (tai. u. 2 | 8 | 1) iti mantravarṇāt | tasmātparameśvara evāyamakṣisthānaḥ pratyetavyaḥ | asmiṃ śca pakṣe ‘ dṛśyate ’ iti prasiddhavadupādānaṃ śāstrādyapekṣaṃ vidvadviṣayaṃ prarocanārthamiti vyākhyeyam || 17 ||

records.literal_translation: As for the claim that the eye-located referent may instead be reflection-self, knowing self, or deity-self, this is answered: none of these alternatives can be accepted here. Why? Because of non-stability and non-fit. Reflection-self is not continuously present in the eye: it appears only when a person is near and disappears when absent. But the statement 'the person in the eye' teaches as object of contemplation what is to be seen in one's own eye by immediate relation; it is not reasonable to imagine that, during worship, one places some shadow-producing person nearby and meditates on that. Another text also shows non-stability of shadow-self. And immortality and related attributes do not fit reflection-self. Likewise for knowing self: since it has a general relation to the whole body and senses, one cannot restrict it to eye-location alone (whereas for Brahman, though all-pervasive, specific locations like heart are taught for realization). Immortality and fearlessness also do not fit the empirical knowing self: though non-different from the Supreme ultimately, in empirical superimposition mortality and fear are imposed through ignorance, desire, and action. Samyadvama and related lordly attributes likewise do not fit it due to non-sovereignty. For deity-self, even if eye-location is granted from ray-related texts, primary selfhood does not fit because it is still other-oriented; immortality etc. do not truly fit due to scriptural accounts of origin and dissolution; even deity-immortality is only long duration. Its power too is dependent on the Supreme, not intrinsic, as texts of cosmic fear make clear. Therefore the eye-located one should be understood as the Supreme Lord alone. In this reading, the phrase 'is seen' is to be interpreted as a pedagogical, scripture-informed presentation oriented toward the learned, meant to inspire contemplative engagement.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.17 excludes reflection/jiva/devata candidates by anavasthiti and guna-asambhava filters, preserving Ishvara as the sole stable referent of akshi-purusha.

records.source_text: अनवस्थितेरसम्भवाच्च नेतरः ॥ १७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: anavasthiterasambhavācca netaraḥ || 17 ||
records.transliteration: anavasthiterasambhavācca netaraḥ || 17 ||
records.literal_translation: Not the other one, because of non-stability and because of non-applicability. (17)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.17: alternative candidates are rejected on anavasthiti and asambhava grounds.

records.source_text: ‘ य इमं च लोकं परं च लोकं सर्वाणि च भूतानि योऽन्तरो यमयति ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । १) इत्युपक्रम्य श्रूयते — ‘ यः पृथिव्यां तिष्ठन्पृथिव्या अन्तरो यं पृथिवी न वेद यस्य पृथिवी शरीरं यः पृथिवीमन्तरो यमयत्येष त आत्मान्तर्याम्यमृतः ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । ३) इत्यादि । अ त्रा धिदैवतमधिलोकमधिवेदमधियज्ञमधिभूतमध्यात्मं च कश्चिदन्तरवस्थितो यमयिता अन्तर्यामीति श्रूयते । स किमधिदैवाद्यभिमानी देवतात्मा कश्चित् , किं वा प्राप्ताणिमाद्यैश्वर्यः कश्चिद्योगी , किं वा परमात्मा , किं वार्थान्तरं किञ्चित् , इत्यपूर्वसंज्ञादर्शनात्संशयः । किं तावन्नः प्रतिभाति ? संज्ञाया अप्रसिद्धत्वात्संज्ञिनाप्यप्रसिद्धेनार्थान्तरेण केनचिद्भवितव्यमिति । अथवा नानिरूपितरूपमर्थान्तरं शक्यमस्त्यभ्युपगन्तुम् । अन्तर्यामिशब्दश्चान्तर्यमनयोगेन प्रवृत्तो नात्यन्तमप्रसिद्धः । तस्मात्पृथिव्याद्यभिमानी कश्चिद्देवोऽन्तर्यामी स्यात् । तथा च श्रूयते — ‘ पृथिव्येव यस्यायतनमग्निर्लोको मनो ज्योतिः ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ९ । १०) इत्यादि । स च कार्यकरणवत्त्वात्पृथिव्यादीनन्तस्तिष्ठन्यमयतीति युक्तं देवतात्मनो यमयितृत्वम् । योगिनो वा कस्यचित्सिद्धस्य सर्वानुप्रवेशेन यमयितृत्वं स्यात् । न तु परमात्मा प्रतीयेत , अकार्यकरणत्वात् — इ त्येवं प्राप्ते इदमुच्यते —
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ ya imaṃ ca lokaṃ paraṃ ca lokaṃ sarvāṇi ca bhūtāni yo'ntaro yamayati ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 1) ityupakramya śrūyate — ‘ yaḥ pṛthivyāṃ tiṣṭhanpṛthivyā antaro yaṃ pṛthivī na veda yasya pṛthivī śarīraṃ yaḥ pṛthivīmantaro yamayatyeṣa ta ātmāntaryāmyamṛtaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 3) ityādi | a trā dhidaivatamadhilokamadhivedamadhiyajñamadhibhūtamadhyātmaṃ ca kaścidantaravasthito yamayitā antaryāmīti śrūyate | sa kimadhidaivādyabhimānī devatātmā kaścit , kiṃ vā prāptāṇimādyaiśvaryaḥ kaścidyogī , kiṃ vā paramātmā , kiṃ vārthāntaraṃ kiñcit , ityapūrvasaṃjñādarśanātsaṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāvannaḥ pratibhāti ? saṃjñāyā aprasiddhatvātsaṃjñināpyaprasiddhenārthāntareṇa kenacidbhavitavyamiti | athavā nānirūpitarūpamarthāntaraṃ śakyamastyabhyupagantum | antaryāmiśabdaścāntaryamanayogena pravṛtto nātyantamaprasiddhaḥ | tasmātpṛthivyādyabhimānī kaściddevo'ntaryāmī syāt | tathā ca śrūyate — ‘ pṛthivyeva yasyāyatanamagnirloko mano jyotiḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 9 | 10) ityādi | sa ca kāryakaraṇavattvātpṛthivyādīnantastiṣṭhanyamayatīti yuktaṃ devatātmano yamayitṛtvam | yogino vā kasyacitsiddhasya sarvānupraveśena yamayitṛtvaṃ syāt | na tu paramātmā pratīyeta , akāryakaraṇatvāt — i tyevaṃ prāpte idamucyate —
records.transliteration: ‘ ya imaṃ ca lokaṃ paraṃ ca lokaṃ sarvāṇi ca bhūtāni yo'ntaro yamayati ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 1) ityupakramya śrūyate — ‘ yaḥ pṛthivyāṃ tiṣṭhanpṛthivyā antaro yaṃ pṛthivī na veda yasya pṛthivī śarīraṃ yaḥ pṛthivīmantaro yamayatyeṣa ta ātmāntaryāmyamṛtaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 3) ityādi | a trā dhidaivatamadhilokamadhivedamadhiyajñamadhibhūtamadhyātmaṃ ca kaścidantaravasthito yamayitā antaryāmīti śrūyate | sa kimadhidaivādyabhimānī devatātmā kaścit , kiṃ vā prāptāṇimādyaiśvaryaḥ kaścidyogī , kiṃ vā paramātmā , kiṃ vārthāntaraṃ kiñcit , ityapūrvasaṃjñādarśanātsaṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāvannaḥ pratibhāti ? saṃjñāyā aprasiddhatvātsaṃjñināpyaprasiddhenārthāntareṇa kenacidbhavitavyamiti | athavā nānirūpitarūpamarthāntaraṃ śakyamastyabhyupagantum | antaryāmiśabdaścāntaryamanayogena pravṛtto nātyantamaprasiddhaḥ | tasmātpṛthivyādyabhimānī kaściddevo'ntaryāmī syāt | tathā ca śrūyate — ‘ pṛthivyeva yasyāyatanamagnirloko mano jyotiḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 9 | 10) ityādi | sa ca kāryakaraṇavattvātpṛthivyādīnantastiṣṭhanyamayatīti yuktaṃ devatātmano yamayitṛtvam | yogino vā kasyacitsiddhasya sarvānupraveśena yamayitṛtvaṃ syāt | na tu paramātmā pratīyeta , akāryakaraṇatvāt — i tyevaṃ prāpte idamucyate —
records.literal_translation: The text begins: 'He who, within this world, the other world, and all beings, rules from within,' and then says, 'He who stands in earth, within earth, whom earth does not know, whose body is earth, who rules earth from within - He is your Self, the inner controller, immortal,' and so on. Here, across deity, world, Veda, sacrifice, beings, and inner-self domains, a certain inward ruler is taught. Doubt arises: is this some deity-self presiding over those domains, or some yogin with attained subtle powers, or the Supreme Self, or some other previously unknown referent? Because an unprecedented designation is seen, uncertainty arises. One thought is: since the term is unfamiliar, perhaps a distinct unfamiliar entity is meant. Or, since an entirely undefined new entity is difficult to accept, perhaps 'inner-controller' by derivation points to some known inner-regulator, such as a domain-deity. Texts about domain deities are cited; and because such a deity has causal instruments, its inner regulating role may seem plausible. Or perhaps a perfected yogin with pervasive entry could regulate. Not the Supreme, one may say, since He has no action-instruments. In this prima facie state, it is said -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.2.18 sets multiple antaryamin candidates (deity/yogin/Brahman/other) based on term novelty and functional control considerations.

records.source_text: योऽन्तर्याम्यधिदैवादिषु श्रूयते , स परमात्मैव स्यात् , नान्य इति । कुतः ? तद्धर्मव्यपदेशात् । तस्य हि परमात्मनो धर्मा इह निर्दिश्यमाना दृश्यन्ते । पृथिव्यादि तावदधिदैवादिभेदभिन्नं समस्तं विकारजातमन्तस्तिष्ठन्यमयतीति परमात्मनो यमयितृत्वं धर्म उपपद्यते । सर्वविकारकारणत्वे सति सर्वशक्त्युपपत्तेः । ‘ एष त आत्मान्तर्याम्यमृतः ’ इति चात्मत्वामृतत्वे मुख्ये परमात्मन उपपद्येते । ‘ यं पृथिवी न वेद ’ इति च पृथिवीदेवताया अविज्ञेयमन्तर्यामिणं ब्रुवन्देवतात्मनोऽन्यमन्तर्यामिणं दर्शयति । पृथिवी देवता ह्यहमस्मि पृथिवीत्यात्मानं विजानीयात् । तथा ‘ अदृष्टोऽश्रुतः ’ इत्यादिव्यपदेशो रूपादिविहीनत्वात्परमात्मन उपपद्यत इति । यत्त्वकार्यकरणस्य परमात्मनो यमयितृत्वं नोपपद्यत इति , नैष दोषः ; यान्नियच्छति तत्कार्यकरणैरेव तस्य कार्यकरणवत्त्वोपपत्तेः । तस्या प्य न्यो नियन्तेत्यनवस्थादोषश्च न सम्भवति , भेदाभावात् । भेदे हि सत्यनवस्थादोषोपपत्तिः । तस्मात्परमात्मैवान्तर्यामी ॥ १८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: yo'ntaryāmyadhidaivādiṣu śrūyate , sa paramātmaiva syāt , nānya iti | kutaḥ ? taddharmavyapadeśāt | tasya hi paramātmano dharmā iha nirdiśyamānā dṛśyante | pṛthivyādi tāvadadhidaivādibhedabhinnaṃ samastaṃ vikārajātamantastiṣṭhanyamayatīti paramātmano yamayitṛtvaṃ dharma upapadyate | sarvavikārakāraṇatve sati sarvaśaktyupapatteḥ | ‘ eṣa ta ātmāntaryāmyamṛtaḥ ’ iti cātmatvāmṛtatve mukhye paramātmana upapadyete | ‘ yaṃ pṛthivī na veda ’ iti ca pṛthivīdevatāyā avijñeyamantaryāmiṇaṃ bruvandevatātmano'nyamantaryāmiṇaṃ darśayati | pṛthivī devatā hyahamasmi pṛthivītyātmānaṃ vijānīyāt | tathā ‘ adṛṣṭo'śrutaḥ ’ ityādivyapadeśo rūpādivihīnatvātparamātmana upapadyata iti | yattvakāryakaraṇasya paramātmano yamayitṛtvaṃ nopapadyata iti , naiṣa doṣaḥ ; yānniyacchati tatkāryakaraṇaireva tasya kāryakaraṇavattvopapatteḥ | tasyā pya nyo niyantetyanavasthādoṣaśca na sambhavati , bhedābhāvāt | bhede hi satyanavasthādoṣopapattiḥ | tasmātparamātmaivāntaryāmī || 18 ||
records.transliteration: yo'ntaryāmyadhidaivādiṣu śrūyate , sa paramātmaiva syāt , nānya iti | kutaḥ ? taddharmavyapadeśāt | tasya hi paramātmano dharmā iha nirdiśyamānā dṛśyante | pṛthivyādi tāvadadhidaivādibhedabhinnaṃ samastaṃ vikārajātamantastiṣṭhanyamayatīti paramātmano yamayitṛtvaṃ dharma upapadyate | sarvavikārakāraṇatve sati sarvaśaktyupapatteḥ | ‘ eṣa ta ātmāntaryāmyamṛtaḥ ’ iti cātmatvāmṛtatve mukhye paramātmana upapadyete | ‘ yaṃ pṛthivī na veda ’ iti ca pṛthivīdevatāyā avijñeyamantaryāmiṇaṃ bruvandevatātmano'nyamantaryāmiṇaṃ darśayati | pṛthivī devatā hyahamasmi pṛthivītyātmānaṃ vijānīyāt | tathā ‘ adṛṣṭo'śrutaḥ ’ ityādivyapadeśo rūpādivihīnatvātparamātmana upapadyata iti | yattvakāryakaraṇasya paramātmano yamayitṛtvaṃ nopapadyata iti , naiṣa doṣaḥ ; yānniyacchati tatkāryakaraṇaireva tasya kāryakaraṇavattvopapatteḥ | tasyā pya nyo niyantetyanavasthādoṣaśca na sambhavati , bhedābhāvāt | bhede hi satyanavasthādoṣopapattiḥ | tasmātparamātmaivāntaryāmī || 18 ||
records.literal_translation: The inner controller heard in deity and related domains must be the Supreme Self alone, not another. Why? Because the attributes stated there are His attributes. The role of inward regulator over all modifications across deity and other divisions coheres for the Supreme, since as cause of all modifications all powers are coherent in Him. The expressions 'your Self, the inner controller, immortal' fit primarily only the Supreme in both selfhood and immortality. And by saying 'whom earth does not know' with respect to the earth-deity, the text shows an inner controller distinct from that deity-self, who would otherwise know itself as 'I am earth.' Likewise expressions such as unseen and unheard fit the Supreme as devoid of sensory form. As for the objection that one without action-instruments cannot regulate, there is no fault: He regulates those with instruments through their own instruments. Nor does infinite regress arise ('another must regulate Him too'), because there is no real second distinct regulator there; regress requires real difference. Therefore the inner controller is the Supreme Self alone. (18)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.18 identifies Antaryamin as Brahman via taddharma-vyapadesha, distinctness from domain deities, and regress-blocking non-duality logic.

records.source_text: अन्तर्याम्यधिदैवादिषु तद्धर्मव्यपदेशात् ॥ १८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: antaryāmyadhidaivādiṣu taddharmavyapadeśāt || 18 ||
records.transliteration: antaryāmyadhidaivādiṣu taddharmavyapadeśāt || 18 ||
records.literal_translation: In the inner-controller passage regarding deity and related domains, because of designation of His attributes. (18)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.18: antaryamin is Brahman, established by explicit ascription of Brahmanic attributes.

records.source_text: स्यादेतत् — अदृष्टत्वादयो धर्माः साङ्ख्यस्मृतिकल्पितस्य प्रधानस्याप्युपपद्यन्ते , रूपादिहीनतया तस्य तैरभ्युपगमात् । ‘ अप्रतर्क्य म विज्ञेयं प्रसुप्तमिव सर्वतः ’ (मनु. १ । ५) इति हि स्मरन्ति । तस्यापि नियन्तृत्वं सर्वविकारकारणत्वादुपपद्यते । तस्मात्प्रधानमन्तर्यामिशब्दं स्यात् । ‘ ईक्षतेर्नाशब्दम् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । ५) इत्यत्र निराकृतमपि सत् प्रधानमिहादृष्टत्वादिव्यपदेशसम्भवेन पुनराशङ्क्यते । अत उत्तरमुच्यते — न च स्मार्तं प्रधानमन्तर्यामिशब्दं भवितुमर्हति । कस्मात् ? अतद्धर्माभिलापात् । यद्यप्यदृष्टत्वादिव्यपदेशः प्रधानस्य सम्भवति , तथापि न द्रष्टृत्वादिव्यपदेशः सम्भवति , प्रधानस्याचेतनत्वेन तैरभ्युपगमात् । ‘ अदृष्टो द्रष्टाश्रुतः श्रोतामतो मन्ताविज्ञातो विज्ञाता ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । २३) इति हि वाक्यशेष इह भवति । आत्मत्वमपि न प्रधानस्योपपद्यते ॥ १९ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: syādetat — adṛṣṭatvādayo dharmāḥ sāṅkhyasmṛtikalpitasya pradhānasyāpyupapadyante , rūpādihīnatayā tasya tairabhyupagamāt | ‘ apratarkya ma vijñeyaṃ prasuptamiva sarvataḥ ’ (manu. 1 | 5) iti hi smaranti | tasyāpi niyantṛtvaṃ sarvavikārakāraṇatvādupapadyate | tasmātpradhānamantaryāmiśabdaṃ syāt | ‘ īkṣaternāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) ityatra nirākṛtamapi sat pradhānamihādṛṣṭatvādivyapadeśasambhavena punarāśaṅkyate | ata uttaramucyate — na ca smārtaṃ pradhānamantaryāmiśabdaṃ bhavitumarhati | kasmāt ? ataddharmābhilāpāt | yadyapyadṛṣṭatvādivyapadeśaḥ pradhānasya sambhavati , tathāpi na draṣṭṛtvādivyapadeśaḥ sambhavati , pradhānasyācetanatvena tairabhyupagamāt | ‘ adṛṣṭo draṣṭāśrutaḥ śrotāmato mantāvijñāto vijñātā ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 23) iti hi vākyaśeṣa iha bhavati | ātmatvamapi na pradhānasyopapadyate || 19 ||
records.transliteration: syādetat — adṛṣṭatvādayo dharmāḥ sāṅkhyasmṛtikalpitasya pradhānasyāpyupapadyante , rūpādihīnatayā tasya tairabhyupagamāt | ‘ apratarkya ma vijñeyaṃ prasuptamiva sarvataḥ ’ (manu. 1 | 5) iti hi smaranti | tasyāpi niyantṛtvaṃ sarvavikārakāraṇatvādupapadyate | tasmātpradhānamantaryāmiśabdaṃ syāt | ‘ īkṣaternāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) ityatra nirākṛtamapi sat pradhānamihādṛṣṭatvādivyapadeśasambhavena punarāśaṅkyate | ata uttaramucyate — na ca smārtaṃ pradhānamantaryāmiśabdaṃ bhavitumarhati | kasmāt ? ataddharmābhilāpāt | yadyapyadṛṣṭatvādivyapadeśaḥ pradhānasya sambhavati , tathāpi na draṣṭṛtvādivyapadeśaḥ sambhavati , pradhānasyācetanatvena tairabhyupagamāt | ‘ adṛṣṭo draṣṭāśrutaḥ śrotāmato mantāvijñāto vijñātā ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 23) iti hi vākyaśeṣa iha bhavati | ātmatvamapi na pradhānasyopapadyate || 19 ||
records.literal_translation: One may object: properties like invisibility and so on can apply also to the Pradhana posited in Sankhya-smrti, since it is admitted there as devoid of form. Smrti says, 'unthinkable, unknowable, as if asleep on every side.' Its ruling function too could be argued from being cause of all modifications. Therefore perhaps 'inner controller' names Pradhana. Even though Pradhana was rejected earlier by 'because of seeing, not from word' (1.1.5), it is raised again here because statements of invisibility etc. seem possible. The reply: the Smrti-Pradhana cannot be the inner controller. Why? Because attributes not belonging to it are declared. Though invisibility-type predicates might be applied to Pradhana, seerhood-type predicates cannot, since it is admitted as non-conscious. The continuation here says, 'unseen, the seer; unheard, the hearer; unthought, the thinker; unknown, the knower.' Selfhood too cannot apply to Pradhana. (19)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.19 restages a Pradhana purvapaksha via adrsyatva compatibility, then rejects it by ataddharma-abhilapa: antaryamin text ascribes conscious knower predicates and atmatva, impossible for acaitanya Pradhana.

records.source_text: न च स्मार्तमतद्धर्माभिलापात् ॥ १९ ॥

records.transliteration: na ca smārtamataddharmābhilāpāt || 19 ||
records.literal_translation: And not the Smrti-based one, because attributes not belonging to it are spoken. (19)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.19: Smrti-Pradhana is excluded as antaryamin since the passage attributes non-Pradhana properties.

records.source_text: नेति पूर्वसूत्रादनुवर्तते । शारीरश्च नान्तर्यामी स्यात् । कस्मात् ? यद्यपि द्रष्टृत्वादयो धर्मास्तस्य सम्भवन्ति , तथापि घटाकाशवदुपाधिपरिच्छिन्नत्वान्न कार्त्स्न्येन पृथिव्यादिष्वन्तरवस्थातुं नियन्तुं च शक्नोति । अपि चोभयेऽपि हि शाखिनः काण्वा माध्यन्दिनाश्चान्तर्यामिणो भेदेनैनं शारीरं पृथिव्यादिवदधिष्ठानत्वेन नियम्यत्वेन चाधीयते — ‘ यो विज्ञाने तिष्ठन् ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ७ । २२) इति काण्वाः । ‘ य आत्मनि तिष्ठन् ’ इति माध्यन्दिनाः । ‘ य आत्मनि तिष्ठन् ’ इत्यस्मिंस्तावत् पाठे भवत्यात्मशब्दः शारीरस्य वाचकः । ‘ यो विज्ञाने तिष्ठन् ’ इत्यस्मिन्नपि पाठे विज्ञानशब्देन शारीर उच्यते , विज्ञानमयो हि शारीर इति । तस्माच्छारीरादन्य ईश्वरोऽन्तर्यामीति सिद्धम् । कथं पुनरेकस्मिन्देहे द्वौ द्रष्टारावुपपद्येते यश्चायमीश्वरोऽन्तर्यामी , यश्चायमितरः शारीरः ? का पुन रि हानुपपत्तिः ? ‘ नान्यो ऽतोऽस्ति द्रष्टा ’ इत्यादिश्रुतिवचनं विरुध्येत । अत्र हि प्रकृतादन्तर्यामिणोऽन्यं द्रष्टारं श्रोतारं मन्तारं विज्ञातारं चात्मानं प्रतिषेधति । नियन्त्रन्तर प्रतिषेधार्थमेतद्वचनमिति चेत् , न ; नियन्त्रन्तराप्रसङ्गा दविशेष श्रवणाच्च । अत्रोच्यते — अविद्याप्रत्युपस्थापितकार्यकरणोपाधिनिमित्तोऽयं शारीरान्तर्यामिणोर्भेदव्यपदेशः , न पारमार्थिकः । एको हि प्रत्यगात्मा भवति , न द्वौ प्रत्यगात्मानौ सम्भवतः । एकस्यैव तु भेदव्यवहार उपाधिकृतः , यथा घटाकाशो महाकाश इति । ततश्च ज्ञातृज्ञेयादिभेदश्रुतयः प्रत्यक्षादीनि च प्रमाणानि संसारानुभवो विधिप्रतिषेधशास्त्रं चेति सर्वमेतदुपपद्यते । तथा च श्रुतिः — ‘ यत्र हि द्वैतमिव भवति तदितर इतरं पश्यति ’ इत्यविद्याविषये सर्वं व्यवहारं दर्शयति । ‘ यत्र त्वस्य सर्वमात्मैवाभूत्तत्केन कं पश्येत् ’ इति विद्याविषये सर्वं व्यवहारं वारयति ॥ २० ॥
records.transliteration_iast: neti pūrvasūtrādanuvartate | śārīraśca nāntaryāmī syāt | kasmāt ? yadyapi draṣṭṛtvādayo dharmāstasya sambhavanti , tathāpi ghaṭākāśavadupādhiparicchinnatvānna kārtsnyena pṛthivyādiṣvantaravasthātuṃ niyantuṃ ca śaknoti | api cobhaye'pi hi śākhinaḥ kāṇvā mādhyandināścāntaryāmiṇo bhedenainaṃ śārīraṃ pṛthivyādivadadhiṣṭhānatvena niyamyatvena cādhīyate — ‘ yo vijñāne tiṣṭhan ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 7 | 22) iti kāṇvāḥ | ‘ ya ātmani tiṣṭhan ’ iti mādhyandināḥ | ‘ ya ātmani tiṣṭhan ’ ityasmiṃstāvat pāṭhe bhavatyātmaśabdaḥ śārīrasya vācakaḥ | ‘ yo vijñāne tiṣṭhan ’ ityasminnapi pāṭhe vijñānaśabdena śārīra ucyate , vijñānamayo hi śārīra iti | tasmācchārīrādanya īśvaro'ntaryāmīti siddham | kathaṃ punarekasmindehe dvau draṣṭārāvupapadyete yaścāyamīśvaro'ntaryāmī , yaścāyamitaraḥ śārīraḥ ? kā puna ri hānupapattiḥ ? ‘ nānyo 'to'sti draṣṭā ’ ityādiśrutivacanaṃ virudhyeta | atra hi prakṛtādantaryāmiṇo'nyaṃ draṣṭāraṃ śrotāraṃ mantāraṃ vijñātāraṃ cātmānaṃ pratiṣedhati | niyantrantara pratiṣedhārthametadvacanamiti cet , na ; niyantrantarāprasaṅgā daviśeṣa śravaṇācca | atrocyate — avidyāpratyupasthāpitakāryakaraṇopādhinimitto'yaṃ śārīrāntaryāmiṇorbhedavyapadeśaḥ , na pāramārthikaḥ | eko hi pratyagātmā bhavati , na dvau pratyagātmānau sambhavataḥ | ekasyaiva tu bhedavyavahāra upādhikṛtaḥ , yathā ghaṭākāśo mahākāśa iti | tataśca jñātṛjñeyādibhedaśrutayaḥ pratyakṣādīni ca pramāṇāni saṃsārānubhavo vidhipratiṣedhaśāstraṃ ceti sarvametadupapadyate | tathā ca śrutiḥ — ‘ yatra hi dvaitamiva bhavati taditara itaraṃ paśyati ’ ityavidyāviṣaye sarvaṃ vyavahāraṃ darśayati | ‘ yatra tvasya sarvamātmaivābhūttatkena kaṃ paśyet ’ iti vidyāviṣaye sarvaṃ vyavahāraṃ vārayati || 20 ||

records.literal_translation: 'Not' continues from the previous sutra: and the embodied self too is not the inner controller. Why? Although seerhood and related predicates apply to it, being delimited by adjuncts like pot-space it cannot wholly abide within earth and all entities and govern them. Also, in both recensions (Kanva and Madhyandina), the embodied self is read as distinct from the inner controller and as something governed, like earth etc.: Kanvas read 'who stands in knowledge'; Madhyandinas read 'who stands in the self.' In the latter, 'self' denotes the embodied self; in the former too, 'knowledge' denotes the embodied self (as knowledge-formed). Hence an Ishvara other than the embodied self is established as inner controller. But how can two seers be in one body, this inner-controlling Ishvara and this other embodied self? What is the inconsistency here? One might say sruti 'there is no other seer than this' would be contradicted, since it denies any seer, hearer, thinker, knower other than the inner controller. If one says that sentence merely denies another regulator, no: another regulator is not at issue, and the hearing is unqualified. Reply: this distinction between embodied self and inner controller is due to body-sense adjuncts projected by ignorance, not ultimate. In truth there is one inward self, not two. Difference-talk is adjunct-caused, as pot-space and great-space. Thus scriptures of knower-known difference, perception and other pramanas, worldly experience, and injunction-prohibition teaching all stand. Sruti itself marks all transaction as in the sphere of ignorance: 'where there is, as it were, duality, one sees another'; and in knowledge it halts all transaction: 'where everything has become the Self, by what should one see whom?' (20)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.20 excludes jiva as antaryamin at vyavahara level (upadhi-paricchinna incapacity), while reconciling non-dual sruti through avidya-based bheda-vyavahara and paramarthika eka-atman.

records.source_text: यदि प्रधान मा त्मत्वद्रष्टृत्वाद्यसम्भवान्नान्तर्याम्यभ्युपगम्यते , शारीरस्तर्ह्यन्तर्यामी भवतु । शारीरो हि चेतनत्वाद्द्रष्टा श्रोता मन्ता विज्ञाता च भवति , आत्मा च प्रत्यक्त्वात् । अमृतश्च , धर्माधर्मफलोपभोगोपपत्तेः । अदृष्टत्वादयश्च धर्माः शारीरे प्रसिद्धाः । दर्शनादिक्रियायाः कर्तरि प्रवृत्तिविरोधात् । ‘ न दृष्टेर्द्रष्टारं पश्येः ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ४ । २) इत्यादिश्रुतिभ्यश्च । तस्य च कार्यकरणसङ्घातमन्तर्यमयितुं शीलम् , भोक्तृत्वात् । तस्माच्छारीरोऽन्तर्यामीत्यत उत्तरं पठति —
records.transliteration_iast: yadi pradhāna mā tmatvadraṣṭṛtvādyasambhavānnāntaryāmyabhyupagamyate , śārīrastarhyantaryāmī bhavatu | śārīro hi cetanatvāddraṣṭā śrotā mantā vijñātā ca bhavati , ātmā ca pratyaktvāt | amṛtaśca , dharmādharmaphalopabhogopapatteḥ | adṛṣṭatvādayaśca dharmāḥ śārīre prasiddhāḥ | darśanādikriyāyāḥ kartari pravṛttivirodhāt | ‘ na dṛṣṭerdraṣṭāraṃ paśyeḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 4 | 2) ityādiśrutibhyaśca | tasya ca kāryakaraṇasaṅghātamantaryamayituṃ śīlam , bhoktṛtvāt | tasmācchārīro'ntaryāmītyata uttaraṃ paṭhati —
records.transliteration: yadi pradhāna mā tmatvadraṣṭṛtvādyasambhavānnāntaryāmyabhyupagamyate , śārīrastarhyantaryāmī bhavatu | śārīro hi cetanatvāddraṣṭā śrotā mantā vijñātā ca bhavati , ātmā ca pratyaktvāt | amṛtaśca , dharmādharmaphalopabhogopapatteḥ | adṛṣṭatvādayaśca dharmāḥ śārīre prasiddhāḥ | darśanādikriyāyāḥ kartari pravṛttivirodhāt | ‘ na dṛṣṭerdraṣṭāraṃ paśyeḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 4 | 2) ityādiśrutibhyaśca | tasya ca kāryakaraṇasaṅghātamantaryamayituṃ śīlam , bhoktṛtvāt | tasmācchārīro'ntaryāmītyata uttaraṃ paṭhati —
records.literal_translation: If Pradhana is not accepted as inner controller due to impossibility of selfhood and seerhood etc., then let the embodied self be inner controller. For the embodied self, being conscious, is seer, hearer, thinker, and knower, and is self by inwardness. It is also immortal, given the intelligibility of experiencing fruits of dharma and adharma. Invisibility and related properties are known for the embodied self too, since perceptual actions cannot operate upon their own agent, and from srutis such as 'you cannot see the seer of seeing.' It is also disposed to regulate the body-sense aggregate from within, because it is the enjoyer. Therefore the embodied self is inner controller. To this, the answer-sutra is read -
records.technical_translation: Purvapaksha before 1.2.20 shifts from Pradhana to jiva: conscious-agent and inward-self predicates are marshaled to claim antaryamin status.

records.source_text: शारीरश्चोभयेऽपि हि भेदेनैनमधीयते ॥ २० ॥
records.transliteration_iast: śārīraścobhaye'pi hi bhedenainamadhīyate || 20 ||
records.transliteration: śārīraścobhaye'pi hi bhedenainamadhīyate || 20 ||
records.literal_translation: And the embodied self too; for in both recensions this one is studied as distinct. (20)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.20: jiva is not antaryamin, since both textual recensions treat it as distinct from the inner controller.

records.source_text: ‘ अथ परा यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ’(मु. उ. १ । १ । ५) , ‘ यत्त द द्रेश्यमग्राह्यमगोत्रमवर्णमचक्षुःश्रोत्रं तदपाणिपादम् , नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं तदव्ययं यद्भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति धीराः ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ६) इति श्रूयते । तत्र संशयः — किमयमदृश्यत्वादिगुणको भूतयोनिः प्रधानं स्यात् , उत शारीरः , आहोस्वित्परमेश्वर इति । तत्र प्रधानमचेतनं भूतयोनिरिति युक्तम् , अचेतनानामेव तस्य दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपादानात् । ‘ यथोर्णनाभिः सृजते गृह्णते च यथा पृथिव्यामोषधयः सम्भवन्ति । यथा सतः पुरुषात्केशलोमानि तथाक्षरात्सम्भवतीह विश्वम् ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ७) इति । ननूर्णनाभिः पुरुषश्च चेतनाविह दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपात्तौ ; नेति ब्रूमः । न हि केवलस्य चेतनस्य तत्र सूत्रयोनित्वं केशलोमयोनित्वं चास्ति । चेतनाधिष्ठितं ह्यचेतनमूर्णनाभिशरीरं सूत्रस्य योनिः , पुरुषशरीरं च केशलोम्नामिति प्रसिद्धम् । अपि च पूर्वत्रादृष्टत्वाद्यभिलापसम्भवेऽपि द्रष्टृत्वाद्यभिलापासम्भवान्न प्रधानमभ्युपगतम् । इह त्वदृश्यत्वादयो धर्माः प्रधाने सम्भवन्ति । न चात्र विरुध्यमानो धर्मः कश्चिदभिलप्यते । ननु ‘ यः सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित् ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ९) इत्ययं वाक्यशेषोऽचेतने प्रधाने न सम्भवति , कथं प्रधानं भूतयोनिः प्रतिज्ञायत इति ; अत्रोच्यते — ‘ यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ’ ‘ यत्तदद्रेश्यम् ’ इत्यक्षरशब्देनादृश्यत्वादिगुणकं भूतयोनिं श्रावयित्वा , पुनरन्ते श्रावयिष्यति — ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । २) इति । तत्र यः परोऽक्षराच्छ्रुतः , स सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित्सम्भविष्यति । प्रधानमेव त्वक्षरशब्दनिर्दिष्टं भूतयोनिः । यदा तु योनिशब्दो निमित्तवाची , तदा शारीरोऽपि भूतयोनिः स्यात् , धर्माधर्माभ्यां भूतजातस्योपार्जनादिति । एवं प्राप्ते अभिधीयते —
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ atha parā yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’(mu. u. 1 | 1 | 5) , ‘ yatta da dreśyamagrāhyamagotramavarṇamacakṣuḥśrotraṃ tadapāṇipādam , nityaṃ vibhuṃ sarvagataṃ susūkṣmaṃ tadavyayaṃ yadbhūtayoniṃ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 6) iti śrūyate | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimayamadṛśyatvādiguṇako bhūtayoniḥ pradhānaṃ syāt , uta śārīraḥ , āhosvitparameśvara iti | tatra pradhānamacetanaṃ bhūtayoniriti yuktam , acetanānāmeva tasya dṛṣṭāntatvenopādānāt | ‘ yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivyāmoṣadhayaḥ sambhavanti | yathā sataḥ puruṣātkeśalomāni tathākṣarātsambhavatīha viśvam ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 7) iti | nanūrṇanābhiḥ puruṣaśca cetanāviha dṛṣṭāntatvenopāttau ; neti brūmaḥ | na hi kevalasya cetanasya tatra sūtrayonitvaṃ keśalomayonitvaṃ cāsti | cetanādhiṣṭhitaṃ hyacetanamūrṇanābhiśarīraṃ sūtrasya yoniḥ , puruṣaśarīraṃ ca keśalomnāmiti prasiddham | api ca pūrvatrādṛṣṭatvādyabhilāpasambhave'pi draṣṭṛtvādyabhilāpāsambhavānna pradhānamabhyupagatam | iha tvadṛśyatvādayo dharmāḥ pradhāne sambhavanti | na cātra virudhyamāno dharmaḥ kaścidabhilapyate | nanu ‘ yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavit ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 9) ityayaṃ vākyaśeṣo'cetane pradhāne na sambhavati , kathaṃ pradhānaṃ bhūtayoniḥ pratijñāyata iti ; atrocyate — ‘ yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’ ‘ yattadadreśyam ’ ityakṣaraśabdenādṛśyatvādiguṇakaṃ bhūtayoniṃ śrāvayitvā , punarante śrāvayiṣyati — ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 2) iti | tatra yaḥ paro'kṣarācchrutaḥ , sa sarvajñaḥ sarvavitsambhaviṣyati | pradhānameva tvakṣaraśabdanirdiṣṭaṃ bhūtayoniḥ | yadā tu yoniśabdo nimittavācī , tadā śārīro'pi bhūtayoniḥ syāt , dharmādharmābhyāṃ bhūtajātasyopārjanāditi | evaṃ prāpte abhidhīyate —
records.transliteration: ‘ atha parā yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’(mu. u. 1 | 1 | 5) , ‘ yatta da dreśyamagrāhyamagotramavarṇamacakṣuḥśrotraṃ tadapāṇipādam , nityaṃ vibhuṃ sarvagataṃ susūkṣmaṃ tadavyayaṃ yadbhūtayoniṃ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 6) iti śrūyate | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimayamadṛśyatvādiguṇako bhūtayoniḥ pradhānaṃ syāt , uta śārīraḥ , āhosvitparameśvara iti | tatra pradhānamacetanaṃ bhūtayoniriti yuktam , acetanānāmeva tasya dṛṣṭāntatvenopādānāt | ‘ yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivyāmoṣadhayaḥ sambhavanti | yathā sataḥ puruṣātkeśalomāni tathākṣarātsambhavatīha viśvam ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 7) iti | nanūrṇanābhiḥ puruṣaśca cetanāviha dṛṣṭāntatvenopāttau ; neti brūmaḥ | na hi kevalasya cetanasya tatra sūtrayonitvaṃ keśalomayonitvaṃ cāsti | cetanādhiṣṭhitaṃ hyacetanamūrṇanābhiśarīraṃ sūtrasya yoniḥ , puruṣaśarīraṃ ca keśalomnāmiti prasiddham | api ca pūrvatrādṛṣṭatvādyabhilāpasambhave'pi draṣṭṛtvādyabhilāpāsambhavānna pradhānamabhyupagatam | iha tvadṛśyatvādayo dharmāḥ pradhāne sambhavanti | na cātra virudhyamāno dharmaḥ kaścidabhilapyate | nanu ‘ yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavit ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 9) ityayaṃ vākyaśeṣo'cetane pradhāne na sambhavati , kathaṃ pradhānaṃ bhūtayoniḥ pratijñāyata iti ; atrocyate — ‘ yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’ ‘ yattadadreśyam ’ ityakṣaraśabdenādṛśyatvādiguṇakaṃ bhūtayoniṃ śrāvayitvā , punarante śrāvayiṣyati — ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 2) iti | tatra yaḥ paro'kṣarācchrutaḥ , sa sarvajñaḥ sarvavitsambhaviṣyati | pradhānameva tvakṣaraśabdanirdiṣṭaṃ bhūtayoniḥ | yadā tu yoniśabdo nimittavācī , tadā śārīro'pi bhūtayoniḥ syāt , dharmādharmābhyāṃ bhūtajātasyopārjanāditi | evaṃ prāpte abhidhīyate —
records.literal_translation: Scripture says: 'Now the higher, by which that imperishable is known,' and 'that which is unseen, ungraspable, without lineage, without color, without eye or ear, without hands or feet, eternal, all-pervading, omnipresent, extremely subtle, undecaying, the source of beings, seen by the wise.' Here doubt arises: is this source of beings with invisibility and related qualities the Pradhana, or the embodied self, or the Supreme Lord? One may hold Pradhana, though non-conscious, to be source of beings, since non-conscious examples are cited: 'as the spider sends forth and withdraws,' 'as plants arise from earth,' 'as hairs from a living person, so the universe arises from the imperishable.' If objected that spider and person are conscious examples, reply: not so, for mere consciousness there is not sourcehood of thread or hair; rather non-conscious body, under conscious presiding, is the source. Also, earlier Pradhana was refused because seerhood predicates were impossible despite invisibility predicates; here only invisibility-type predicates appear and no conflicting predicate is yet declared. If one objects that the continuation 'who is all-knowing, all-wise' cannot fit inert Pradhana, and so Pradhana cannot be promised as source, reply: after first making heard through 'by which that imperishable is known' and 'that unseen...' the source-of-beings marked by invisibility and called imperishable, the text later teaches, 'higher than the imperishable is the highest.' The one heard as beyond the imperishable may then be all-knowing. Thus the source-of-beings denoted by imperishable would be Pradhana. And if 'source' is taken as efficient-cause term, then embodied self too could be source, by producing embodied existence through dharma and adharma. In this obtained situation, it is said -
records.technical_translation: Purvapaksha opening for 1.2.21 proposes Pradhana (or secondarily jiva) as bhuta-yoni from Mundaka akshara/adrsyatva descriptors, attempting to defer sarvajnatva to a later 'para' referent.

records.source_text: योऽयमदृश्यत्वादिगुणको भूतयोनिः , स परमेश्वर एव स्यात् , नान्य इति । कथमेतदवगम्यते ? धर्मोक्तेः । परमेश्वरस्य हि धर्म इहोच्यमानो दृश्यते — ‘ यः सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित् ’ इति । न हि प्रधानस्याचेतनस्य शारीरस्य वोपाधिपरिच्छिन्नदृष्टेः सर्वज्ञत्वं सर्ववित्त्वं वा सम्भवति । न न्व क्षरशब्दनिर्दिष्टाद्भूतयोनेः परस्यैव तत्सर्वज्ञत्वं सर्ववित्त्वं च , न भूतयोनिविषयमित्युक्तम् ; अत्रोच्यते — नैवं सम्भवति ; यत्कारणम् ‘ अक्षरात्सम्भवतीह विश्वम् ’ इति प्रकृतं भूतयोनिमिह जायमानप्रकृतित्वेन निर्दिश्य , अनन्तरमपि जायमानप्रकृतित्वेनैव सर्वज्ञं निर्दिशति — ‘ यः सर्वज्ञः सर्वविद्यस्य ज्ञानमयं तपः । तस्मादेतद्ब्रह्म नाम रूपमन्नं च जायते ’ इति । तस्मान्निर्देशसाम्येन प्रत्यभिज्ञायमानत्वात्प्रकृतस्यैवाक्षरस्य भूतयोनेः सर्वज्ञत्वं सर्ववित्त्वं च धर्म उच्यत इति गम्यते । ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ इत्यत्रापि न प्रकृताद्भूतयोनेरक्षरात्परः कश्चिदभिधीयते । कथमेतदवगम्यते ? ‘ ये ना क्षरं पुरुषं वेद सत्यं प्रोवाच तां तत्त्वतो ब्रह्मविद्याम् ’ (मु. उ. १ । २ । १३) इति प्रकृतस्यैवाक्षरस्य भूतयोनेरदृश्यत्वादिगुणकस्य वक्तव्यत्वेन प्रतिज्ञातत्वात् । कथं तर्हि ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ इति व्यपदिश्यत इति ? उत्तरसूत्रे तद्वक्ष्यामः । अपि चा त्र द्वे विद्ये वेदितव्ये उक्ते — ‘ परा चैवापरा च ’ इति । तत्रापरामृग्वेदादिलक्षणां विद्यामुक्त्वा ब्रवीति ‘ अथ परा यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ’ इत्यादि । तत्र परस्या विद्याया विषयत्वेनाक्षरं श्रुतम् । यदि पुनः परमेश्वरादन्यददृश्यत्वादिगुणकमक्षरं परिकल्प्येत , नेयं परा विद्या स्यात् । परापरविभागो ह्य यं विद्ययोः अभ्युदयनिःश्रेयसफलतया परिकल्प्यते । न च प्रधानविद्या निःश्रेयसफला केनचिदभ्युपगम्यते । तिस्रश्च विद्याः प्रतिज्ञायेरन् , त्वत्पक्षेऽक्षराद्भूतयोनेः परस्य परमात्मनः प्रतिपाद्यमानत्वात् । द्वे एव तु विद्ये वेदितव्ये इह निर्दिष्टे । ‘ कस्मि न्नु भगवो विज्ञाते सर्वमिदं विज्ञातं भवति ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ३) इति चैकविज्ञानेन सर्वविज्ञानापेक्षणं सर्वात्मके ब्रह्मणि विवक्ष्यमाणेऽवकल्पते , नाचेतनमात्रैकायतने प्रधाने , भोग्यव्यतिरिक्ते वा भोक्तरि । अपि च ‘ स ब्रह्मविद्यां सर्वविद्याप्रतिष्ठामथर्वाय ज्येष्ठपुत्राय प्राह ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । १) इति ब्रह्मविद्यां प्राधान्येनोपक्रम्य परापरविभागेन परां विद्यामक्षराधिगमनीं दर्शयन् तस्या ब्रह्मविद्यात्वं दर्शयति । सा च ब्रह्मविद्यासमाख्या तदधिगम्यस्य अक्षरस्याब्रह्मत्वे बाधिता स्यात् । अपरा ऋग्वेदादिलक्षणा कर्मविद्या ब्रह्मविद्योपक्रमे उपन्यस्यते ब्रह्मविद्याप्रशंसायै — ‘ प्लवा ह्येते अदृढा यज्ञरूपा अष्टादशोक्तमवरं येषु कर्म । एतच्छ्रेयो येऽभिनन्दन्ति मूढा जरामृत्युं ते पुनरेवापि यन्ति ’ (मु. उ. १ । २ । ७) इत्येवमादिनिन्दावचनात् । निन्दित्वा चापरां विद्यां ततो विरक्तस्य परविद्याधिकारं दर्शयति — ‘ परीक्ष्य लोकान्कर्मचितान्ब्राह्मणो निर्वेदमायान्नास्त्यकृतः कृतेन । तद्विज्ञानार्थं स गुरुमेवाभिगच्छेत्समित्पाणिः श्रोत्रियं ब्रह्मनिष्ठम् ’ (मु. उ. १ । २ । १२) इति । यत्तूक्तम् — अचेतनानां पृथिव्यादीनां दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपादानाद्दार्ष्टान्तिकेनाप्यचेतनेनैव भूतयोनिना भवितव्यमिति , तदयुक्तम् ; न हि दृष्टान्तदार्ष्टान्तिकयोरत्यन्तसाम्येन भवितव्यमिति नियमोऽस्ति । अपि च स्थूलाः पृथिव्यादयो दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपात्ता इति न स्थूल एव दार्ष्टान्तिको भूतयोनिरभ्युपगम्यते । तस्माददृश्यत्वादिगुणको भूतयोनिः परमेश्वर एव ॥ २१ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: yo'yamadṛśyatvādiguṇako bhūtayoniḥ , sa parameśvara eva syāt , nānya iti | kathametadavagamyate ? dharmokteḥ | parameśvarasya hi dharma ihocyamāno dṛśyate — ‘ yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavit ’ iti | na hi pradhānasyācetanasya śārīrasya vopādhiparicchinnadṛṣṭeḥ sarvajñatvaṃ sarvavittvaṃ vā sambhavati | na nva kṣaraśabdanirdiṣṭādbhūtayoneḥ parasyaiva tatsarvajñatvaṃ sarvavittvaṃ ca , na bhūtayoniviṣayamityuktam ; atrocyate — naivaṃ sambhavati ; yatkāraṇam ‘ akṣarātsambhavatīha viśvam ’ iti prakṛtaṃ bhūtayonimiha jāyamānaprakṛtitvena nirdiśya , anantaramapi jāyamānaprakṛtitvenaiva sarvajñaṃ nirdiśati — ‘ yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavidyasya jñānamayaṃ tapaḥ | tasmādetadbrahma nāma rūpamannaṃ ca jāyate ’ iti | tasmānnirdeśasāmyena pratyabhijñāyamānatvātprakṛtasyaivākṣarasya bhūtayoneḥ sarvajñatvaṃ sarvavittvaṃ ca dharma ucyata iti gamyate | ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ ityatrāpi na prakṛtādbhūtayonerakṣarātparaḥ kaścidabhidhīyate | kathametadavagamyate ? ‘ ye nā kṣaraṃ puruṣaṃ veda satyaṃ provāca tāṃ tattvato brahmavidyām ’ (mu. u. 1 | 2 | 13) iti prakṛtasyaivākṣarasya bhūtayoneradṛśyatvādiguṇakasya vaktavyatvena pratijñātatvāt | kathaṃ tarhi ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ iti vyapadiśyata iti ? uttarasūtre tadvakṣyāmaḥ | api cā tra dve vidye veditavye ukte — ‘ parā caivāparā ca ’ iti | tatrāparāmṛgvedādilakṣaṇāṃ vidyāmuktvā bravīti ‘ atha parā yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’ ityādi | tatra parasyā vidyāyā viṣayatvenākṣaraṃ śrutam | yadi punaḥ parameśvarādanyadadṛśyatvādiguṇakamakṣaraṃ parikalpyeta , neyaṃ parā vidyā syāt | parāparavibhāgo hya yaṃ vidyayoḥ abhyudayaniḥśreyasaphalatayā parikalpyate | na ca pradhānavidyā niḥśreyasaphalā kenacidabhyupagamyate | tisraśca vidyāḥ pratijñāyeran , tvatpakṣe'kṣarādbhūtayoneḥ parasya paramātmanaḥ pratipādyamānatvāt | dve eva tu vidye veditavye iha nirdiṣṭe | ‘ kasmi nnu bhagavo vijñāte sarvamidaṃ vijñātaṃ bhavati ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 3) iti caikavijñānena sarvavijñānāpekṣaṇaṃ sarvātmake brahmaṇi vivakṣyamāṇe'vakalpate , nācetanamātraikāyatane pradhāne , bhogyavyatirikte vā bhoktari | api ca ‘ sa brahmavidyāṃ sarvavidyāpratiṣṭhāmatharvāya jyeṣṭhaputrāya prāha ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 1) iti brahmavidyāṃ prādhānyenopakramya parāparavibhāgena parāṃ vidyāmakṣarādhigamanīṃ darśayan tasyā brahmavidyātvaṃ darśayati | sā ca brahmavidyāsamākhyā tadadhigamyasya akṣarasyābrahmatve bādhitā syāt | aparā ṛgvedādilakṣaṇā karmavidyā brahmavidyopakrame upanyasyate brahmavidyāpraśaṃsāyai — ‘ plavā hyete adṛḍhā yajñarūpā aṣṭādaśoktamavaraṃ yeṣu karma | etacchreyo ye'bhinandanti mūḍhā jarāmṛtyuṃ te punarevāpi yanti ’ (mu. u. 1 | 2 | 7) ityevamādinindāvacanāt | ninditvā cāparāṃ vidyāṃ tato viraktasya paravidyādhikāraṃ darśayati — ‘ parīkṣya lokānkarmacitānbrāhmaṇo nirvedamāyānnāstyakṛtaḥ kṛtena | tadvijñānārthaṃ sa gurumevābhigacchetsamitpāṇiḥ śrotriyaṃ brahmaniṣṭham ’ (mu. u. 1 | 2 | 12) iti | yattūktam — acetanānāṃ pṛthivyādīnāṃ dṛṣṭāntatvenopādānāddārṣṭāntikenāpyacetanenaiva bhūtayoninā bhavitavyamiti , tadayuktam ; na hi dṛṣṭāntadārṣṭāntikayoratyantasāmyena bhavitavyamiti niyamo'sti | api ca sthūlāḥ pṛthivyādayo dṛṣṭāntatvenopāttā iti na sthūla eva dārṣṭāntiko bhūtayonirabhyupagamyate | tasmādadṛśyatvādiguṇako bhūtayoniḥ parameśvara eva || 21 ||

records.literal_translation: This source of beings characterized by invisibility and the like is the Supreme Lord alone, not another. How is this known? Because the declared attribute belongs to Him. For the attribute stated here - 'who is all-knowing, all-wise' - is seen to belong to the Supreme. Neither inert Pradhana nor the embodied self, whose cognition is adjunct-delimited, can possess omniscience and all-comprehension. If one says that omniscience belongs only to what is beyond the imperishable source, not to the source itself, reply: this is untenable. For after introducing the source as that from which the universe arises, the text immediately describes the all-knowing in the same productive-causal role: 'whose tapas is knowledge; from Him arise Brahman, name, form, and food.' By sameness of indication and recognition of the same referent, omniscience and all-comprehension are taught of that very previously introduced imperishable source. Even in 'higher than the imperishable is the highest,' no other entity beyond the introduced imperishable source is intended. How known? Because the text had pledged to teach that very imperishable source with invisibility and related traits: 'the one who knows that imperishable person truly taught that Brahma-vidya.' Then why is it spoken as 'higher than the imperishable'? We will explain this in the next sutra. Also, two knowledges are declared: lower and higher. After stating lower knowledge as Rgveda etc., it says, 'now the higher by which that imperishable is known'; thus imperishable is heard as the object of higher knowledge. If one imagined another imperishable with such traits, distinct from the Supreme, this would not be higher knowledge. The lower-higher split is construed as worldly prosperity versus liberation; no one accepts Pradhana-knowledge as liberating. On your view three knowledges would be required, since a Supreme beyond the imperishable source would still need teaching; but only two are taught. And the request 'what, when known, makes all this known?' is coherent when Brahman, the self of all, is intended - not with inert Pradhana or a non-total enjoyer. Further, the text begins by foregrounding Brahma-vidya, then via lower/higher division presents higher knowledge as leading to the imperishable, showing it as Brahma-vidya; that title would collapse if the knowable imperishable were non-Brahman. Lower knowledge (Rgveda etc.) is introduced within a Brahma-vidya opening to praise Brahma-vidya, as shown by later censure of ritual rafts and return to old age and death. Having criticized lower knowledge, it grants eligibility for higher knowledge to the dispassionate seeker who approaches a teacher. As for the claim that because examples like earth are non-conscious, the illustrated source must be non-conscious: not valid, since strict identity between example and exemplified is not required. And because gross entities like earth are cited as examples, one does not infer the source must be gross. Therefore the source of beings marked by invisibility and related traits is the Supreme Lord alone. (21)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.21 identifies Mundaka bhuta-yoni as Brahman by dharma-ukti (sarvajna/sarvavit), referential continuity across cause-statements, two-vidya architecture, and liberation-oriented epistemic framing; Pradhana/jiva readings collapse textual economy.

records.source_text: अदृश्यत्वादिगुणको धर्मोक्तेः ॥ २१ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: adṛśyatvādiguṇako dharmokteḥ || 21 ||
records.transliteration: adṛśyatvādiguṇako dharmokteḥ || 21 ||
records.literal_translation: Characterized by invisibility and the like, because of statement of the attribute. (21)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.21: the adrsyatva-qualified bhuta-yoni is Brahman, established by explicit statement of Brahmanic attributes.

records.source_text: इतश्च परमेश्वर एव भूतयोनिः , नेतरौ — शारीरः प्रधानं वा । कस्मात् ? विशेषणभेदव्यपदेशाभ्याम् । विशिनष्टि हि प्रकृतं भूतयोनिं शारीराद्विलक्षणत्वेन — ‘ दिव्यो ह्यमूर्तः पुरुषः सबाह्याभ्यन्तरो ह्यजः । अप्राणो ह्यमनाः शुभ्रः ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । २) इति । न ह्येतद्दिव्यत्वादिविशेषणम् अविद्याप्रत्युपस्थापितनामरूपपरिच्छेदाभिमानिनः तद्धर्मान्स्वात्मनि कल्पयतः शारीरस्योपपद्यते । तस्मात्साक्षादौपनिषदः पुरुष इहोच्यते । तथा प्रधानादपि प्रकृतं भूतयोनिं भेदेन व्यपदिशति — ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ इति । अक्षरमव्याकृतं नामरूपबीजशक्तिरूपं भूतसूक्ष्ममीश्वराश्रयं तस्यैवोपाधिभूतम् , सर्वस्माद्विकारात्परो यो ऽविकारः , तस्मात्परतः परः इति भेदेन व्यपदिशन् परमात्मानमिह विवक्षितं दर्शयति । नात्र प्रधानं नाम किञ्चित्स्वतन्त्रं तत्त्वमभ्युपगम्य , तस्माद्भेदव्यपदेश उच्यते । किं तर्हि ? यदि प्रधानमपि कल्प्यमानं श्रुत्यविरोधेनाव्याकृतादिशब्दवाच्यं भूतसूक्ष्मं परिकल्प्येत , परिकल्प्यताम् । तस्माद्भेदव्यपदेशात् परमेश्वरो भूतयोनिरित्येतदिह प्रतिपाद्यते ॥ २२ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca parameśvara eva bhūtayoniḥ , netarau — śārīraḥ pradhānaṃ vā | kasmāt ? viśeṣaṇabhedavyapadeśābhyām | viśinaṣṭi hi prakṛtaṃ bhūtayoniṃ śārīrādvilakṣaṇatvena — ‘ divyo hyamūrtaḥ puruṣaḥ sabāhyābhyantaro hyajaḥ | aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhraḥ ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 2) iti | na hyetaddivyatvādiviśeṣaṇam avidyāpratyupasthāpitanāmarūpaparicchedābhimāninaḥ taddharmānsvātmani kalpayataḥ śārīrasyopapadyate | tasmātsākṣādaupaniṣadaḥ puruṣa ihocyate | tathā pradhānādapi prakṛtaṃ bhūtayoniṃ bhedena vyapadiśati — ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ iti | akṣaramavyākṛtaṃ nāmarūpabījaśaktirūpaṃ bhūtasūkṣmamīśvarāśrayaṃ tasyaivopādhibhūtam , sarvasmādvikārātparo yo 'vikāraḥ , tasmātparataḥ paraḥ iti bhedena vyapadiśan paramātmānamiha vivakṣitaṃ darśayati | nātra pradhānaṃ nāma kiñcitsvatantraṃ tattvamabhyupagamya , tasmādbhedavyapadeśa ucyate | kiṃ tarhi ? yadi pradhānamapi kalpyamānaṃ śrutyavirodhenāvyākṛtādiśabdavācyaṃ bhūtasūkṣmaṃ parikalpyeta , parikalpyatām | tasmādbhedavyapadeśāt parameśvaro bhūtayonirityetadiha pratipādyate || 22 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca parameśvara eva bhūtayoniḥ , netarau — śārīraḥ pradhānaṃ vā | kasmāt ? viśeṣaṇabhedavyapadeśābhyām | viśinaṣṭi hi prakṛtaṃ bhūtayoniṃ śārīrādvilakṣaṇatvena — ‘ divyo hyamūrtaḥ puruṣaḥ sabāhyābhyantaro hyajaḥ | aprāṇo hyamanāḥ śubhraḥ ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 2) iti | na hyetaddivyatvādiviśeṣaṇam avidyāpratyupasthāpitanāmarūpaparicchedābhimāninaḥ taddharmānsvātmani kalpayataḥ śārīrasyopapadyate | tasmātsākṣādaupaniṣadaḥ puruṣa ihocyate | tathā pradhānādapi prakṛtaṃ bhūtayoniṃ bhedena vyapadiśati — ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ iti | akṣaramavyākṛtaṃ nāmarūpabījaśaktirūpaṃ bhūtasūkṣmamīśvarāśrayaṃ tasyaivopādhibhūtam , sarvasmādvikārātparo yo 'vikāraḥ , tasmātparataḥ paraḥ iti bhedena vyapadiśan paramātmānamiha vivakṣitaṃ darśayati | nātra pradhānaṃ nāma kiñcitsvatantraṃ tattvamabhyupagamya , tasmādbhedavyapadeśa ucyate | kiṃ tarhi ? yadi pradhānamapi kalpyamānaṃ śrutyavirodhenāvyākṛtādiśabdavācyaṃ bhūtasūkṣmaṃ parikalpyeta , parikalpyatām | tasmādbhedavyapadeśāt parameśvaro bhūtayonirityetadiha pratipādyate || 22 ||
records.literal_translation: For this reason too, only the Supreme Lord is the source of beings, not the other two - embodied self or Pradhana. Why? From qualifying attributes and declarations of distinction. The text qualifies the discussed source of beings as distinct from embodied self: 'for that divine, formless Person, both within and without, unborn, without breath, without mind, pure.' Such divine qualifiers do not fit the embodied self that, through ignorance, superimposes body-name-form limits and their properties upon itself. Therefore the directly Upanishadic Person is taught here. Likewise it declares the discussed source distinct even from Pradhana: 'higher than the imperishable is the highest.' 'Imperishable' here means the unmanifest seed-power of name and form, subtle being-element, dependent on Ishvara and serving as His adjunct. By distinguishing the Supreme Self as beyond that changeless principle, beyond all modifications, the text shows its intent is Paramatman. No independent Sankhya Pradhana is admitted here as a separate ultimate. If one still imagines some unmanifest subtle principle denoted by terms like avyakrta without opposing sruti, let it be imagined; even then, by declaration of distinction from it, the teaching here is that the source of beings is the Supreme Lord. (22)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.22 confirms bhuta-yoni as Brahman via visesana and bheda-vyapadesha: textually marked transcendence over jiva and over akshara/avyakrta adjunct-principle.

records.source_text: विशेषणभेदव्यपदेशाभ्यां च नेतरौ ॥ २२ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: viśeṣaṇabhedavyapadeśābhyāṃ ca netarau || 22 ||
records.transliteration: viśeṣaṇabhedavyapadeśābhyāṃ ca netarau || 22 ||
records.literal_translation: And not the other two, because of qualifying attributes and declarations of distinction. (22)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.22: bhuta-yoni is not jiva or Pradhana, established by visesana and bheda-vyapadesha indicators.

records.source_text: अपि च ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ इत्यस्यानन्तरम् ‘ एतस्माज्जायते प्राणः ’ इति प्राणप्रभृतीनां पृथिवीपर्यन्तानां तत्त्वानां सर्गमुक्त्वा , तस्यैव भूतयोनेः सर्वविकारात्मकं रूपमुपन्यस्यमानं पश्यामः — ‘ अग्निर्मूर्धा चक्षुषी चन्द्रसूर्यौ दिशः श्रोत्रे वाग्विवृताश्च वेदाः । वायुः प्राणो हृदयं विश्वमस्य पद्भ्यां पृथिवी ह्येष सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । ४) इति । तच्च परमेश्वरस्यैवोचितम् , सर्वविकारकारणत्वात् । न शारीरस्य तनुमहिम्नः ; नापि प्रधानस्य अयं रूपोपन्यासः सम्भवति , सर्वभूतान्तरात्मत्वासम्भवात् । तस्मात्परमेश्वर एव भूतयोनिः , नेतराविति गम्यते । कथं पुनर्भूतयोनेरयं रूपोपन्यास इति गम्यते ? प्रकरणात् , ‘ एषः ’ इति च प्रकृतानुकर्षणात् । भूतयोनिं हि प्रकृत्य ‘ एतस्माज्जायते प्राणः ’ ‘ एष सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा ’ इति वचनं भूतयोनिविषयमेव भवति । यथा उपाध्यायं प्रकृत्य ‘ एतस्मादधीष्व , एष वेदवेदाङ्गपारगः ’ इति वचनमुपाध्यायविषयं भवति , तद्वत् । कथं पुनरदृश्यत्वादिगुणकस्य भूतयोनेर्विग्रहवद्रूपं सम्भवति ? सर्वात्मत्वविवक्षयेदमुच्यते , न तु विग्रहवत्त्वविवक्षया इत्यदोषः , ‘ अहमन्नम् ’ ‘ अहमन्नादः ’ (तै. उ. ३ । १० । ६) इत्यादिवत् ॥
records.transliteration_iast: api ca ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ ityasyānantaram ‘ etasmājjāyate prāṇaḥ ’ iti prāṇaprabhṛtīnāṃ pṛthivīparyantānāṃ tattvānāṃ sargamuktvā , tasyaiva bhūtayoneḥ sarvavikārātmakaṃ rūpamupanyasyamānaṃ paśyāmaḥ — ‘ agnirmūrdhā cakṣuṣī candrasūryau diśaḥ śrotre vāgvivṛtāśca vedāḥ | vāyuḥ prāṇo hṛdayaṃ viśvamasya padbhyāṃ pṛthivī hyeṣa sarvabhūtāntarātmā ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 4) iti | tacca parameśvarasyaivocitam , sarvavikārakāraṇatvāt | na śārīrasya tanumahimnaḥ ; nāpi pradhānasya ayaṃ rūpopanyāsaḥ sambhavati , sarvabhūtāntarātmatvāsambhavāt | tasmātparameśvara eva bhūtayoniḥ , netarāviti gamyate | kathaṃ punarbhūtayonerayaṃ rūpopanyāsa iti gamyate ? prakaraṇāt , ‘ eṣaḥ ’ iti ca prakṛtānukarṣaṇāt | bhūtayoniṃ hi prakṛtya ‘ etasmājjāyate prāṇaḥ ’ ‘ eṣa sarvabhūtāntarātmā ’ iti vacanaṃ bhūtayoniviṣayameva bhavati | yathā upādhyāyaṃ prakṛtya ‘ etasmādadhīṣva , eṣa vedavedāṅgapāragaḥ ’ iti vacanamupādhyāyaviṣayaṃ bhavati , tadvat | kathaṃ punaradṛśyatvādiguṇakasya bhūtayonervigrahavadrūpaṃ sambhavati ? sarvātmatvavivakṣayedamucyate , na tu vigrahavattvavivakṣayā ityadoṣaḥ , ‘ ahamannam ’ ‘ ahamannādaḥ ’ (tai. u. 3 | 10 | 6) ityādivat ||

records.literal_translation: Further, after 'higher than the imperishable is the highest,' and after stating creation of principles from prana down to earth ('from this are born prana...'), we see the text presenting a form of that very source of beings as the totality of modifications: 'fire is His head, moon and sun His eyes, directions His ears, Vedas His speech, wind His breath, the universe His heart, earth from His feet; this indeed is the inner self of all beings.' This is proper only to the Supreme Lord as cause of all transformations. Not to embodied self, whose body-glory is limited; nor to Pradhana, since inner-selfhood of all beings cannot apply. Therefore only the Supreme Lord is source of beings. How is this form-presentation known to concern source-of-beings? From context and from pulling forward the prior referent by 'this.' Having introduced source-of-beings, statements like 'from this is born prana' and 'this is the inner self of all beings' concern that same topic, just as after introducing a teacher one says, 'study from this one; this is master of Veda and Vedanga.' If asked how an invisibility-qualified source can have form-like description, no fault: the intention is to express all-selfhood, not corporeality, like 'I am food, I am eater of food.'
records.technical_translation: Bhashya supporting 1.2.23 reads cosmic-body imagery as rupa-upanyasa of the previously introduced bhuta-yoni, secured by prakarana continuity and pronoun anukarsana; this suits Brahman-causality alone.

records.source_text: अन्ये पुन र्मन्यन्ते — नायं भूतयोनेः रूपोपन्यासः , जायमानत्वेनोपन्यासात् । ‘ एतस्माज्जायते प्राणो मनः सर्वेन्द्रियाणि च । खं वायुर्ज्योतिरापः पृथिवी विश्वस्य धारिणी ’ इति हि पूर्वत्र प्राणादि पृथिव्यन्तं तत्त्वजातं जायमानत्वेन निरदिक्षत् । उत्तरत्रापि च ‘ तस्मादग्निः समिधो यस्य सूर्यः ’ इत्येवमादि ‘ अतश्च सर्वा ओषधयो रसश्च ’ इत्येवमन्तं जायमानत्वेनैव निर्देक्ष्यति । इहैव कथमकस्मादन्तराले भूतयोनेः रूपमुपन्यसेत् ? सर्वात्मत्वमपि सृष्टिं परिसमाप्योपदेक्ष्यति — ‘ पुरुष एवेदं विश्वं कर्म ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । १०) इत्यादिना । श्रुति स्मृत्योश्च त्रैलोक्यशरीरस्य प्रजापतेर्जन्मादि निर्दिश्यमानमुपलभामहे — ‘ हिरण्यगर्भः समवर्तताग्रे भूतस्य जातः पतिरेक आसीत् । स दाधार पृथिवीं द्यामुतेमां कस्मै देवाय हविषा विधेम ’ (ऋ. सं. १० । १२१ । १) इति ; समवर्ततेति अजायतेत्यर्थः — तथा , ‘ स वै शरीरी प्रथमः स वै पुरुष उच्यते । आदिकर्ता स भूतानां ब्रह्माग्रे समवर्तत ’ इति च । विकार पुरुषस्यापि सर्वभूतान्तरात्मत्वं सम्भवति , प्राणात्मना सर्वभूतानामध्यात्ममवस्थानात् । अस्मिन्पक्षे ‘ पुरुष एवेदं विश्वं कर्म ’ इत्यादिसर्वरूपोपन्यासः परमेश्वरप्रतिपत्तिहेतुरिति व्याख्येयम् ॥ २३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: anye puna rmanyante — nāyaṃ bhūtayoneḥ rūpopanyāsaḥ , jāyamānatvenopanyāsāt | ‘ etasmājjāyate prāṇo manaḥ sarvendriyāṇi ca | khaṃ vāyurjyotirāpaḥ pṛthivī viśvasya dhāriṇī ’ iti hi pūrvatra prāṇādi pṛthivyantaṃ tattvajātaṃ jāyamānatvena niradikṣat | uttaratrāpi ca ‘ tasmādagniḥ samidho yasya sūryaḥ ’ ityevamādi ‘ ataśca sarvā oṣadhayo rasaśca ’ ityevamantaṃ jāyamānatvenaiva nirdekṣyati | ihaiva kathamakasmādantarāle bhūtayoneḥ rūpamupanyaset ? sarvātmatvamapi sṛṣṭiṃ parisamāpyopadekṣyati — ‘ puruṣa evedaṃ viśvaṃ karma ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 10) ityādinā | śruti smṛtyośca trailokyaśarīrasya prajāpaterjanmādi nirdiśyamānamupalabhāmahe — ‘ hiraṇyagarbhaḥ samavartatāgre bhūtasya jātaḥ patireka āsīt | sa dādhāra pṛthivīṃ dyāmutemāṃ kasmai devāya haviṣā vidhema ’ (ṛ. saṃ. 10 | 121 | 1) iti ; samavartateti ajāyatetyarthaḥ — tathā , ‘ sa vai śarīrī prathamaḥ sa vai puruṣa ucyate | ādikartā sa bhūtānāṃ brahmāgre samavartata ’ iti ca | vikāra puruṣasyāpi sarvabhūtāntarātmatvaṃ sambhavati , prāṇātmanā sarvabhūtānāmadhyātmamavasthānāt | asminpakṣe ‘ puruṣa evedaṃ viśvaṃ karma ’ ityādisarvarūpopanyāsaḥ parameśvarapratipattiheturiti vyākhyeyam || 23 ||

records.literal_translation: Others, however, think: this is not a form-presentation of the source of beings, because the sequence presents things as born. Earlier it specified prana through earth as generated; later too it will continue generation language ('from Him fire...') through herbs and essence. Why suddenly insert in the middle a form-description of the source? Even all-selfhood will be taught later after finishing creation ('the Person alone is this all, action...'). Sruti and smrti also describe birth etc. of Prajapati with the three-world body ('Hiranyagarbha arose at first...'; 'that embodied first one is called Purusha...'). Even the effected cosmic person may be inner self of all beings by abiding as life-principle in all. On this view, passages such as 'the Person alone is this all' should be interpreted instead as establishing the Supreme Lord.
records.technical_translation: Alternative reading before 1.2.23 resists immediate rupa-upanyasa by treating the section as uninterrupted cosmogenesis and reallocating universal-form predicates to Hiranyagarbha/vikara-purusha.

records.source_text: कुतश्च परमेश्वरो भूतयोनिः ? —
records.transliteration_iast: kutaśca parameśvaro bhūtayoniḥ ? —
records.transliteration: kutaśca parameśvaro bhūtayoniḥ ? —
records.literal_translation: And from what is the source of beings known to be the Supreme Lord? -
records.technical_translation: Transition to Sutra 1.2.23 posing the inferential ground for Brahman-identification of bhuta-yoni.

records.source_text: रूपोपन्यासाच्च ॥ २३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: rūpopanyāsācca || 23 ||
records.transliteration: rūpopanyāsācca || 23 ||
records.literal_translation: And because of presentation of form. (23)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.23: bhuta-yoni is Brahman on the strength of rupa-upanyasa (cosmic-form presentation).

records.source_text: ‘ को न आत्मा किं ब्रह्म ’ (छा. उ. ५ । ११ । १) इति ‘ आत्मान मे वेमं वैश्वानरं सम्प्रत्यध्येषि तमेव नो ब्रूहि ’ (छा. उ. ५ । ११ । ६) इति चोपक्रम्य द्युसूर्यवाय्वाकाशवारिपृथिवीनां सुतेजस्त्वादिगुणयोगमेकैकोपासननिन्दया च वैश्वानरं प्रत्येषां मूर्धादिभावमुपदिश्याम्नायते — ‘ यस्त्वेत मे वं प्रादेशमात्रमभिविमानमात्मानं वैश्वानरमुपास्ते , स सर्वेषु लोकेषु सर्वेषु भूतेषु सर्वेष्वात्मस्वन्नमत्ति ;’ (छा. उ. ५ । १८ । १) , ‘ तस्य ह वा एतस्यात्मनो वैश्वानरस्य मूर्धैव सुतेजाश्चक्षुर्विश्वरूपः प्राणः पृथग्वर्त्मात्मा सन्देहो बहुलो वस्तिरेव रयिः पृथिव्येव पादा वुर एव वेदिर्लोमानि बर्हिर्हृदयं गार्हपत्यो मनोऽन्वाहार्यपचन आस्यमाहवनीयः ’ (छा. उ. ५ । १८ । २) इत्यादि । तत्र संशयः — किं वैश्वानरशब्देन जाठरोऽग्निरुपदिश्यते , उत भूताग्निः , अथ तदभिमानिनी देवता , अथवा शारीरः , आहोस्वित्परमेश्वरः इति । किं पुनरत्र संशयकारणम् ? वैश्वानर इति जाठरभूताग्निदेवतानां साधारणशब्दप्रयोगात् , आत्मेति च शारीरपरमेश्वरयोः । तत्र कस्योपादानं न्याय्यं कस्य वा हानमिति भवति संशयः । किं ताव त्प्राप्तम् ? जाठरोऽग्निरिति । कुतः ? तत्र हि विशेषेण क्वचित्प्रयोगो दृश्यते — ‘ अयमग्निर्वैश्वानरो योऽयमन्तः पुरुषे येनेदमन्नं पच्यते यदिदमद्यते ’ (बृ. उ. ५ । ९ । १) इत्यादौ । अग्निमात्रं वा स्यात् , सामान्येनापि प्रयोगदर्शनात् — ‘ विश्वस्मा अग्निं भुवनाय देवा वैश्वानरं केतुमह्नामकृण्वन् ’ (ऋ. सं. १० । ८८ । १२) इत्यादौ । अग्निशरीरा वा देवता स्यात् , तस्यामपि प्रयोगदर्शनात् — ‘ वैश्वानरस्य सुमतौ स्याम राजा हि कं भुवनानामभिश्रीः ’ (ऋ. सं. १ । ९८ । १) इत्येवमाद्यायाः श्रुतेर्देवतायामैश्वर्याद्युपेतायां सम्भवात् । अ था त्मशब्दसामानाधिकरण्यादुपक्रमे च ‘ को न आत्मा किं ब्रह्म ’ इति केवलात्मशब्दप्रयोगादात्मशब्दवशेन वैश्वानरशब्दः परिणेय इत्युच्यते , तथापि शारीर आत्मा स्यात् । तस्य भोक्तृत्वेन वैश्वानरसन्निकर्षात् , प्रादेशमात्रमिति च विशेषणस्य तस्मिन्नुपाधिपरिच्छिन्ने सम्भवात् । तस्मान्नेश्वरो वैश्वानर इत्येवं प्राप्ते ततः
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ ko na ātmā kiṃ brahma ’ (chā. u. 5 | 11 | 1) iti ‘ ātmāna me vemaṃ vaiśvānaraṃ sampratyadhyeṣi tameva no brūhi ’ (chā. u. 5 | 11 | 6) iti copakramya dyusūryavāyvākāśavāripṛthivīnāṃ sutejastvādiguṇayogamekaikopāsananindayā ca vaiśvānaraṃ pratyeṣāṃ mūrdhādibhāvamupadiśyāmnāyate — ‘ yastveta me vaṃ prādeśamātramabhivimānamātmānaṃ vaiśvānaramupāste , sa sarveṣu lokeṣu sarveṣu bhūteṣu sarveṣvātmasvannamatti ;’ (chā. u. 5 | 18 | 1) , ‘ tasya ha vā etasyātmano vaiśvānarasya mūrdhaiva sutejāścakṣurviśvarūpaḥ prāṇaḥ pṛthagvartmātmā sandeho bahulo vastireva rayiḥ pṛthivyeva pādā vura eva vedirlomāni barhirhṛdayaṃ gārhapatyo mano'nvāhāryapacana āsyamāhavanīyaḥ ’ (chā. u. 5 | 18 | 2) ityādi | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kiṃ vaiśvānaraśabdena jāṭharo'gnirupadiśyate , uta bhūtāgniḥ , atha tadabhimāninī devatā , athavā śārīraḥ , āhosvitparameśvaraḥ iti | kiṃ punaratra saṃśayakāraṇam ? vaiśvānara iti jāṭharabhūtāgnidevatānāṃ sādhāraṇaśabdaprayogāt , ātmeti ca śārīraparameśvarayoḥ | tatra kasyopādānaṃ nyāyyaṃ kasya vā hānamiti bhavati saṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāva tprāptam ? jāṭharo'gniriti | kutaḥ ? tatra hi viśeṣeṇa kvacitprayogo dṛśyate — ‘ ayamagnirvaiśvānaro yo'yamantaḥ puruṣe yenedamannaṃ pacyate yadidamadyate ’ (bṛ. u. 5 | 9 | 1) ityādau | agnimātraṃ vā syāt , sāmānyenāpi prayogadarśanāt — ‘ viśvasmā agniṃ bhuvanāya devā vaiśvānaraṃ ketumahnāmakṛṇvan ’ (ṛ. saṃ. 10 | 88 | 12) ityādau | agniśarīrā vā devatā syāt , tasyāmapi prayogadarśanāt — ‘ vaiśvānarasya sumatau syāma rājā hi kaṃ bhuvanānāmabhiśrīḥ ’ (ṛ. saṃ. 1 | 98 | 1) ityevamādyāyāḥ śruterdevatāyāmaiśvaryādyupetāyāṃ sambhavāt | a thā tmaśabdasāmānādhikaraṇyādupakrame ca ‘ ko na ātmā kiṃ brahma ’ iti kevalātmaśabdaprayogādātmaśabdavaśena vaiśvānaraśabdaḥ pariṇeya ityucyate , tathāpi śārīra ātmā syāt | tasya bhoktṛtvena vaiśvānarasannikarṣāt , prādeśamātramiti ca viśeṣaṇasya tasminnupādhiparicchinne sambhavāt | tasmānneśvaro vaiśvānara ityevaṃ prāpte tataḥ

records.literal_translation: Beginning with 'Who is our self? what is Brahman?' and 'You are now inquiring of me about this self, the Vaishvanara; tell us that very one,' scripture teaches that heaven, sun, wind, space, water, and earth become head and other limbs of Vaishvanara, while criticizing one-sided meditations on each as merely possessing qualities like bright radiance. It says: 'who worships this Vaishvanara self as of measure of a span and inwardly apprehended, he eats food in all worlds, all beings, all selves'; and again, 'of this Vaishvanara self, heaven indeed is head, sun eye, wind breath, space trunk, waters bladder, earth feet, chest altar, hairs sacred grass, heart garhapatya fire, mind anvaharyapacana, mouth ahavaniya,' etc. Doubt arises: does 'Vaishvanara' here denote digestive fire, elemental fire, the presiding deity, the embodied self, or the Supreme Lord? Why doubt? Because 'Vaishvanara' is commonly used for digestive/elemental/deity fire, while 'self' is common to embodied self and Supreme. Which should be retained and which abandoned is uncertain. Prima facie, digestive fire seems likely: texts specifically use it ('this fire within man by which food is cooked'). Or perhaps fire in general, from general usage ('the gods made Agni Vaishvanara'). Or the fire-deity, since usages support that and lordly traits may fit deity. And if one pushes by self-word apposition and opening line 'Who is our self? what is Brahman?,' still perhaps embodied self: as enjoyer it is near Vaishvanara, and the qualifier 'measure of a span' can fit an adjunct-limited self. So it seems Vaishvanara is not Ishvara; then -
records.technical_translation: Purvapaksha setup for 1.2.24 maps lexical polysemy of Vaishvanara/atman across jatharagni, bhutagni, devata, jiva, and Brahman, arguing initially for non-Ishvara readings.

records.source_text: इद मुच्यते — वैश्वानरः परमात्मा भवितुमर्हति । कुतः ? साधारणशब्दविशेषात् । साधारणशब्दयोर्विशेषः साधारणशब्दविशेषः । यद्यप्येतावुभावप्यात्मवैश्वानरशब्दौ साधारणशब्दौ — वैश्वानरशब्दस्तु त्रयस्य साधारणः , आत्मशब्दश्च द्वयस्य , तथापि विशेषो दृश्यते , येन परमेश्वरपरत्वं तयोरभ्युपगम्यते — ‘ तस्य ह वा एतस्यात्मनो वैश्वानरस्य मूर्धैव सुतेजाः ’ इत्यादिः । अत्र हि परमेश्वर एव द्युमूर्धत्वादिविशिष्टोऽवस्थान्तरगतः प्रत्यगात्मत्वेनोपन्यस्त आध्यानायेति गम्यते , कारणत्वात् । कारणस्य हि सर्वाभिः कार्यगताभिरवस्थाभिरवस्थावत्त्वात् द्युलोकाद्यवयवत्वमुपपद्यते । ‘ स सर्वेषु लोकेषु सर्वेषु भूतेषु सर्वेष्वात्मस्वन्नमत्ति ’ इति च सर्वलोकाद्याश्रयं फलं श्रूयमाणं परमकारणपरिग्रहे सम्भवति , ‘ एवं हास्य सर्वे पाप्मानः प्रदूयन्ते ’ (छा. उ. ५ । २४ । ३) इति च तद्विदः सर्वपाप्मप्रदाहश्रवणम् , ‘ को न आत्मा किं ब्रह्म ’ इति चात्मब्रह्मशब्दाभ्यामुपक्रमः ; — इत्येवमेतानि लिङ्गानि परमेश्वरमेव गमयन्ति । तस्मात्परमेश्वर एव वैश्वानरः ॥ २४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ida mucyate — vaiśvānaraḥ paramātmā bhavitumarhati | kutaḥ ? sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣāt | sādhāraṇaśabdayorviśeṣaḥ sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣaḥ | yadyapyetāvubhāvapyātmavaiśvānaraśabdau sādhāraṇaśabdau — vaiśvānaraśabdastu trayasya sādhāraṇaḥ , ātmaśabdaśca dvayasya , tathāpi viśeṣo dṛśyate , yena parameśvaraparatvaṃ tayorabhyupagamyate — ‘ tasya ha vā etasyātmano vaiśvānarasya mūrdhaiva sutejāḥ ’ ityādiḥ | atra hi parameśvara eva dyumūrdhatvādiviśiṣṭo'vasthāntaragataḥ pratyagātmatvenopanyasta ādhyānāyeti gamyate , kāraṇatvāt | kāraṇasya hi sarvābhiḥ kāryagatābhiravasthābhiravasthāvattvāt dyulokādyavayavatvamupapadyate | ‘ sa sarveṣu lokeṣu sarveṣu bhūteṣu sarveṣvātmasvannamatti ’ iti ca sarvalokādyāśrayaṃ phalaṃ śrūyamāṇaṃ paramakāraṇaparigrahe sambhavati , ‘ evaṃ hāsya sarve pāpmānaḥ pradūyante ’ (chā. u. 5 | 24 | 3) iti ca tadvidaḥ sarvapāpmapradāhaśravaṇam , ‘ ko na ātmā kiṃ brahma ’ iti cātmabrahmaśabdābhyāmupakramaḥ ; — ityevametāni liṅgāni parameśvarameva gamayanti | tasmātparameśvara eva vaiśvānaraḥ || 24 ||
records.transliteration: ida mucyate — vaiśvānaraḥ paramātmā bhavitumarhati | kutaḥ ? sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣāt | sādhāraṇaśabdayorviśeṣaḥ sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣaḥ | yadyapyetāvubhāvapyātmavaiśvānaraśabdau sādhāraṇaśabdau — vaiśvānaraśabdastu trayasya sādhāraṇaḥ , ātmaśabdaśca dvayasya , tathāpi viśeṣo dṛśyate , yena parameśvaraparatvaṃ tayorabhyupagamyate — ‘ tasya ha vā etasyātmano vaiśvānarasya mūrdhaiva sutejāḥ ’ ityādiḥ | atra hi parameśvara eva dyumūrdhatvādiviśiṣṭo'vasthāntaragataḥ pratyagātmatvenopanyasta ādhyānāyeti gamyate , kāraṇatvāt | kāraṇasya hi sarvābhiḥ kāryagatābhiravasthābhiravasthāvattvāt dyulokādyavayavatvamupapadyate | ‘ sa sarveṣu lokeṣu sarveṣu bhūteṣu sarveṣvātmasvannamatti ’ iti ca sarvalokādyāśrayaṃ phalaṃ śrūyamāṇaṃ paramakāraṇaparigrahe sambhavati , ‘ evaṃ hāsya sarve pāpmānaḥ pradūyante ’ (chā. u. 5 | 24 | 3) iti ca tadvidaḥ sarvapāpmapradāhaśravaṇam , ‘ ko na ātmā kiṃ brahma ’ iti cātmabrahmaśabdābhyāmupakramaḥ ; — ityevametāni liṅgāni parameśvarameva gamayanti | tasmātparameśvara eva vaiśvānaraḥ || 24 ||
records.literal_translation: It is said: Vaishvanara ought to be the Supreme Self. Why? Because of specification of common words. The two terms 'self' and 'Vaishvanara' are indeed common words - Vaishvanara common to three, self common to two - yet a specific determination appears by which both are accepted as referring to the Supreme Lord: 'of this Vaishvanara self, heaven is head...' etc. Here the Supreme Lord alone, qualified by heaven-as-head and so on, is presented as inward self in another mode for meditation, because He is cause. For the cause can stand in all states found in effects; hence having heaven-world and others as limbs is fitting. Also the fruit heard - 'he eats food in all worlds, all beings, all selves' - fits only when the supreme cause is taken. And hearing that all sins of the knower are burned, and the opening with self/Brahman terms ('Who is our self? what is Brahman?'), all these indicators point only to the Supreme Lord. Therefore Vaishvanara is indeed the Supreme Lord. (24)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.24 resolves Vaishvanara polysemy via sadharana-sabda-visesa: cosmic-limb specification, Brahmanic fruits, and opening brahman-atman markers establish Ishvara-reference.

records.source_text: वैश्वानरः साधारणशब्दविशेषात् ॥ २४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: vaiśvānaraḥ sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣāt || 24 ||
records.transliteration: vaiśvānaraḥ sādhāraṇaśabdaviśeṣāt || 24 ||
records.literal_translation: Vaishvanara, because of specification of a common word. (24)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.24: Vaisvanara denotes Brahman through specification constraining otherwise common lexical usage.

records.source_text: इतश्च परमेश्वर एव वैश्वानरः ; यस्मात्परमेश्वरस्यैव ‘ अग्निरास्यं द्यौर्मूर्धा ’ इतीदृशं त्रैलोक्यात्मकं रूपं स्मर्यते — ‘ यस्याग्निरास्यं द्यौर्मूर्धा खं नाभिश्चरणौ क्षितिः । सूर्यश्चक्षुर्दिशः श्रोत्रं तस्मै लोकात्मने नमः ’ इति । एतत्स्मर्यमाणं रूपं मूलभूतां श्रुतिमनुमापयदस्य वैश्वानरशब्दस्य परमेश्वरपरत्वे अनुमानं लिङ्गं गमकं स्यादित्यर्थः । इतिशब्दो हेत्वर्थे — यस्मादिदं गमकम् , तस्मादपि वैश्वानरः परमात्मैवेत्यर्थः । यद्यपि स्तुति रि यम् — ‘ तस्मै लोकात्मने नमः ’ इति , तथापि स्तुतित्वमपि नासति मूलभूते वेदवाक्ये सम्यक् ईदृशेन रूपेण सम्भवति । ‘ द्यां मूर्धानं यस्य विप्रा वदन्ति खं वै नाभिं चन्द्रसूर्यौ च नेत्रे । दिशः श्रोत्रे विद्धि पादौ क्षितिं च सोऽचिन्त्यात्मा सर्वभूतप्रणेता ’ इत्येवंजातीयका च स्मृतिरिहोदाहर्तव्या ॥ २५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca parameśvara eva vaiśvānaraḥ ; yasmātparameśvarasyaiva ‘ agnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā ’ itīdṛśaṃ trailokyātmakaṃ rūpaṃ smaryate — ‘ yasyāgnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā khaṃ nābhiścaraṇau kṣitiḥ | sūryaścakṣurdiśaḥ śrotraṃ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti | etatsmaryamāṇaṃ rūpaṃ mūlabhūtāṃ śrutimanumāpayadasya vaiśvānaraśabdasya parameśvaraparatve anumānaṃ liṅgaṃ gamakaṃ syādityarthaḥ | itiśabdo hetvarthe — yasmādidaṃ gamakam , tasmādapi vaiśvānaraḥ paramātmaivetyarthaḥ | yadyapi stuti ri yam — ‘ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti , tathāpi stutitvamapi nāsati mūlabhūte vedavākye samyak īdṛśena rūpeṇa sambhavati | ‘ dyāṃ mūrdhānaṃ yasya viprā vadanti khaṃ vai nābhiṃ candrasūryau ca netre | diśaḥ śrotre viddhi pādau kṣitiṃ ca so'cintyātmā sarvabhūtapraṇetā ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā ca smṛtirihodāhartavyā || 25 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca parameśvara eva vaiśvānaraḥ ; yasmātparameśvarasyaiva ‘ agnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā ’ itīdṛśaṃ trailokyātmakaṃ rūpaṃ smaryate — ‘ yasyāgnirāsyaṃ dyaurmūrdhā khaṃ nābhiścaraṇau kṣitiḥ | sūryaścakṣurdiśaḥ śrotraṃ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti | etatsmaryamāṇaṃ rūpaṃ mūlabhūtāṃ śrutimanumāpayadasya vaiśvānaraśabdasya parameśvaraparatve anumānaṃ liṅgaṃ gamakaṃ syādityarthaḥ | itiśabdo hetvarthe — yasmādidaṃ gamakam , tasmādapi vaiśvānaraḥ paramātmaivetyarthaḥ | yadyapi stuti ri yam — ‘ tasmai lokātmane namaḥ ’ iti , tathāpi stutitvamapi nāsati mūlabhūte vedavākye samyak īdṛśena rūpeṇa sambhavati | ‘ dyāṃ mūrdhānaṃ yasya viprā vadanti khaṃ vai nābhiṃ candrasūryau ca netre | diśaḥ śrotre viddhi pādau kṣitiṃ ca so'cintyātmā sarvabhūtapraṇetā ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā ca smṛtirihodāhartavyā || 25 ||
records.literal_translation: For this reason too, Vaishvanara is only the Supreme Lord: because this very three-world form - 'fire is His mouth, heaven His head' - is remembered only of the Supreme. Smrti says: 'whose mouth is fire, heaven the head, space the navel, earth the feet, sun the eye, directions the ear - to that world-self, reverence.' This remembered form, by pointing back to an underlying sruti-root, serves as inferential indicator that the term Vaishvanara here refers to the Supreme Lord. The word 'iti' here carries causal force: since this is an indicator, therefore too Vaishvanara is the Supreme Self. Though the cited text is eulogistic ('to that world-self, reverence'), even such praise would not arise correctly in this exact form without a real Vedic root statement. A similar smrti should also be cited: 'whose head the sages call heaven, whose navel space, whose eyes moon and sun, whose ears the directions, whose feet earth - that inconceivable self, leader of all beings.' (25)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.25 adduces smrti as anumana-linga for Vaisvanara=Brahman, arguing remembered cosmic-body formulas presuppose an original sruti source.

records.transliteration_iast: smaryamāṇamanumānaṃ syāditi || 25 ||
records.transliteration: smaryamāṇamanumānaṃ syāditi || 25 ||
records.literal_translation: That which is remembered may be an inferential indicator - thus. (25)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.25: remembered (smrti) material can function as anumana-linga supporting Brahman-reference of Vaisvanara.
