records.literal_translation: If it is said that mantra and arthavada, being for another purpose, cannot establish deity embodiment and related points, we reply: cognition and non-cognition are what establish presence and absence, not merely whether a statement is 'primary' or 'secondary.' Even while pursuing another task, one still cognizes grass and leaves fallen on the road as present. Objection: this is uneven; there direct perception operates, whereas here arthavada joined to injunction as a single sentence only serves praise and cannot separately establish narrative content; just as in 'one should not drink liquor,' the unitary sentence yields prohibition, not an independent positive injunction from a part. Reply: the analogy is uneven. In prohibition-sentences, unified syntactic force can block sub-sentence autonomy; but in injunction-plus-arthavada, arthavada-words first yield their own narrative sense and then, by purposive force, praise the injunction. In 'one desiring prosperity should offer a white oblation to Vayu,' the injunction-terms are linked to injunction; not so with arthavada terms like 'Vayu is the swiftest deity... he leads him to prosperity,' which do not produce separate injunctions such as 'one should offer to speed itself.' Rather, by describing Vayu's nature they praise an act with such a deity-specification. Where the subordinate meaning is established elsewhere, arthavada functions as reiteration; where contrary to other pramana, as figurative praise; where neither condition holds, one should prefer factual assertion (vidyamanavada) over forced figurative reading, because cognition-guided method favors that. The same applies to mantra. Further, injunctions that prescribe oblations to deities like Indra presuppose some form of those deities; one cannot superimpose a wholly formless referent in the mind, nor offer to a deity not mentally instantiated. Sruti says: when the oblation is taken for a deity, meditate on that deity before uttering vashat. Mere word cannot itself be the object's full form, since word and meaning differ. Therefore forms of Indra and others as conveyed in mantra/arthavada should not be denied by a contrary textual strategy. Itihasa and Purana too, when grounded through this route in mantra/arthavada, can support deity embodiment; they may also have roots in perception, since what is not directly perceived by us may have been directly perceived by ancient seers. Tradition recalls Vyasa and others interacting directly with deities. To deny that all earlier people had capacities greater than ours would flatten the world's diversity; it would also undermine rulings like Rajasuya injunctions and make social-ordering shastra pointless by projecting present disorder onto all times. Hence ancients, due to heightened dharma, could directly engage deities. Smriti also speaks of contact with chosen deities through svadhyaya; yogic attainments such as anima cannot be dismissed as mere bravado; sruti extols yoga's power ('for one with body made by yoga-fire, no disease, old age, or death'). The capacities of rishis who saw mantra and brahmana cannot be measured by our capacity. Therefore Itihasa-Purana has roots; and common tradition too should not be treated as baseless where possibility stands. Thus it is coherent that mantra and related sources establish deity embodiment etc.; and from possible desire and capacity, eligibility of gods and others for Brahma-vidya is coherent. Teachings on krama-mukti also fit this line.
records.technical_translation: Extended Badarayana defense in 1.3.33: epistemic argument for treating mantra/arthavada as evidentially meaningful (often vidyamanavada), plus ritual-psychological presupposition of determinate devata-forms, culminating in reaffirmed deva-adhikara.

records.source_text: यथा मनुष्याधिकारनियममपोद्य देवादीनामपि विद्यास्वधिकार उक्तः , तथैव द्विजात्यधिकारनियमापवादेन शूद्रस्याप्यधिकारः स्यादित्येतामाशङ्कां निवर्तयितुमिदमधिकरणमारभ्यते । तत्र शूद्रस्या प्यधिकारः स्यादिति तावत्प्राप्तम् ; अर्थित्वसामर्थ्ययोः सम्भवात् , ‘ तस्माच्छूद्रो यज्ञेऽनवकॢप्तः ’ (तै. सं. ७ । १ । १ । ६) इतिवत् ‘ शूद्रो विद्यायामनवकॢप्त ’ इति निषेधाश्रवणात् । यच्च कर्मस्वनधिकारकारणं शूद्रस्यानग्नित्वम् , न तद्विद्यास्वधिकारस्यापवादकं लिङ्गम् । न ह्याहवनीयादिरहितेन विद्या वेदितुं न शक्यते । भवति च श्रौतं लिङ्गं शूद्राधिकारस्योपोद्बलकम् । संवर्गविद्यायां हि जानश्रुतिं पौत्रायणं शुश्रूषुं शूद्रशब्देन परामृशति — ‘ अह हारे त्वा शूद्र तवैव सह गोभिरस्तु ’ (छा. उ. ४ । २ । ३) इति । विदुरप्रभृतयश्च शूद्रयोनिप्रभवा अपि विशिष्टविज्ञानसम्पन्नाः स्मर्यन्ते । तस्मा दधिक्रियते शूद्रो विद्यास्वित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: yathā manuṣyādhikāraniyamamapodya devādīnāmapi vidyāsvadhikāra uktaḥ , tathaiva dvijātyadhikāraniyamāpavādena śūdrasyāpyadhikāraḥ syādityetāmāśaṅkāṃ nivartayitumidamadhikaraṇamārabhyate | tatra śūdrasyā pyadhikāraḥ syāditi tāvatprāptam ; arthitvasāmarthyayoḥ sambhavāt , ‘ tasmācchūdro yajñe'navakḷptaḥ ’ (tai. saṃ. 7 | 1 | 1 | 6) itivat ‘ śūdro vidyāyāmanavakḷpta ’ iti niṣedhāśravaṇāt | yacca karmasvanadhikārakāraṇaṃ śūdrasyānagnitvam , na tadvidyāsvadhikārasyāpavādakaṃ liṅgam | na hyāhavanīyādirahitena vidyā vedituṃ na śakyate | bhavati ca śrautaṃ liṅgaṃ śūdrādhikārasyopodbalakam | saṃvargavidyāyāṃ hi jānaśrutiṃ pautrāyaṇaṃ śuśrūṣuṃ śūdraśabdena parāmṛśati — ‘ aha hāre tvā śūdra tavaiva saha gobhirastu ’ (chā. u. 4 | 2 | 3) iti | viduraprabhṛtayaśca śūdrayoniprabhavā api viśiṣṭavijñānasampannāḥ smaryante | tasmā dadhikriyate śūdro vidyāsvityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: yathā manuṣyādhikāraniyamamapodya devādīnāmapi vidyāsvadhikāra uktaḥ , tathaiva dvijātyadhikāraniyamāpavādena śūdrasyāpyadhikāraḥ syādityetāmāśaṅkāṃ nivartayitumidamadhikaraṇamārabhyate | tatra śūdrasyā pyadhikāraḥ syāditi tāvatprāptam ; arthitvasāmarthyayoḥ sambhavāt , ‘ tasmācchūdro yajñe'navakḷptaḥ ’ (tai. saṃ. 7 | 1 | 1 | 6) itivat ‘ śūdro vidyāyāmanavakḷpta ’ iti niṣedhāśravaṇāt | yacca karmasvanadhikārakāraṇaṃ śūdrasyānagnitvam , na tadvidyāsvadhikārasyāpavādakaṃ liṅgam | na hyāhavanīyādirahitena vidyā vedituṃ na śakyate | bhavati ca śrautaṃ liṅgaṃ śūdrādhikārasyopodbalakam | saṃvargavidyāyāṃ hi jānaśrutiṃ pautrāyaṇaṃ śuśrūṣuṃ śūdraśabdena parāmṛśati — ‘ aha hāre tvā śūdra tavaiva saha gobhirastu ’ (chā. u. 4 | 2 | 3) iti | viduraprabhṛtayaśca śūdrayoniprabhavā api viśiṣṭavijñānasampannāḥ smaryante | tasmā dadhikriyate śūdro vidyāsvityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: Just as the rule limiting eligibility to humans was set aside and gods were granted eligibility in vidya, one might similarly suspect that by setting aside a twice-born-only rule even the Shudra would be eligible. To remove that doubt this adhikarana begins. At first glance, Shudra eligibility seems arguable: desire and capacity may be present; and while there is an explicit prohibition like 'therefore the Shudra is not fit for sacrifice,' no similarly explicit prohibition for vidya is heard. The reason for ineligibility in karma - lack of sacred fires - does not by itself negate eligibility in vidya, since knowing vidya does not require ahavaniya etc. There also appears to be scriptural support: in Samvarga-vidya Janashruti Pautrayana seeking instruction is addressed with the word 'Shudra.' And figures like Vidura, though born in Shudra lineage, are remembered as possessing exceptional knowledge. Therefore one might conclude: the Shudra is eligible for vidyas. In response we say -
records.technical_translation: Opening of 1.3.34 frames prima facie argument for shudra-adhikara in brahma-vidya from desire/capacity and selected textual indicators, preparing formal refutation.

records.source_text: न शूद्रस्याधिकारः , वेदाध्ययनाभावात् । अधीतवेदो हि विदितवेदार्थो वेदार्थेष्वधिक्रियते । न च शूद्रस्य वेदाध्ययनमस्ति । उपनयनपूर्वकत्वाद्वेदाध्ययनस्य , उपनयनस्य च वर्णत्रयविषयत्वात् । यत्तु अर्थित्वम् , न तदसति सामर्थ्येऽधिकारकारणं भवति । सामर्थ्यमपि न लौकिकं केवलमधिकारकारणं भवति ; शास्त्रीयेऽर्थे शास्त्रीयस्य सामर्थ्यस्यापेक्षितत्वात् । शास्त्रीयस्य च सामर्थ्यस्याध्ययननिराकरणेन निराकृतत्वात् । यच्चेदम् ‘ शूद्रो यज्ञेऽनवकॢप्तः ’ इति , तत् न्यायपूर्वकत्वाद्विद्यायामप्यनवकॢप्तत्वं द्योतयति ; न्यायस्य साधारणत्वात् । य त्पुनः संवर्गविद्यायां शूद्रशब्दश्रवणं लिङ्गं मन्यसे , न तल्लिङ्गम् ; न्यायाभावात् । न्यायोक्ते हि लिङ्गदर्शनं द्योतकं भवति । न चात्र न्यायोऽस्ति । कामं चायं शूद्रशब्दः संवर्गविद्यायामेवैकस्यां शूद्रमधिकुर्यात् , तद्विषयत्वात् । न सर्वासु विद्यासु । अर्थवाद स्थत्वात्तु न क्वचिदप्ययं शूद्रमधिकर्तुमुत्सहते । शक्यते चा यं शूद्रशब्दोऽधिकृतविषयो योजयितुम् । कथमित्युच्यते — ‘ कम्वर एनमेतत्सन्तं सयुग्वानमिव रैक्वमात्थ ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १ । ३) इत्यस्माद्धंसवाक्यादात्मनोऽनादरं श्रुतवतो जानश्रुतेः पौत्रायणस्य शुक् उत्पेदे । तामृषी रैक्वः शूद्रशब्देनानेन सूचयांबभूव आत्मनः परोक्षज्ञताख्यापनायेति गम्यते । जातिशूद्रस्यानधिकारात् । कथं पुनः शूद्रशब्देन शुगुत्पन्ना सूच्यत इति , उच्यते — तदाद्रवणात् ; शुचमभिदुद्राव , शुचा वा अभिदुद्रुवे , शुचा वा रैक्वमभिदुद्राव — इति शूद्रः ; अवयवार्थसम्भवात् , रूढ्यर्थस्य चासम्भवात् । दृश्यते चायमर्थोऽस्यामाख्यायिकायाम् ॥ ३४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ , vedādhyayanābhāvāt | adhītavedo hi viditavedārtho vedārtheṣvadhikriyate | na ca śūdrasya vedādhyayanamasti | upanayanapūrvakatvādvedādhyayanasya , upanayanasya ca varṇatrayaviṣayatvāt | yattu arthitvam , na tadasati sāmarthye'dhikārakāraṇaṃ bhavati | sāmarthyamapi na laukikaṃ kevalamadhikārakāraṇaṃ bhavati ; śāstrīye'rthe śāstrīyasya sāmarthyasyāpekṣitatvāt | śāstrīyasya ca sāmarthyasyādhyayananirākaraṇena nirākṛtatvāt | yaccedam ‘ śūdro yajñe'navakḷptaḥ ’ iti , tat nyāyapūrvakatvādvidyāyāmapyanavakḷptatvaṃ dyotayati ; nyāyasya sādhāraṇatvāt | ya tpunaḥ saṃvargavidyāyāṃ śūdraśabdaśravaṇaṃ liṅgaṃ manyase , na talliṅgam ; nyāyābhāvāt | nyāyokte hi liṅgadarśanaṃ dyotakaṃ bhavati | na cātra nyāyo'sti | kāmaṃ cāyaṃ śūdraśabdaḥ saṃvargavidyāyāmevaikasyāṃ śūdramadhikuryāt , tadviṣayatvāt | na sarvāsu vidyāsu | arthavāda sthatvāttu na kvacidapyayaṃ śūdramadhikartumutsahate | śakyate cā yaṃ śūdraśabdo'dhikṛtaviṣayo yojayitum | kathamityucyate — ‘ kamvara enametatsantaṃ sayugvānamiva raikvamāttha ’ (chā. u. 4 | 1 | 3) ityasmāddhaṃsavākyādātmano'nādaraṃ śrutavato jānaśruteḥ pautrāyaṇasya śuk utpede | tāmṛṣī raikvaḥ śūdraśabdenānena sūcayāṃbabhūva ātmanaḥ parokṣajñatākhyāpanāyeti gamyate | jātiśūdrasyānadhikārāt | kathaṃ punaḥ śūdraśabdena śugutpannā sūcyata iti , ucyate — tadādravaṇāt ; śucamabhidudrāva , śucā vā abhidudruve , śucā vā raikvamabhidudrāva — iti śūdraḥ ; avayavārthasambhavāt , rūḍhyarthasya cāsambhavāt | dṛśyate cāyamartho'syāmākhyāyikāyām || 34 ||
records.transliteration: na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ , vedādhyayanābhāvāt | adhītavedo hi viditavedārtho vedārtheṣvadhikriyate | na ca śūdrasya vedādhyayanamasti | upanayanapūrvakatvādvedādhyayanasya , upanayanasya ca varṇatrayaviṣayatvāt | yattu arthitvam , na tadasati sāmarthye'dhikārakāraṇaṃ bhavati | sāmarthyamapi na laukikaṃ kevalamadhikārakāraṇaṃ bhavati ; śāstrīye'rthe śāstrīyasya sāmarthyasyāpekṣitatvāt | śāstrīyasya ca sāmarthyasyādhyayananirākaraṇena nirākṛtatvāt | yaccedam ‘ śūdro yajñe'navakḷptaḥ ’ iti , tat nyāyapūrvakatvādvidyāyāmapyanavakḷptatvaṃ dyotayati ; nyāyasya sādhāraṇatvāt | ya tpunaḥ saṃvargavidyāyāṃ śūdraśabdaśravaṇaṃ liṅgaṃ manyase , na talliṅgam ; nyāyābhāvāt | nyāyokte hi liṅgadarśanaṃ dyotakaṃ bhavati | na cātra nyāyo'sti | kāmaṃ cāyaṃ śūdraśabdaḥ saṃvargavidyāyāmevaikasyāṃ śūdramadhikuryāt , tadviṣayatvāt | na sarvāsu vidyāsu | arthavāda sthatvāttu na kvacidapyayaṃ śūdramadhikartumutsahate | śakyate cā yaṃ śūdraśabdo'dhikṛtaviṣayo yojayitum | kathamityucyate — ‘ kamvara enametatsantaṃ sayugvānamiva raikvamāttha ’ (chā. u. 4 | 1 | 3) ityasmāddhaṃsavākyādātmano'nādaraṃ śrutavato jānaśruteḥ pautrāyaṇasya śuk utpede | tāmṛṣī raikvaḥ śūdraśabdenānena sūcayāṃbabhūva ātmanaḥ parokṣajñatākhyāpanāyeti gamyate | jātiśūdrasyānadhikārāt | kathaṃ punaḥ śūdraśabdena śugutpannā sūcyata iti , ucyate — tadādravaṇāt ; śucamabhidudrāva , śucā vā abhidudruve , śucā vā raikvamabhidudrāva — iti śūdraḥ ; avayavārthasambhavāt , rūḍhyarthasya cāsambhavāt | dṛśyate cāyamartho'syāmākhyāyikāyām || 34 ||
records.literal_translation: The Shudra has no eligibility, because there is no Vedic study. Only one who has studied Veda and understood Vedic meaning is eligible in Vedic matters. But the Shudra has no Vedic study, since study is preceded by initiation, and initiation pertains to the three varnas. Mere desire does not become a ground of eligibility where required capacity is absent. Nor is merely worldly capacity enough; in scriptural matters scripturally qualified capacity is required, and that has been negated here by exclusion from study. The statement 'the Shudra is unfit for sacrifice' by general reasoning also indicates unfitness for vidya. As for hearing the word Shudra in Samvarga-vidya as an indicator, that is no indicator without supporting rule. At most, even if admitted, it could concern only that specific vidya, not all vidyas; and being in arthavada-context it cannot establish eligibility at all. The term can instead be interpreted for one already otherwise eligible: from the swan-dialogue line ('you thought him yoked with carts' etc.), Janashruti heard disparagement and grief arose; Raikva used the word Shudra to signal that sorrow and his own remote-knowing. Since caste-Shudra has no eligibility here, the word is explained etymologically: one who ran toward sorrow, or was run over by sorrow, or ran toward Raikva in sorrow - thus 'Shudra.' This avayava-based sense is possible while the conventional caste sense is not; and the narrative supports this reading.
records.technical_translation: Main siddhanta of 1.3.34: shudra-anadhikara in Vedic vidya is argued from adhyayana/upanayana structure and reinterpretation of 'shudra' in ChU 4.2.3 as etymological-shoka marker, not varna designation.

records.source_text: शुगस्य तदनादरश्रवणात्तदाद्रवणात्सूच्यते हि ॥ ३४ ॥

A later philological pass should revisit social-category and adhikara terminology choices in records 1.3.089-1.3.096.
records.source_text: इतश्च न जातिशूद्रो जानश्रुतिः ; यत्कारणं प्रकरणनिरूपणेन क्षत्रियत्व मस्योत्तरत्र चैत्ररथेनाभिप्रतारिणा क्षत्रियेण समभिव्याहाराल्लिङ्गाद्गम्यते । उत्तरत्र हि संवर्गविद्यावाक्यशेषे चैत्ररथिरभिप्रतारी क्षत्रियः सङ्कीर्त्यते — ‘ अथ ह शौनकं च कापेयमभिप्रतारिणं च काक्षसेनिं परिविष्यमाणौ ब्रह्मचारी बिभिक्षे ’ (छा. उ. ४ । ३ । ५) इति । चैत्ररथित्वं चा भिप्रतारिणः कापेययोगादवगन्तव्यम् । कापेययोगो हि चित्ररथस्यावगतः ‘ एतेन वै चित्ररथं कापेया अयाजयन् ’ (ताण्ड्य. ब्रा. २० । १२ । ५) इति । समानान्वययाजिनां च प्रायेण समानान्वया याजका भवन्ति । ‘ तस्मा च्चैत्ररथिर्नामैकः क्षत्रपतिरजायत ’ इति च क्षत्रपतित्वावगमात्क्षत्रियत्वमस्यावगन्तव्यम् । तेन क्षत्रियेणाभिप्रतारिणा सह समानायां विद्यायां सङ्कीर्तनं जानश्रुतेरपि क्षत्रियत्वं सूचयति । समानानामेव हि प्रायेण समभिव्याहारा भवन्ति । क्षत्तृप्रेषणाद्यैश्वर्ययोगाच्च जानश्रुतेः क्षत्रियत्वावगतिः । अतो न शूद्रस्याधिकारः ॥ ३५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca na jātiśūdro jānaśrutiḥ ; yatkāraṇaṃ prakaraṇanirūpaṇena kṣatriyatva masyottaratra caitrarathenābhipratāriṇā kṣatriyeṇa samabhivyāhārālliṅgādgamyate | uttaratra hi saṃvargavidyāvākyaśeṣe caitrarathirabhipratārī kṣatriyaḥ saṅkīrtyate — ‘ atha ha śaunakaṃ ca kāpeyamabhipratāriṇaṃ ca kākṣaseniṃ pariviṣyamāṇau brahmacārī bibhikṣe ’ (chā. u. 4 | 3 | 5) iti | caitrarathitvaṃ cā bhipratāriṇaḥ kāpeyayogādavagantavyam | kāpeyayogo hi citrarathasyāvagataḥ ‘ etena vai citrarathaṃ kāpeyā ayājayan ’ (tāṇḍya. brā. 20 | 12 | 5) iti | samānānvayayājināṃ ca prāyeṇa samānānvayā yājakā bhavanti | ‘ tasmā ccaitrarathirnāmaikaḥ kṣatrapatirajāyata ’ iti ca kṣatrapatitvāvagamātkṣatriyatvamasyāvagantavyam | tena kṣatriyeṇābhipratāriṇā saha samānāyāṃ vidyāyāṃ saṅkīrtanaṃ jānaśruterapi kṣatriyatvaṃ sūcayati | samānānāmeva hi prāyeṇa samabhivyāhārā bhavanti | kṣattṛpreṣaṇādyaiśvaryayogācca jānaśruteḥ kṣatriyatvāvagatiḥ | ato na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ || 35 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca na jātiśūdro jānaśrutiḥ ; yatkāraṇaṃ prakaraṇanirūpaṇena kṣatriyatva masyottaratra caitrarathenābhipratāriṇā kṣatriyeṇa samabhivyāhārālliṅgādgamyate | uttaratra hi saṃvargavidyāvākyaśeṣe caitrarathirabhipratārī kṣatriyaḥ saṅkīrtyate — ‘ atha ha śaunakaṃ ca kāpeyamabhipratāriṇaṃ ca kākṣaseniṃ pariviṣyamāṇau brahmacārī bibhikṣe ’ (chā. u. 4 | 3 | 5) iti | caitrarathitvaṃ cā bhipratāriṇaḥ kāpeyayogādavagantavyam | kāpeyayogo hi citrarathasyāvagataḥ ‘ etena vai citrarathaṃ kāpeyā ayājayan ’ (tāṇḍya. brā. 20 | 12 | 5) iti | samānānvayayājināṃ ca prāyeṇa samānānvayā yājakā bhavanti | ‘ tasmā ccaitrarathirnāmaikaḥ kṣatrapatirajāyata ’ iti ca kṣatrapatitvāvagamātkṣatriyatvamasyāvagantavyam | tena kṣatriyeṇābhipratāriṇā saha samānāyāṃ vidyāyāṃ saṅkīrtanaṃ jānaśruterapi kṣatriyatvaṃ sūcayati | samānānāmeva hi prāyeṇa samabhivyāhārā bhavanti | kṣattṛpreṣaṇādyaiśvaryayogācca jānaśruteḥ kṣatriyatvāvagatiḥ | ato na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ || 35 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too Janashruti is not a Shudra by birth: his Kshatriya status is inferred from contextual analysis and from the marker of his later joint mention with the Kshatriya Abhipratarin, descendant of Chaitraratha. In the later continuation of the Samvarga-vidya, Abhipratarin Chaitrarathi is named ('then, as Shaunaka Kapeya and Abhipratarin Kakshaseni were being served, a brahmacarin sought alms'). Abhipratarin's Chaitraratha linkage is known through his Kapeya connection, and that Kapeya connection is known for Chitraratha in other Vedic references. Those who share ritual lineage generally have officiants of similar lineage. A text also notes a Chaitrarathi as a Kshatrapati, indicating Kshatriya status. Janashruti being named in the same vidya-context with that Kshatriya Abhipratarin indicates Janashruti too is Kshatriya; generally, co-mention occurs among social equals. Janashruti's royal powers (sending attendants and the like) also indicate Kshatriya status. Therefore there is no eligibility here for the Shudra.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.35 adduces contextual-linga evidence to reclassify Janashruti as Kshatriya, preserving the prior anadhikara argument for birth-Shudra in Veda-based vidya.

records.source_text: क्षत्रियत्वगतेश्चोत्तरत्र चैत्ररथेन लिङ्गात् ॥ ३५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: kṣatriyatvagateścottaratra caitrarathena liṅgāt || 35 ||
records.transliteration: kṣatriyatvagateścottaratra caitrarathena liṅgāt || 35 ||
records.literal_translation: And because Kshatriya status is established - from the marker in the later passage with Chaitraratha. (35)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.35: Janashruti is inferred as Kshatriya from subsequent contextual indicators, not as Shudra by caste.

records.source_text: इतश्च न शूद्रस्याधिकारः , यद्विद्याप्रदेशेषूपनयनादयः संस्काराः परामृश्यन्ते — ‘ तं होपनिन्ये ’ (श. ब्रा. ११ । ५ । ३ । १३) ‘ अ धीहि भगव इति होपससाद ’ (छा. उ. ७ । १ । १) ‘ ब्रह्मपरा ब्रह्मनिष्ठाः परं ब्रह्मान्वेषमाणा एष ह वै तत्सर्वं वक्ष्यतीति ते ह समित्पाणयो भगवन्तं पिप्पलादमुपसन्नाः ’ (प्र. उ. १ । १) इति च । ‘ ता न्हानुपनीयैव ’ (छा. उ. ५ । ११ । ७) इत्यपि प्रदर्शितैवोपनयनप्राप्तिर्भवति । शूद्रस्य च संस्काराभावोऽभिलप्यते ‘ शूद्रश्चतुर्थो वर्ण एकजातिः ’ (मनु. स्मृ. १० । ४) इत्येकजातित्वस्मरणात् । ‘ न शूद्रे पातकं किञ्चिन्न च संस्कारमर्हति ’ (मनु. स्मृ. १० । १२ । ६) इत्यादिभिश्च ॥ ३६ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ , yadvidyāpradeśeṣūpanayanādayaḥ saṃskārāḥ parāmṛśyante — ‘ taṃ hopaninye ’ (śa. brā. 11 | 5 | 3 | 13) ‘ a dhīhi bhagava iti hopasasāda ’ (chā. u. 7 | 1 | 1) ‘ brahmaparā brahmaniṣṭhāḥ paraṃ brahmānveṣamāṇā eṣa ha vai tatsarvaṃ vakṣyatīti te ha samitpāṇayo bhagavantaṃ pippalādamupasannāḥ ’ (pra. u. 1 | 1) iti ca | ‘ tā nhānupanīyaiva ’ (chā. u. 5 | 11 | 7) ityapi pradarśitaivopanayanaprāptirbhavati | śūdrasya ca saṃskārābhāvo'bhilapyate ‘ śūdraścaturtho varṇa ekajātiḥ ’ (manu. smṛ. 10 | 4) ityekajātitvasmaraṇāt | ‘ na śūdre pātakaṃ kiñcinna ca saṃskāramarhati ’ (manu. smṛ. 10 | 12 | 6) ityādibhiśca || 36 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ , yadvidyāpradeśeṣūpanayanādayaḥ saṃskārāḥ parāmṛśyante — ‘ taṃ hopaninye ’ (śa. brā. 11 | 5 | 3 | 13) ‘ a dhīhi bhagava iti hopasasāda ’ (chā. u. 7 | 1 | 1) ‘ brahmaparā brahmaniṣṭhāḥ paraṃ brahmānveṣamāṇā eṣa ha vai tatsarvaṃ vakṣyatīti te ha samitpāṇayo bhagavantaṃ pippalādamupasannāḥ ’ (pra. u. 1 | 1) iti ca | ‘ tā nhānupanīyaiva ’ (chā. u. 5 | 11 | 7) ityapi pradarśitaivopanayanaprāptirbhavati | śūdrasya ca saṃskārābhāvo'bhilapyate ‘ śūdraścaturtho varṇa ekajātiḥ ’ (manu. smṛ. 10 | 4) ityekajātitvasmaraṇāt | ‘ na śūdre pātakaṃ kiñcinna ca saṃskāramarhati ’ (manu. smṛ. 10 | 12 | 6) ityādibhiśca || 36 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too there is no eligibility for the Shudra: in sections teaching vidya, rites such as initiation are referred to - 'he initiated him,' 'approach, sir, teach me,' and 'those intent on Brahman, with fuel in hand, approached Pippalada.' Even the line 'he taught only after initiating them' shows initiation as prerequisite. And absence of such sacraments for the Shudra is stated, including remembrance that the fourth varna is single-born, and statements such as 'there is no sacrament for the Shudra.' (36)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.36 argues anadhikara from saṃskāra-preconditions (especially upanayana) explicitly tied to vidya entry in shruti and denied in smriti for Shudra status.

records.transliteration: saṃskāraparāmarśāttadabhāvābhilāpācca || 36 ||

records.source_text: इतश्च न शूद्रस्याधिकारः ; यत्सत्यवचनेन शूद्रत्वाभावे निर्धारिते जाबालं गौतम उपनेतुमनुशासितुं च प्रववृते — ‘ नैतदब्राह्मणो विवक्तुमर्हति समिधं सोम्याहरोप त्वा नेष्ये न सत्यादगाः ’ (छा. उ. ४ । ४ । ५) इति श्रुतिलिङ्गात् ॥ ३७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ ; yatsatyavacanena śūdratvābhāve nirdhārite jābālaṃ gautama upanetumanuśāsituṃ ca pravavṛte — ‘ naitadabrāhmaṇo vivaktumarhati samidhaṃ somyāharopa tvā neṣye na satyādagāḥ ’ (chā. u. 4 | 4 | 5) iti śrutiliṅgāt || 37 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ ; yatsatyavacanena śūdratvābhāve nirdhārite jābālaṃ gautama upanetumanuśāsituṃ ca pravavṛte — ‘ naitadabrāhmaṇo vivaktumarhati samidhaṃ somyāharopa tvā neṣye na satyādagāḥ ’ (chā. u. 4 | 4 | 5) iti śrutiliṅgāt || 37 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too there is no eligibility for the Shudra: when truthfulness established absence of Shudra status, Gautama proceeded to initiate and instruct Jabala. This is indicated by the shruti marker: 'one who is not a brahmana could not speak thus; bring fuel, dear one, I shall initiate you - you have not departed from truth.' (37)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.37 treats the Satyakama Jabala episode as linga: pedagogical initiation follows determination of non-Shudra status.

records.source_text: इतश्च न शूद्रस्याधिकारः ; यदस्य स्मृतेः श्रवणाध्ययनार्थप्रतिषेधो भवति । वेदश्रवणप्रतिषेधः , वेदाध्ययनप्रतिषेधः , तदर्थज्ञानानुष्ठानयोश्च प्रतिषेधः शूद्रस्य स्मर्यते । श्रवणप्रतिषेधस्तावत् — ‘ अथ हास्य वेदमुपशृण्वतस्त्रपुजतुभ्यां श्रोत्रप्रतिपूरणम् ’ इति ; ‘ पद्यु ह वा एतच्छ्मशानं यच्छूद्रस्तस्माच्छूद्रसमीपे नाध्येतव्यम् ’ इति च । अत एवाध्ययनप्रतिषेधः । यस्य हि समीपेऽपि नाध्येतव्यं भवति , स कथमश्रुतमधीयीत । भवति च ‘ वेदोच्चारणे जिह्वाच्छेदः , धारणे शरीरभेद ’(गौ॰ध॰सू॰ २-३-४) इति । अत एव चार्थादर्थज्ञानानुष्ठानयोः प्रतिषेधो भवति — ‘ न शूद्राय मतिं दद्यात् ’(म॰स्मृ॰ ४-८०) इति , ‘ द्विजातीनामध्ययनमिज्या दानम् ’(गौ॰ध॰सू॰ २-१-१) इति च । येषां पुनः पूर्वकृतसंस्कारवशाद्विदुरधर्मव्याधप्रभृतीनां ज्ञानोत्पत्तिः , तेषां न शक्यते फलप्राप्तिः प्रतिषेद्धुम् , ज्ञानस्यैकान्तिकफलत्वात् । ‘ श्रावये च्चतुरो वर्णान् ’(म॰भा॰ १२-३२७-४९) इति चेतिहासपुराणाधिगमे चातुर्वर्ण्यस्याधिकारस्मरणात् । वेदपूर्वकस्तु नास्त्यधिकारः शूद्राणामिति स्थितम् ॥ ३८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ ; yadasya smṛteḥ śravaṇādhyayanārthapratiṣedho bhavati | vedaśravaṇapratiṣedhaḥ , vedādhyayanapratiṣedhaḥ , tadarthajñānānuṣṭhānayośca pratiṣedhaḥ śūdrasya smaryate | śravaṇapratiṣedhastāvat — ‘ atha hāsya vedamupaśṛṇvatastrapujatubhyāṃ śrotrapratipūraṇam ’ iti ; ‘ padyu ha vā etacchmaśānaṃ yacchūdrastasmācchūdrasamīpe nādhyetavyam ’ iti ca | ata evādhyayanapratiṣedhaḥ | yasya hi samīpe'pi nādhyetavyaṃ bhavati , sa kathamaśrutamadhīyīta | bhavati ca ‘ vedoccāraṇe jihvācchedaḥ , dhāraṇe śarīrabheda ’(gau॰dha॰sū॰ 2-3-4) iti | ata eva cārthādarthajñānānuṣṭhānayoḥ pratiṣedho bhavati — ‘ na śūdrāya matiṃ dadyāt ’(ma॰smṛ॰ 4-80) iti , ‘ dvijātīnāmadhyayanamijyā dānam ’(gau॰dha॰sū॰ 2-1-1) iti ca | yeṣāṃ punaḥ pūrvakṛtasaṃskāravaśādviduradharmavyādhaprabhṛtīnāṃ jñānotpattiḥ , teṣāṃ na śakyate phalaprāptiḥ pratiṣeddhum , jñānasyaikāntikaphalatvāt | ‘ śrāvaye ccaturo varṇān ’(ma॰bhā॰ 12-327-49) iti cetihāsapurāṇādhigame cāturvarṇyasyādhikārasmaraṇāt | vedapūrvakastu nāstyadhikāraḥ śūdrāṇāmiti sthitam || 38 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca na śūdrasyādhikāraḥ ; yadasya smṛteḥ śravaṇādhyayanārthapratiṣedho bhavati | vedaśravaṇapratiṣedhaḥ , vedādhyayanapratiṣedhaḥ , tadarthajñānānuṣṭhānayośca pratiṣedhaḥ śūdrasya smaryate | śravaṇapratiṣedhastāvat — ‘ atha hāsya vedamupaśṛṇvatastrapujatubhyāṃ śrotrapratipūraṇam ’ iti ; ‘ padyu ha vā etacchmaśānaṃ yacchūdrastasmācchūdrasamīpe nādhyetavyam ’ iti ca | ata evādhyayanapratiṣedhaḥ | yasya hi samīpe'pi nādhyetavyaṃ bhavati , sa kathamaśrutamadhīyīta | bhavati ca ‘ vedoccāraṇe jihvācchedaḥ , dhāraṇe śarīrabheda ’(gau॰dha॰sū॰ 2-3-4) iti | ata eva cārthādarthajñānānuṣṭhānayoḥ pratiṣedho bhavati — ‘ na śūdrāya matiṃ dadyāt ’(ma॰smṛ॰ 4-80) iti , ‘ dvijātīnāmadhyayanamijyā dānam ’(gau॰dha॰sū॰ 2-1-1) iti ca | yeṣāṃ punaḥ pūrvakṛtasaṃskāravaśādviduradharmavyādhaprabhṛtīnāṃ jñānotpattiḥ , teṣāṃ na śakyate phalaprāptiḥ pratiṣeddhum , jñānasyaikāntikaphalatvāt | ‘ śrāvaye ccaturo varṇān ’(ma॰bhā॰ 12-327-49) iti cetihāsapurāṇādhigame cāturvarṇyasyādhikārasmaraṇāt | vedapūrvakastu nāstyadhikāraḥ śūdrāṇāmiti sthitam || 38 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too there is no eligibility for the Shudra: smriti lays down prohibition regarding hearing, study, and application of Veda for him. Prohibition of hearing is stated, as are statements against Vedic recitation in proximity and punitive rules regarding utterance and retention. From that follows prohibition of study: one forbidden even proximity to recitation cannot study what he has not heard. From that further follows prohibition of Vedic meaning-knowledge and its ritual implementation, supported by rules such as not imparting Vedic counsel to Shudra and by restricting study/sacrifice/gift to twice-born groups. Yet where knowledge arises through prior-life saṃskāra - as in cases like Vidura and Dharmavyadha - the resulting fruit cannot be denied, because knowledge has determinate fruit. Also, because Itihasa-Purana study is remembered as available to all four varnas, access there is acknowledged. The settled conclusion is: Shudras have no eligibility in what is Veda-based.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.38 distinguishes Veda-mediated adhikara (denied) from possible non-Vedic knowledge channels and Itihasa-Purana access (admitted), while preserving the core anadhikara claim.

records.source_text: अवसितः प्रासङ्गिकोऽधिकारविचारः । प्रकृतामेवेदानीं वाक्यार्थविचारणां प्रवर्तयिष्यामः । ‘ यदिदं किञ्च जगत्सर्वं प्राण एजति निःसृतम् । महद्भयं वज्र मुद्यतं य एत द्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ’ (क. उ. २ । ३ । २) इति एतद्वाक्यम् ‘ एजृ कम्पने ’ इति धात्वर्थानुगमाल्लक्षितम् । अस्मिन्वाक्ये सर्वमिदं जगत् प्राणाश्रयं स्पन्दते , महच्च किञ्चिद्भयकारणं वज्रशब्दितमुद्यतम् , तद्विज्ञानाच्चामृतत्वप्राप्तिरिति श्रूयते । तत्र , कोऽसौ प्राणः , किं तद्भयानकं वज्रम् , इत्यप्रतिपत्तेर्विचारे क्रियमाणे , प्राप्तं तावत् — प्रसिद्धेः पञ्चवृत्ति र्वायुः प्राण इति । प्रसिद्धेरेव चाशनिर्वज्रं स्यात् । वायो श्चेदं माहात्म्यं सङ्कीर्त्यते । कथम् ? सर्वमिदं जगत् पञ्चवृत्तौ वायौ प्राणशब्दिते प्रतिष्ठाय एजति । वायु निमित्तमेव च महद्भयानकं वज्रमुद्यम्यते । वायौ हि पर्जन्यभावेन विवर्तमाने विद्युत्स्तनयित्नुवृष्ट्यशनयो विवर्तन्त इत्याचक्षते । वायुविज्ञानादेव चेदममृतत्वम् । तथा हि श्रुत्यन्तरम् — ‘ वायुरेव व्यष्टिर्वायुः समष्टिरप पुनर्मृत्युं जयति य एवं वेद ’ इति । तस्माद्वायुरयमिह प्रतिपत्तव्यः इत्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: avasitaḥ prāsaṅgiko'dhikāravicāraḥ | prakṛtāmevedānīṃ vākyārthavicāraṇāṃ pravartayiṣyāmaḥ | ‘ yadidaṃ kiñca jagatsarvaṃ prāṇa ejati niḥsṛtam | mahadbhayaṃ vajra mudyataṃ ya eta dviduramṛtāste bhavanti ’ (ka. u. 2 | 3 | 2) iti etadvākyam ‘ ejṛ kampane ’ iti dhātvarthānugamāllakṣitam | asminvākye sarvamidaṃ jagat prāṇāśrayaṃ spandate , mahacca kiñcidbhayakāraṇaṃ vajraśabditamudyatam , tadvijñānāccāmṛtatvaprāptiriti śrūyate | tatra , ko'sau prāṇaḥ , kiṃ tadbhayānakaṃ vajram , ityapratipattervicāre kriyamāṇe , prāptaṃ tāvat — prasiddheḥ pañcavṛtti rvāyuḥ prāṇa iti | prasiddhereva cāśanirvajraṃ syāt | vāyo ścedaṃ māhātmyaṃ saṅkīrtyate | katham ? sarvamidaṃ jagat pañcavṛttau vāyau prāṇaśabdite pratiṣṭhāya ejati | vāyu nimittameva ca mahadbhayānakaṃ vajramudyamyate | vāyau hi parjanyabhāvena vivartamāne vidyutstanayitnuvṛṣṭyaśanayo vivartanta ityācakṣate | vāyuvijñānādeva cedamamṛtatvam | tathā hi śrutyantaram — ‘ vāyureva vyaṣṭirvāyuḥ samaṣṭirapa punarmṛtyuṃ jayati ya evaṃ veda ’ iti | tasmādvāyurayamiha pratipattavyaḥ ityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: avasitaḥ prāsaṅgiko'dhikāravicāraḥ | prakṛtāmevedānīṃ vākyārthavicāraṇāṃ pravartayiṣyāmaḥ | ‘ yadidaṃ kiñca jagatsarvaṃ prāṇa ejati niḥsṛtam | mahadbhayaṃ vajra mudyataṃ ya eta dviduramṛtāste bhavanti ’ (ka. u. 2 | 3 | 2) iti etadvākyam ‘ ejṛ kampane ’ iti dhātvarthānugamāllakṣitam | asminvākye sarvamidaṃ jagat prāṇāśrayaṃ spandate , mahacca kiñcidbhayakāraṇaṃ vajraśabditamudyatam , tadvijñānāccāmṛtatvaprāptiriti śrūyate | tatra , ko'sau prāṇaḥ , kiṃ tadbhayānakaṃ vajram , ityapratipattervicāre kriyamāṇe , prāptaṃ tāvat — prasiddheḥ pañcavṛtti rvāyuḥ prāṇa iti | prasiddhereva cāśanirvajraṃ syāt | vāyo ścedaṃ māhātmyaṃ saṅkīrtyate | katham ? sarvamidaṃ jagat pañcavṛttau vāyau prāṇaśabdite pratiṣṭhāya ejati | vāyu nimittameva ca mahadbhayānakaṃ vajramudyamyate | vāyau hi parjanyabhāvena vivartamāne vidyutstanayitnuvṛṣṭyaśanayo vivartanta ityācakṣate | vāyuvijñānādeva cedamamṛtatvam | tathā hi śrutyantaram — ‘ vāyureva vyaṣṭirvāyuḥ samaṣṭirapa punarmṛtyuṃ jayati ya evaṃ veda ’ iti | tasmādvāyurayamiha pratipattavyaḥ ityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: The incidental discussion of eligibility is concluded. We now resume examination of the sentence-meaning proper. The text says: 'Whatever this whole world is, it trembles as issued forth from prana; the great fear, the upraised thunderbolt - those who know this become immortal.' Taking ejati by the root sense 'to tremble,' we hear that the whole world vibrates grounded in prana, that there is some great fear-causing upraised thunderbolt, and that by knowing it immortality is attained. There arises inquiry: what is this prana, and what is that fearsome thunderbolt? At first, one may take prana in its common sense as air with five functions, and the thunderbolt as literal lightning by common usage. Then the passage would praise air: the world depends on fivefold air and vibrates; from air's transformations arise lightning, thunder, rain, and bolt as great fear; and by knowing air comes immortality, as another text says one who knows air as individual and total overcomes death. Thus one may conclude that air is intended here. To that we reply -
records.technical_translation: Opening of 1.3.39 sets a purvapaksha reading prana/vajra as vayu and lightning via lexical convention and cosmological function before shifting to Brahman reading.

records.source_text: ब्रह्मै वेदमिह प्रतिपत्तव्यम् । कुतः ? पूर्वोत्तरालोचनात् । पूर्वोत्तरयोर्हि ग्रन्थभागयोर्ब्रह्मैव निर्दिश्यमानमुपलभामहे । इहैव कथमकस्मादन्तराले वायुं निर्दिश्यमानं प्रतिपद्येमहि ? पूर्वत्र तावत् ‘ तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते । तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन ’ (क. उ. २ । ३ । १) इति ब्रह्म निर्दिष्टम् । तदेव इहापि , सन्निधानात् , ‘ जगत्सर्वं प्राण एजति ’ इति च लोकाश्रयत्वप्रत्यभिज्ञानात् निर्दिष्टमिति गम्यते । प्राणशब्दोऽप्ययं परमात्मन्येव प्रयुक्तः — ‘ प्राणस्य प्राणम् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । १८) इति दर्शनात् । एजयितृत्व मपीदं परमात्मन एवोपपद्यते , न वायुमात्रस्य । तथा चोक्तम् — ‘ न प्राणेन नापानेन मर्त्यो जीवति कश्चन । इतरेण तु जीवन्ति यस्मिन्नेतावुपाश्रितौ ’ (क. उ. २ । २ । ५) इति । उत्तरत्रापि ‘ भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः । भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ’ (क. उ. २ । ३ । ३) इति ब्रह्मैव निर्देक्ष्यते , न वायुः , सवायुकस्य जगतो भयहेतुत्वाभिधानात् । तदेव इहापि सन्निधानात् ‘ महद्भयं वज्रमुद्यतम् ’ इति च भयहेतुत्वप्रत्यभिज्ञानान्निर्दिष्टमिति गम्यते । वज्रशब्दो ऽप्ययं भयहेतुत्वसामान्यात्प्रयुक्तः । यथा हि ‘ वज्रमुद्यतं ममैव शिरसि निपतेत् , यद्यहमस्य शासनं न कुर्याम् ’ इत्यनेन भयेन जनो नियमेन राजादिशासने प्रवर्तते , एवमिदमग्निवायुसूर्यादिकं जगत् अस्मादेव ब्रह्मणो बिभ्यत् नियमेन स्वव्यापारे प्रवर्तत इति — भयानकं वज्रोपमितं ब्रह्म । तथा च ब्रह्मविषयं श्रुत्यन्तरम् — ‘ भीषास्माद्वातः पवते । भीषोदेति सूर्यः । भीषास्मादग्निश्चेन्द्रश्च । मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ’ (तै. उ. २ । ८ । १) इति । अमृतत्वफलश्रवणादपि ब्रह्मैवेदमिति गम्यते । ब्रह्मज्ञानाद्ध्यमृतत्वप्राप्तिः , ‘ तमेव विदित्वाति मृत्युमेति नान्यः पन्था विद्यतेऽयनाय ’ (श्वे. उ. ६ । १५) इति मन्त्रवर्णात् । यत्तु वायु विज्ञानात्क्वचिदमृतत्वमभिहितम् , तदापेक्षिकम् । तत्रैव प्रकरणान्तरकरणेन परमात्मानमभिधाय ‘ अतोऽन्यदार्तम् ’ (बृ. उ. ३ । ४ । २) इति वाय्वादेरार्तत्वाभिधानात् । प्रकरणादप्यत्र परमात्मनिश्चयः । ‘ अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मादन्यत्रास्मात्कृताकृतात् । अन्यत्र भूताच्च भव्याच्च यत्तत्पश्यसि तद्वद ’ (क. उ. १ । २ । १४) इति परमात्मनः पृष्टत्वात् ॥ ३९ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: brahmai vedamiha pratipattavyam | kutaḥ ? pūrvottarālocanāt | pūrvottarayorhi granthabhāgayorbrahmaiva nirdiśyamānamupalabhāmahe | ihaiva kathamakasmādantarāle vāyuṃ nirdiśyamānaṃ pratipadyemahi ? pūrvatra tāvat ‘ tadeva śukraṃ tadbrahma tadevāmṛtamucyate | tasmi~llokāḥ śritāḥ sarve tadu nātyeti kaścana ’ (ka. u. 2 | 3 | 1) iti brahma nirdiṣṭam | tadeva ihāpi , sannidhānāt , ‘ jagatsarvaṃ prāṇa ejati ’ iti ca lokāśrayatvapratyabhijñānāt nirdiṣṭamiti gamyate | prāṇaśabdo'pyayaṃ paramātmanyeva prayuktaḥ — ‘ prāṇasya prāṇam ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 18) iti darśanāt | ejayitṛtva mapīdaṃ paramātmana evopapadyate , na vāyumātrasya | tathā coktam — ‘ na prāṇena nāpānena martyo jīvati kaścana | itareṇa tu jīvanti yasminnetāvupāśritau ’ (ka. u. 2 | 2 | 5) iti | uttaratrāpi ‘ bhayādasyāgnistapati bhayāttapati sūryaḥ | bhayādindraśca vāyuśca mṛtyurdhāvati pañcamaḥ ’ (ka. u. 2 | 3 | 3) iti brahmaiva nirdekṣyate , na vāyuḥ , savāyukasya jagato bhayahetutvābhidhānāt | tadeva ihāpi sannidhānāt ‘ mahadbhayaṃ vajramudyatam ’ iti ca bhayahetutvapratyabhijñānānnirdiṣṭamiti gamyate | vajraśabdo 'pyayaṃ bhayahetutvasāmānyātprayuktaḥ | yathā hi ‘ vajramudyataṃ mamaiva śirasi nipatet , yadyahamasya śāsanaṃ na kuryām ’ ityanena bhayena jano niyamena rājādiśāsane pravartate , evamidamagnivāyusūryādikaṃ jagat asmādeva brahmaṇo bibhyat niyamena svavyāpāre pravartata iti — bhayānakaṃ vajropamitaṃ brahma | tathā ca brahmaviṣayaṃ śrutyantaram — ‘ bhīṣāsmādvātaḥ pavate | bhīṣodeti sūryaḥ | bhīṣāsmādagniścendraśca | mṛtyurdhāvati pañcamaḥ ’ (tai. u. 2 | 8 | 1) iti | amṛtatvaphalaśravaṇādapi brahmaivedamiti gamyate | brahmajñānāddhyamṛtatvaprāptiḥ , ‘ tameva viditvāti mṛtyumeti nānyaḥ panthā vidyate'yanāya ’ (śve. u. 6 | 15) iti mantravarṇāt | yattu vāyu vijñānātkvacidamṛtatvamabhihitam , tadāpekṣikam | tatraiva prakaraṇāntarakaraṇena paramātmānamabhidhāya ‘ ato'nyadārtam ’ (bṛ. u. 3 | 4 | 2) iti vāyvāderārtatvābhidhānāt | prakaraṇādapyatra paramātmaniścayaḥ | ‘ anyatra dharmādanyatrādharmādanyatrāsmātkṛtākṛtāt | anyatra bhūtācca bhavyācca yattatpaśyasi tadvada ’ (ka. u. 1 | 2 | 14) iti paramātmanaḥ pṛṣṭatvāt || 39 ||

records.literal_translation: What is to be understood here is Brahman alone. Why? From examining what comes before and after. In both adjacent portions of the text, Brahman is what is taught; why should we abruptly read mere air in between? Earlier the text says 'that alone is the pure, that is Brahman, that is called immortal; all worlds rest in it; none passes beyond it.' By proximity, the same is intended here too; and from recognition of being the support of worlds in 'the whole world trembles in prana.' This word prana is also used for the Supreme Self elsewhere ('the prana of prana'). This power of making all tremble fits the Supreme, not air alone; as taught, mortals do not live by inhalation/exhalation themselves but by that on which both depend. Later too, 'from fear of Him fire burns, from fear the sun shines, from fear Indra and Vayu, and death as fifth run' points to Brahman, not Vayu, since the whole world including Vayu is said to stand in fear. Hence by proximity, 'great fear, upraised thunderbolt' likewise refers to that same cause of fear. 'Thunderbolt' here is used by similarity as fear-cause: just as people obey a king out of fear of an upraised thunderbolt-like punishment, so fire, wind, sun, etc. proceed in ordered function out of fear of Brahman; thus Brahman is figuratively likened to a terrifying thunderbolt. Another text supports this: from His dread wind blows, sun rises, fire and Indra act, death runs fifth. Hearing immortality as fruit also indicates Brahman, since immortality comes from Brahman-knowledge ('knowing Him alone one crosses death'). Where immortality is sometimes stated from knowledge of Vayu, that is relative, as shown where after distinguishing the Supreme, Vayu etc. are said to be afflicted. The section-context too fixes the Supreme, because what was asked was that beyond dharma/adharma, beyond done/undone, beyond past/future.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.39 resolves prana/vajra as Brahman through contextual continuity, semantic recognition, fear-causality hierarchy, and moksha-fruit criteria; vayu-reading is downgraded to secondary/relative contexts.

records.source_text: ‘ एष सम्प्रसादोऽस्माच्छरीरात्समुत्थाय परं ज्योतिरुपसम्पद्य स्वेन रूपेणाभिनिष्पद्यते ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १२ । ३) इति श्रूयते । तत्र संशय्यते — किं ज्योतिःशब्दं चक्षुर्विषयतमोपहं तेजः , किं वा परं ब्रह्मेति । किं तावत्प्राप्तम् ? प्रसिद्धमेव तेजो ज्योतिः शब्दमिति । कुतः ? तत्र ज्योतिःशब्दस्य रूढत्वात् । ‘ ज्योतिश्चरणाभिधानात् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । २४) इत्यत्र हि प्रकरणाज्ज्योतिःशब्दः स्वार्थं परित्यज्य ब्रह्मणि वर्तते । न चेह तद्वत्किञ्चित्स्वार्थपरित्यागे कारणं दृश्यते । तथा च नाडीखण्डे — ‘ अथ यत्रैतदस्माच्छरीरादुत्क्रामत्यथैतैरेव रश्मिभिरूर्ध्वमाक्रमते ’ (छा. उ. ८ । ६ । ५) इति मुमुक्षोरादित्यप्राप्तिरभिहिता । तस्मात्प्रसिद्धमेव तेजो ज्योतिःशब्दमिति , एवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ eṣa samprasādo'smāccharīrātsamutthāya paraṃ jyotirupasampadya svena rūpeṇābhiniṣpadyate ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 3) iti śrūyate | tatra saṃśayyate — kiṃ jyotiḥśabdaṃ cakṣurviṣayatamopahaṃ tejaḥ , kiṃ vā paraṃ brahmeti | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? prasiddhameva tejo jyotiḥ śabdamiti | kutaḥ ? tatra jyotiḥśabdasya rūḍhatvāt | ‘ jyotiścaraṇābhidhānāt ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 24) ityatra hi prakaraṇājjyotiḥśabdaḥ svārthaṃ parityajya brahmaṇi vartate | na ceha tadvatkiñcitsvārthaparityāge kāraṇaṃ dṛśyate | tathā ca nāḍīkhaṇḍe — ‘ atha yatraitadasmāccharīrādutkrāmatyathaitaireva raśmibhirūrdhvamākramate ’ (chā. u. 8 | 6 | 5) iti mumukṣorādityaprāptirabhihitā | tasmātprasiddhameva tejo jyotiḥśabdamiti , evaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: ‘ eṣa samprasādo'smāccharīrātsamutthāya paraṃ jyotirupasampadya svena rūpeṇābhiniṣpadyate ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 3) iti śrūyate | tatra saṃśayyate — kiṃ jyotiḥśabdaṃ cakṣurviṣayatamopahaṃ tejaḥ , kiṃ vā paraṃ brahmeti | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? prasiddhameva tejo jyotiḥ śabdamiti | kutaḥ ? tatra jyotiḥśabdasya rūḍhatvāt | ‘ jyotiścaraṇābhidhānāt ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 24) ityatra hi prakaraṇājjyotiḥśabdaḥ svārthaṃ parityajya brahmaṇi vartate | na ceha tadvatkiñcitsvārthaparityāge kāraṇaṃ dṛśyate | tathā ca nāḍīkhaṇḍe — ‘ atha yatraitadasmāccharīrādutkrāmatyathaitaireva raśmibhirūrdhvamākramate ’ (chā. u. 8 | 6 | 5) iti mumukṣorādityaprāptirabhihitā | tasmātprasiddhameva tejo jyotiḥśabdamiti , evaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: The text says: 'This serene one, rising from this body, attaining the supreme light, manifests in its own form.' Here doubt arises: does 'light' denote ordinary visible light that dispels darkness, or the Supreme Brahman? Prima facie one may take ordinary light, because that is the established lexical sense of jyotis. In another case ('because jyotis and quarters are mentioned') jyotis is read as Brahman only by force of context after giving up its primary sense. Here no such reason for abandoning primary sense appears. Also in the nadi section it is said that the seeker ascends upward through rays and reaches the sun. Therefore one may conclude that jyotis here means ordinary light. To that we reply -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.3.40 frames para-jyotis either as physical luminosity or Brahman, pressing lexical default and nadi/rays ascent evidence for the former.

records.source_text: परमेव ब्रह्म ज्योतिःशब्दम् । कस्मात् ? दर्शनात् । तस्य हीह प्रकरणे वक्तव्यत्वेनानुवृत्तिर्दृश्यते ; ‘ य आत्मापहतपाप्मा ’ (छा. उ. ८ । ७ । १) इत्यपहतपाप्मत्वादिगुणकस्यात्मनः प्रकरणादावन्वेष्टव्यत्वेन विजिज्ञासितव्यत्वेन च प्रतिज्ञानात् । ‘ एतं त्वेव ते भूयोऽनुव्याख्यास्यामि ’ (छा. उ. ८ । ९ । ३) इति चानुसन्धानात् । ‘ अशरीरं वाव सन्तं न प्रियाप्रिये स्पृशतः ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १२ । १) इति चाशरीरतायै ज्योतिःसम्पत्तेरस्याभिधानात् । ब्रह्मभावाच्चान्यत्राशरीरतानुपपत्तेः । ‘ परं ज्योतिः ’ ‘ स उत्तमः पुरुषः ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १२ । ३) इति च विशेषणात् । य त्तूक्तं मुमुक्षोरादित्यप्राप्तिरभिहितेति , नासावात्यन्तिको मोक्षः , गत्युत्क्रान्तिसम्बन्धात् । न ह्यात्यन्तिके मोक्षे गत्युत्क्रान्ती स्त इति वक्ष्यामः ॥ ४० ॥
records.transliteration_iast: parameva brahma jyotiḥśabdam | kasmāt ? darśanāt | tasya hīha prakaraṇe vaktavyatvenānuvṛttirdṛśyate ; ‘ ya ātmāpahatapāpmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 7 | 1) ityapahatapāpmatvādiguṇakasyātmanaḥ prakaraṇādāvanveṣṭavyatvena vijijñāsitavyatvena ca pratijñānāt | ‘ etaṃ tveva te bhūyo'nuvyākhyāsyāmi ’ (chā. u. 8 | 9 | 3) iti cānusandhānāt | ‘ aśarīraṃ vāva santaṃ na priyāpriye spṛśataḥ ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 1) iti cāśarīratāyai jyotiḥsampatterasyābhidhānāt | brahmabhāvāccānyatrāśarīratānupapatteḥ | ‘ paraṃ jyotiḥ ’ ‘ sa uttamaḥ puruṣaḥ ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 3) iti ca viśeṣaṇāt | ya ttūktaṃ mumukṣorādityaprāptirabhihiteti , nāsāvātyantiko mokṣaḥ , gatyutkrāntisambandhāt | na hyātyantike mokṣe gatyutkrāntī sta iti vakṣyāmaḥ || 40 ||
records.transliteration: parameva brahma jyotiḥśabdam | kasmāt ? darśanāt | tasya hīha prakaraṇe vaktavyatvenānuvṛttirdṛśyate ; ‘ ya ātmāpahatapāpmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 7 | 1) ityapahatapāpmatvādiguṇakasyātmanaḥ prakaraṇādāvanveṣṭavyatvena vijijñāsitavyatvena ca pratijñānāt | ‘ etaṃ tveva te bhūyo'nuvyākhyāsyāmi ’ (chā. u. 8 | 9 | 3) iti cānusandhānāt | ‘ aśarīraṃ vāva santaṃ na priyāpriye spṛśataḥ ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 1) iti cāśarīratāyai jyotiḥsampatterasyābhidhānāt | brahmabhāvāccānyatrāśarīratānupapatteḥ | ‘ paraṃ jyotiḥ ’ ‘ sa uttamaḥ puruṣaḥ ’ (chā. u. 8 | 12 | 3) iti ca viśeṣaṇāt | ya ttūktaṃ mumukṣorādityaprāptirabhihiteti , nāsāvātyantiko mokṣaḥ , gatyutkrāntisambandhāt | na hyātyantike mokṣe gatyutkrāntī sta iti vakṣyāmaḥ || 40 ||
records.literal_translation: The word jyotis here denotes the Supreme Brahman alone. Why? Because the section shows continuous reference to that topic. At the beginning, the Self qualified by freedom from sin and similar attributes is pledged as what is to be sought and known. The text then reconnects, 'I shall explain that very one again.' It also says attainment of this jyotis leads to bodilessness ('being bodiless, one is not touched by pleasant and unpleasant'), and such absolute bodilessness is not coherent apart from Brahman-realization. The qualifiers too are decisive: 'supreme light' and 'that is the highest person.' As for the claim that sun-attainment is taught for the seeker, that is not final liberation, since it belongs to a context of path and departure; we shall explain that in final liberation there is no going or departing.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.40 establishes para-jyotis as Brahman from prakarana-continuity, anuvyakhyana linkage, aśarīratva consequence, and uttama-puruṣa qualifiers; solar attainment belongs to lower/mediate soteriological pathing.

records.source_text: ‘ आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म तदमृतं स आत्मा ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १४ । १) इति श्रूयते । तत्किमाकाशशब्दं परं ब्रह्म , किं वा प्रसिद्धमेव भू ताकाशमिति विचारे — भूतपरिग्रहो युक्तः ; आकाशशब्दस्य तस्मिन् रूढत्वात् । नामरूपनिर्वहणस्य चावकाशदानद्वारेण तस्मिन्योजयितुं शक्यत्वात् । स्रष्टृत्वादेश्च स्पष्टस्य ब्रह्मलिङ्गस्याश्रवणादित्येवं प्राप्ते इदमुच्यते —
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayornirvahitā te yadantarā tadbrahma tadamṛtaṃ sa ātmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) iti śrūyate | tatkimākāśaśabdaṃ paraṃ brahma , kiṃ vā prasiddhameva bhū tākāśamiti vicāre — bhūtaparigraho yuktaḥ ; ākāśaśabdasya tasmin rūḍhatvāt | nāmarūpanirvahaṇasya cāvakāśadānadvāreṇa tasminyojayituṃ śakyatvāt | sraṣṭṛtvādeśca spaṣṭasya brahmaliṅgasyāśravaṇādityevaṃ prāpte idamucyate —
records.transliteration: ‘ ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayornirvahitā te yadantarā tadbrahma tadamṛtaṃ sa ātmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) iti śrūyate | tatkimākāśaśabdaṃ paraṃ brahma , kiṃ vā prasiddhameva bhū tākāśamiti vicāre — bhūtaparigraho yuktaḥ ; ākāśaśabdasya tasmin rūḍhatvāt | nāmarūpanirvahaṇasya cāvakāśadānadvāreṇa tasminyojayituṃ śakyatvāt | sraṣṭṛtvādeśca spaṣṭasya brahmaliṅgasyāśravaṇādityevaṃ prāpte idamucyate —
records.literal_translation: The text says: 'Space indeed is the bearer of name and form; that which is between them is Brahman, that is immortal, that is the Self.' Here inquiry arises: does akasha mean Supreme Brahman, or the commonly known elemental space? Prima facie one may take elemental space, because the word is conventionally fixed there. Also 'bearing name and form' can be mapped to elemental space insofar as it provides room. And explicit Brahman-markers like clear creator-statements are not directly heard in this sentence. Thus one may initially take elemental space. In response it is said -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.3.41 prefers bhutakasha reading via lexical convention and accommodative reading of 'name-form bearing'.

records.source_text: परमेव ब्रह्म इहाकाशशब्दं भवितुमर्हति । कस्मात् ? अर्थान्तरत्वादि व्यपदेशात् । ‘ ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ’ इति हि नामरूपाभ्यामर्थान्तरभूतमाकाशं व्यपदिशति । न च ब्रह्मणोऽन्यन्नामरूपाभ्यामर्थान्तरं सम्भवति , सर्वस्य विकारजातस्य नामरूपाभ्यामेव व्याकृतत्वात् । नामरूपयोरपि निर्वहणं निरङ्कुशं न ब्रह्मणोऽन्यत्र सम्भवति , ‘ अनेन जीवेनात्मनानुप्रविश्य नामरूपे व्याकरवाणि ’ (छा. उ. ६ । ३ । २) इति ब्रह्मकर्तृकत्वश्रवणात् । ननु जीवस्यापि प्रत्यक्षं नामरूपविषयं निर्वोढृत्वमस्ति । बाढ मस्ति । अभेदस्त्विह विवक्षितः । नाम रूपनिर्वहणाभिधानादेव च स्रष्टृत्वादि ब्रह्मलिङ्गमभिहितं भवति । ‘ तद्ब्रह्म तदमृतं स आत्मा ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १४ । १) इति च ब्रह्मवादस्य लिङ्गानि । ‘ आकाश स्तल्लिङ्गात् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । २२) इत्यस्यैवायं प्रपञ्चः ॥ ४१ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: parameva brahma ihākāśaśabdaṃ bhavitumarhati | kasmāt ? arthāntaratvādi vyapadeśāt | ‘ te yadantarā tadbrahma ’ iti hi nāmarūpābhyāmarthāntarabhūtamākāśaṃ vyapadiśati | na ca brahmaṇo'nyannāmarūpābhyāmarthāntaraṃ sambhavati , sarvasya vikārajātasya nāmarūpābhyāmeva vyākṛtatvāt | nāmarūpayorapi nirvahaṇaṃ niraṅkuśaṃ na brahmaṇo'nyatra sambhavati , ‘ anena jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākaravāṇi ’ (chā. u. 6 | 3 | 2) iti brahmakartṛkatvaśravaṇāt | nanu jīvasyāpi pratyakṣaṃ nāmarūpaviṣayaṃ nirvoḍhṛtvamasti | bāḍha masti | abhedastviha vivakṣitaḥ | nāma rūpanirvahaṇābhidhānādeva ca sraṣṭṛtvādi brahmaliṅgamabhihitaṃ bhavati | ‘ tadbrahma tadamṛtaṃ sa ātmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) iti ca brahmavādasya liṅgāni | ‘ ākāśa stalliṅgāt ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 22) ityasyaivāyaṃ prapañcaḥ || 41 ||
records.transliteration: parameva brahma ihākāśaśabdaṃ bhavitumarhati | kasmāt ? arthāntaratvādi vyapadeśāt | ‘ te yadantarā tadbrahma ’ iti hi nāmarūpābhyāmarthāntarabhūtamākāśaṃ vyapadiśati | na ca brahmaṇo'nyannāmarūpābhyāmarthāntaraṃ sambhavati , sarvasya vikārajātasya nāmarūpābhyāmeva vyākṛtatvāt | nāmarūpayorapi nirvahaṇaṃ niraṅkuśaṃ na brahmaṇo'nyatra sambhavati , ‘ anena jīvenātmanānupraviśya nāmarūpe vyākaravāṇi ’ (chā. u. 6 | 3 | 2) iti brahmakartṛkatvaśravaṇāt | nanu jīvasyāpi pratyakṣaṃ nāmarūpaviṣayaṃ nirvoḍhṛtvamasti | bāḍha masti | abhedastviha vivakṣitaḥ | nāma rūpanirvahaṇābhidhānādeva ca sraṣṭṛtvādi brahmaliṅgamabhihitaṃ bhavati | ‘ tadbrahma tadamṛtaṃ sa ātmā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) iti ca brahmavādasya liṅgāni | ‘ ākāśa stalliṅgāt ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 22) ityasyaivāyaṃ prapañcaḥ || 41 ||
records.literal_translation: Here the word akasha should denote Supreme Brahman alone. Why? Because it is taught as other-than name and form and by related indicators. The line 'that which is between those two is Brahman' presents akasha as distinct from name and form. But apart from Brahman there is nothing truly outside name and form, since all transformations are articulated as name-form. Further, unrestricted governance of name and form cannot stand anywhere other than Brahman, as heard: 'having entered with this living self, I shall differentiate name and form.' One may object that empirical selves also manage name and form in some way; granted, but identity-reference is intended here. Merely by stating governance of name and form, creatorhood and similar Brahman-indicators are already conveyed. And the clause 'that is Brahman, that is immortal, that is the Self' provides direct indicators. This is an expanded exposition of the sutra 'akasha, because of that indicator.' (41)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.41 resolves akasha as Brahman from arthantara-vyapadesha (beyond nama-rupa), nirvahana-capacity, and explicit brahma-linga phrases in-text.

records.source_text: व्यपदेशादित्यनुवर्तते । बृहदारण्यके षष्ठे प्रपाठके ‘ कतम आत्मेति योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु हृ द्यन्तर्ज्योतिः पुरुषः ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । ७) इत्युपक्रम्य भूयानात्मविषयः प्रपञ्चः कृतः । तत्किं संसारिस्वरूपमात्रान्वाख्यानपरं वाक्यम् , उतासंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपरमिति संशयः । किं तावत्प्राप्तम् ? संसारिस्वरूपमात्रविषयमेवेति । कुतः ? उपक्रमोपसंहाराभ्याम् । उपक्रमे ‘ योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ इति शारीरलिङ्गात् । उपसंहारे च ‘ स वा एष महानज आत्मा योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । २२) इति तदपरित्यागात् । मध्येऽपि बुद्धान्ताद्यवस्थोपन्यासेन तस्यैव प्रपञ्चनादित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
records.transliteration_iast: vyapadeśādityanuvartate | bṛhadāraṇyake ṣaṣṭhe prapāṭhake ‘ katama ātmeti yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu hṛ dyantarjyotiḥ puruṣaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 7) ityupakramya bhūyānātmaviṣayaḥ prapañcaḥ kṛtaḥ | tatkiṃ saṃsārisvarūpamātrānvākhyānaparaṃ vākyam , utāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamiti saṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? saṃsārisvarūpamātraviṣayameveti | kutaḥ ? upakramopasaṃhārābhyām | upakrame ‘ yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ iti śārīraliṅgāt | upasaṃhāre ca ‘ sa vā eṣa mahānaja ātmā yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 22) iti tadaparityāgāt | madhye'pi buddhāntādyavasthopanyāsena tasyaiva prapañcanādityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.transliteration: vyapadeśādityanuvartate | bṛhadāraṇyake ṣaṣṭhe prapāṭhake ‘ katama ātmeti yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu hṛ dyantarjyotiḥ puruṣaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 7) ityupakramya bhūyānātmaviṣayaḥ prapañcaḥ kṛtaḥ | tatkiṃ saṃsārisvarūpamātrānvākhyānaparaṃ vākyam , utāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamiti saṃśayaḥ | kiṃ tāvatprāptam ? saṃsārisvarūpamātraviṣayameveti | kutaḥ ? upakramopasaṃhārābhyām | upakrame ‘ yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ iti śārīraliṅgāt | upasaṃhāre ca ‘ sa vā eṣa mahānaja ātmā yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 22) iti tadaparityāgāt | madhye'pi buddhāntādyavasthopanyāsena tasyaiva prapañcanādityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —
records.literal_translation: The phrase 'because of indication' continues. In the sixth chapter of the Brhadaranyaka, beginning with 'which is the Self? - this consciousness-formed person among the pranas, the inner light in the heart,' an extended Self-discourse is given. Doubt arises: is the passage merely describing the transmigrating individual, or teaching the non-transmigrating reality? Prima facie it seems to describe only the transmigrating self, from beginning and ending markers: at the beginning, the bodily-sign expression 'this consciousness-formed one among the pranas'; at the end, retention of that same expression, 'this great unborn Self, this consciousness-formed one among the pranas.' Even in the middle, by discussing waking and related states, it appears to elaborate that same individual. In response we say -
records.technical_translation: Opening purvapaksha for 1.3.42 argues sharīra/jiva reading by upakrama-upasamhara continuity and state-analysis references.

records.source_text: परमेश्व रोपदेशपरमेवेदं वाक्यम् , न शारीरमात्रान्वाख्यानपरम् । कस्मात् ? सुषुप्तावुत्क्रान्तौ च शारीराद्भेदेन परमेश्वरस्य व्यपदेशात् । सुषुप्तौ तावत् ‘ अयं पुरुषः प्राज्ञेनात्मना सम्परिष्वक्तो न बाह्यं किञ्चन वेद नान्तरम् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । २१) इति शारीराद्भेदेन परमेश्वरं व्यपदिशति । तत्र पुरुषः शारीरः स्यात् , तस्य वेदितृत्वात् । बाह्याभ्यन्तरवेदनप्रसङ्गे सति तत्प्रतिषेधसम्भवात् । प्राज्ञः परमेश्वरः , सर्वज्ञत्वलक्षणया प्रज्ञया नित्यमवियोगात् । तथोत्क्रान्तावपि ‘ अयं शारीर आत्मा प्राज्ञेनात्मनान्वारूढ उत्सर्जन्याति ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । ३५) इति जीवाद्भेदेन परमेश्वरं व्यपदिशति । तत्रापि शारीरो जीवः स्यात् , शरीरस्वामित्वात् । प्राज्ञस्तु स एव परमेश्वरः । तस्मात्सुषुप्त्युत्क्रान्त्योर्भेदेन व्यपदेशात्परमेश्वर एवात्र विवक्षित इति गम्यते । यदुक्तमाद्यन्तमध्येषु शारीरलिङ्गात् तत्परत्वमस्य वाक्यस्येति , अत्र ब्रूमः — उपक्रमे तावत् ‘ योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ इति न संसारिस्वरूपं विवक्षितम् — किं तर्हि ? — अनूद्य संसारिस्वरूपं परेण ब्रह्मणास्यैकतां विवक्षति । यतः ‘ ध्यायतीव लेलायतीव ’ इत्येवमाद्युत्तरग्रन्थप्रवृत्तिः संसारिधर्मनिराकरणपरा लक्ष्यते । त थोपसंहारेऽपि यथोपक्रममेवोपसंहरति — ‘ स वा एष महानज आत्मा योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु ’ इति । योऽयं विज्ञानमयः प्राणेषु संसारी लक्ष्यते , स वा एष महानज आत्मा परमेश्वर एवास्माभिः प्रतिपादित इत्यर्थः । यस्तु मध्ये बुद्धान्ताद्यवस्थोपन्यासात्संसारिस्वरूपविवक्षां मन्यते , स प्राचीमपि दिशं प्रस्थापितः प्रतीचीमपि दिशं प्रतिष्ठेत । यतो न बुद्धा न्ताद्यवस्थोपन्यासेनावस्थावत्त्वं संसारित्वं वा विवक्षितं — किं तर्हि ? — अवस्थारहितत्वमसंसारित्वं च । कथमेतदवगम्यते ? यत् ‘ अत ऊर्ध्वं विमोक्षायैव ब्रूहि ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । १४) इति पदे पदे पृच्छति । यच्च ‘ अनन्वागतस्तेन भवत्यसङ्गो ह्ययं पुरुषः ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । १५) इति पदे पदे प्रतिवक्ति । ‘ अनन्वागतं पुण्येनानन्वागतं पापेन तीर्णो हि तदा सर्वाञ्शोकान्हृदयस्य भवति ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ३ । २२) इति च । तस्मादसंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपरमेवैतद्वाक्यमित्यवगन्तव्यम् ॥ ४२ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: parameśva ropadeśaparamevedaṃ vākyam , na śārīramātrānvākhyānaparam | kasmāt ? suṣuptāvutkrāntau ca śārīrādbhedena parameśvarasya vyapadeśāt | suṣuptau tāvat ‘ ayaṃ puruṣaḥ prājñenātmanā sampariṣvakto na bāhyaṃ kiñcana veda nāntaram ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 21) iti śārīrādbhedena parameśvaraṃ vyapadiśati | tatra puruṣaḥ śārīraḥ syāt , tasya veditṛtvāt | bāhyābhyantaravedanaprasaṅge sati tatpratiṣedhasambhavāt | prājñaḥ parameśvaraḥ , sarvajñatvalakṣaṇayā prajñayā nityamaviyogāt | tathotkrāntāvapi ‘ ayaṃ śārīra ātmā prājñenātmanānvārūḍha utsarjanyāti ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 35) iti jīvādbhedena parameśvaraṃ vyapadiśati | tatrāpi śārīro jīvaḥ syāt , śarīrasvāmitvāt | prājñastu sa eva parameśvaraḥ | tasmātsuṣuptyutkrāntyorbhedena vyapadeśātparameśvara evātra vivakṣita iti gamyate | yaduktamādyantamadhyeṣu śārīraliṅgāt tatparatvamasya vākyasyeti , atra brūmaḥ — upakrame tāvat ‘ yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ iti na saṃsārisvarūpaṃ vivakṣitam — kiṃ tarhi ? — anūdya saṃsārisvarūpaṃ pareṇa brahmaṇāsyaikatāṃ vivakṣati | yataḥ ‘ dhyāyatīva lelāyatīva ’ ityevamādyuttaragranthapravṛttiḥ saṃsāridharmanirākaraṇaparā lakṣyate | ta thopasaṃhāre'pi yathopakramamevopasaṃharati — ‘ sa vā eṣa mahānaja ātmā yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu ’ iti | yo'yaṃ vijñānamayaḥ prāṇeṣu saṃsārī lakṣyate , sa vā eṣa mahānaja ātmā parameśvara evāsmābhiḥ pratipādita ityarthaḥ | yastu madhye buddhāntādyavasthopanyāsātsaṃsārisvarūpavivakṣāṃ manyate , sa prācīmapi diśaṃ prasthāpitaḥ pratīcīmapi diśaṃ pratiṣṭheta | yato na buddhā ntādyavasthopanyāsenāvasthāvattvaṃ saṃsāritvaṃ vā vivakṣitaṃ — kiṃ tarhi ? — avasthārahitatvamasaṃsāritvaṃ ca | kathametadavagamyate ? yat ‘ ata ūrdhvaṃ vimokṣāyaiva brūhi ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 14) iti pade pade pṛcchati | yacca ‘ ananvāgatastena bhavatyasaṅgo hyayaṃ puruṣaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 15) iti pade pade prativakti | ‘ ananvāgataṃ puṇyenānanvāgataṃ pāpena tīrṇo hi tadā sarvāñśokānhṛdayasya bhavati ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 22) iti ca | tasmādasaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamevaitadvākyamityavagantavyam || 42 ||

— avasthārahitatvamasaṃsāritvaṃ ca | kathametadavagamyate ? yat ‘ ata ūrdhvaṃ vimokṣāyaiva brūhi ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 14) iti pade pade pṛcchati | yacca ‘ ananvāgatastena bhavatyasaṅgo hyayaṃ puruṣaḥ ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 15) iti pade pade prativakti | ‘ ananvāgataṃ puṇyenānanvāgataṃ pāpena tīrṇo hi tadā sarvāñśokānhṛdayasya bhavati ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 3 | 22) iti ca | tasmādasaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamevaitadvākyamityavagantavyam || 42 ||
records.literal_translation: This passage is intended to teach the Supreme Lord, not merely to describe the embodied individual. Why? Because in deep sleep and in departure, the Supreme is designated as distinct from the embodied self. In sleep, 'this person embraced by the knowing Self knows no external, no internal' distinguishes the embodied knower from the supreme knower: the 'person' is the embodied one (since knowing belongs there in context), while the 'knowing one' is the Supreme, inseparable from omniscient awareness. In departure too, 'this embodied self, mounted by the knowing Self, goes forth' distinguishes jiva from Supreme. Therefore by explicit differentiation in sleep and death-transition, the intended referent here is the Supreme. As for the claim that beginning, middle, and end bodily markers prove jiva-intent: in the opening, 'this consciousness-formed one among the pranas' does not intend to stop at jiva-definition; rather, having introduced the empirical self, it intends identity of this with the higher Brahman. This is seen from later flow such as 'as if meditating, as if moving,' which aims at negating samsaric predicates. The conclusion mirrors the opening in that same way: the apparently empirical one is taught as the great unborn Self, i.e., the Supreme. And if someone argues that the middle-state descriptions intend samsaric nature, that is self-defeating: those portions actually intend state-transcendence and non-samsaric character. This is evident because the student repeatedly asks for teaching only for liberation, and the replies repeatedly stress non-attachment ('this person is unattached') and not being touched by merit/demerit, crossing all heart-born sorrow. Therefore this passage is to be understood as teaching the non-samsaric nature alone.
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.42 resolves the Brhadaranyaka passage as Ishvara/Brahman instruction by bheda-vyapadesha in suṣupti-utkranti contexts and by systematic negation of samsari-dharmas.

records.source_text: सुषुप्त्युत्क्रान्त्योर्भेदेन ॥ ४२ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: suṣuptyutkrāntyorbhedena || 42 ||
records.transliteration: suṣuptyutkrāntyorbhedena || 42 ||
records.literal_translation: Because of distinction in deep sleep and departure. (42)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.42: difference-language in suṣupti and utkranti establishes the intended referent as the Supreme, not merely jiva.

qa_uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.

records.source_text: इत श्चासंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपरमेवैतद्वाक्यमित्यवगन्तव्यम् ; यदस्मिन्वाक्ये पत्यादयः शब्दा असंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपराः संसारिस्वभावप्रतिषेधनाश्च भवन्ति — ‘ सर्वस्य वशी सर्वस्येशानः सर्वस्याधिपतिः ’ इत्येवंजातीयका असंसारिस्वभावप्रतिपादनपराः । ‘ स न साधुना कर्मणा भूयान्नो एवासाधुना कनीयान् ’ इत्येवंजातीयकाः संसारिस्वभावप्रतिषेधनाः । तस्मादसंसारी परमेश्वर इहोक्त इत्यवगम्यते ॥ ४३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ita ścāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamevaitadvākyamityavagantavyam ; yadasminvākye patyādayaḥ śabdā asaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāśca bhavanti — ‘ sarvasya vaśī sarvasyeśānaḥ sarvasyādhipatiḥ ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā asaṃsārisvabhāvapratipādanaparāḥ | ‘ sa na sādhunā karmaṇā bhūyānno evāsādhunā kanīyān ’ ityevaṃjātīyakāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāḥ | tasmādasaṃsārī parameśvara ihokta ityavagamyate || 43 ||
records.transliteration: ita ścāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamevaitadvākyamityavagantavyam ; yadasminvākye patyādayaḥ śabdā asaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāśca bhavanti — ‘ sarvasya vaśī sarvasyeśānaḥ sarvasyādhipatiḥ ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā asaṃsārisvabhāvapratipādanaparāḥ | ‘ sa na sādhunā karmaṇā bhūyānno evāsādhunā kanīyān ’ ityevaṃjātīyakāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāḥ | tasmādasaṃsārī parameśvara ihokta ityavagamyate || 43 ||
records.literal_translation: And for this reason too this passage should be understood as teaching the non-samsaric nature: in this passage words such as 'lord' and related terms positively teach non-samsaric nature and negate samsaric traits. Expressions like 'controller of all, ruler of all, overlord of all' present the non-samsaric nature. Expressions like 'he is not greater by good action nor smaller by bad action' negate the defining marks of transmigrating existence. Therefore it is understood that the non-samsaric Supreme Lord is what is taught here. (43)
records.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.43 adds patyadi-shabda evidence: sovereignty predicates and karma-transcendence clauses signal Ishvara, not conditioned jiva.

records.source_text: पत्यादिशब्देभ्यः ॥ ४३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: patyādiśabdebhyaḥ || 43 ||
records.transliteration: patyādiśabdebhyaḥ || 43 ||
records.literal_translation: Because of words such as 'lord'. (43)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.43: patyadi terms indicate the Supreme non-samsaric Self.

records.source_text: ब्रह्म जिज्ञासां प्रतिज्ञाय ब्रह्मणो लक्षणमुक्तम् — ‘ जन्माद्यस्य यतः ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । २) इति । तल्लक्षणं प्रधानस्यापि समानमित्याशङ्क्य तदशब्दत्वेन निराकृतम् — ‘ ईक्षतेर्नाशब्दम् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । ५) इति । गतिसामान्यं च वेदान्तवाक्यानां ब्रह्मकारणवादं प्रति विद्यते , न प्रधानकारणवादं प्रतीति प्रपञ्चितं गतेन ग्रन्थेन । इदं त्विदानीमवशिष्टमाशङ्क्यते — यदुक्तं प्रधानस्याशब्दत्वम् , तदसिद्धम् , कासुचिच्छाखासु प्रधानसमर्पणाभासानां शब्दानां श्रूयमाणत्वात् । अतः प्रधानस्य कारणत्वं वेदसिद्धमेव महद्भिः परमर्षिभिः कपिलप्रभृतिभिः परिगृहीतमिति प्रसज्यते । तद्यावत्तेषां शब्दानामन्यपरत्वं न प्रतिपाद्यते , तावत्सर्वज्ञं ब्रह्म जगतः कारणमिति प्रतिपादितमप्याकुलीभवेत् । अतस्तेषामन्यपरत्वं दर्शयितुं परः सन्दर्भः प्रवर्तते ॥
records.transliteration_iast: brahma jijñāsāṃ pratijñāya brahmaṇo lakṣaṇamuktam — ‘ janmādyasya yataḥ ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 2) iti | tallakṣaṇaṃ pradhānasyāpi samānamityāśaṅkya tadaśabdatvena nirākṛtam — ‘ īkṣaternāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) iti | gatisāmānyaṃ ca vedāntavākyānāṃ brahmakāraṇavādaṃ prati vidyate , na pradhānakāraṇavādaṃ pratīti prapañcitaṃ gatena granthena | idaṃ tvidānīmavaśiṣṭamāśaṅkyate — yaduktaṃ pradhānasyāśabdatvam , tadasiddham , kāsucicchākhāsu pradhānasamarpaṇābhāsānāṃ śabdānāṃ śrūyamāṇatvāt | ataḥ pradhānasya kāraṇatvaṃ vedasiddhameva mahadbhiḥ paramarṣibhiḥ kapilaprabhṛtibhiḥ parigṛhītamiti prasajyate | tadyāvatteṣāṃ śabdānāmanyaparatvaṃ na pratipādyate , tāvatsarvajñaṃ brahma jagataḥ kāraṇamiti pratipāditamapyākulībhavet | atasteṣāmanyaparatvaṃ darśayituṃ paraḥ sandarbhaḥ pravartate ||
records.transliteration: brahma jijñāsāṃ pratijñāya brahmaṇo lakṣaṇamuktam — ‘ janmādyasya yataḥ ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 2) iti | tallakṣaṇaṃ pradhānasyāpi samānamityāśaṅkya tadaśabdatvena nirākṛtam — ‘ īkṣaternāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) iti | gatisāmānyaṃ ca vedāntavākyānāṃ brahmakāraṇavādaṃ prati vidyate , na pradhānakāraṇavādaṃ pratīti prapañcitaṃ gatena granthena | idaṃ tvidānīmavaśiṣṭamāśaṅkyate — yaduktaṃ pradhānasyāśabdatvam , tadasiddham , kāsucicchākhāsu pradhānasamarpaṇābhāsānāṃ śabdānāṃ śrūyamāṇatvāt | ataḥ pradhānasya kāraṇatvaṃ vedasiddhameva mahadbhiḥ paramarṣibhiḥ kapilaprabhṛtibhiḥ parigṛhītamiti prasajyate | tadyāvatteṣāṃ śabdānāmanyaparatvaṃ na pratipādyate , tāvatsarvajñaṃ brahma jagataḥ kāraṇamiti pratipāditamapyākulībhavet | atasteṣāmanyaparatvaṃ darśayituṃ paraḥ sandarbhaḥ pravartate ||
records.literal_translation: Having announced inquiry into Brahman, the characteristic of Brahman was given as "that from which origin and so on proceed". Doubting that this mark might equally fit Pradhana, it was rejected as non-scriptural by the aphorism "not non-scriptural, because of seeing". It was also shown that the general movement of Vedanta sentences supports Brahman as cause, not Pradhana. Yet one residual doubt is now raised: in some recensions words seem to indicate Pradhana; if so, Pradhana's causality could appear Veda-established and sanctioned by sages such as Kapila. Until those words are shown to have another purport, even the established conclusion that omniscient Brahman is world-cause would be unsettled. Therefore the next context proceeds to show that those terms intend something else.
records.technical_translation: Introductory framing for the avyakta-adhikarana: the bhashya recalls earlier anti-pradhana conclusions and identifies the remaining hermeneutic pressure point, namely seeming scriptural support in branch-specific wording. The task is to prove anyaparatva of those terms so Brahman-causality remains intact.

records.source_text: आनुमानिक मपि अनुमाननिरूपितमपि प्रधानम् , एकेषां शाखिनां शब्दवदुपलभ्यते ; काठके हि पठ्यते — ‘ महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ११) इति ; तत्र य एव यन्नामानो यत्क्रमाश्च महदव्यक्तपुरुषाः स्मृतिप्रसिद्धाः , त एवेह प्रत्यभिज्ञायन्ते । तत्राव्यक्तमिति स्मृतिप्रसिद्धेः , शब्दादि हीनत्वाच्च न व्यक्तमव्यक्तमिति व्युत्पत्तिसम्भवात् , स्मृतिप्रसिद्धं प्रधानमभिधीयते । अतस्तस्य शब्दवत्त्वादशब्दत्वमनुपपन्नम् । तदेव च जगतः कारणं श्रुतिस्मृतिन्यायप्रसिद्धिभ्य इति चेत् , नैत देवम् — न ह्ये तत्काठकवाक्यं स्मृतिप्रसिद्धयोर्महदव्यक्तयोरस्तित्वपरम् । न ह्यत्र यादृशं स्मृतिप्रसिद्धं स्वतन्त्रं कारणं त्रिगुणं प्रधानम् , तादृशं प्रत्यभिज्ञायते । शब्दमात्रं ह्यत्राव्यक्तमिति प्रत्यभिज्ञायते । स च शब्दः — न व्यक्तमव्यक्तमिति — यौगिकत्वात् अन्यस्मिन्नपि सूक्ष्मे सुदुर्लक्ष्ये च प्रयुज्यते । न चा यं कस्मिंश्चिद्रूढः । या तु प्रधानवादिनां रूढिः , सा तेषामेव पारिभाषिकी सती न वेदार्थनिरूपणे कारणभावं प्रतिपद्यते । न च क्रम मात्रसामान्यात्समानार्थप्रतिपत्तिर्भवति , असति तद्रूपप्रत्यभिज्ञाने । न ह्य श्वस्थाने गां पश्यन्नश्वोऽयमित्यमूढोऽध्यवस्यति । प्रकरण निरूपणायां चात्र न परपरिकल्पितं प्रधानं प्रतीयते , शरीररूपकविन्यस्तगृहीतेः । शरीरं ह्यत्र रथरूपकविन्यस्तमव्यक्तशब्देन परिगृह्यते । कुतः ? प्रकरणात् परिशेषाच्च । तथा ह्य नन्तरातीतो ग्रन्थ आत्म शरीरादीनां रथिरथादिरूपककॢप्तिं दर्शयति — ‘ आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि शरीरं रथमेव तु । बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च ॥’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ३) ‘ इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयांस्तेषु गोचरान् । आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ४) इति । तै श्चेन्द्रियादिभिरसंयतैः संसारमधिगच्छति , संयतैस्त्वध्वनः पारं तद्विष्णोः परमं पदमाप्नोति इति दर्शयित्वा , किं तदध्वनः पारं विष्णोः परमं पदमित्यस्यामाकाङ्क्षायाम् , तेभ्य एव प्रकृतेभ्यः इन्द्रियादिभ्यः परत्वेन परमात्मानमध्वनः पारं विष्णोः परमं पदं दर्शयति — ‘ इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः ॥’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । १०) ‘ महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः । पुरुषान्न परं किञ्चित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । ११) इति ; तत्र य ए वेन्द्रियादयः पूर्वस्यां रथरूपककल्पनायामश्वादिभावेन प्रकृताः , त एवेह परिगृह्यन्ते , प्रकृतहानाप्रकृतप्रक्रियापरिहाराय । तत्र इन्द्रिय मनोबुद्धयस्तावत्पूर्वत्र इह च समानशब्दा एव । अर्था स्तु ये शब्दादयो विषया इन्द्रियहयगोचरत्वेन निर्दिष्टाः , तेषां चेन्द्रियेभ्यः परत्वम् , इन्द्रियाणां ग्रहत्वं विषयाणामतिग्रहत्वम् इति श्रुतिप्रसिद्धेः । विषयेभ्यश्च मनसः परत्वम् , मनोमूलत्वाद्विषयेन्द्रियव्यवहारस्य । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिः — बुद्धिं ह्यारुह्य भोग्यजातं भोक्तारमुपसर्पति । बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः — यः , सः ‘ आत्मानं रथिनं विद्धि ’ इति रथित्वेनोपक्षिप्तः । कुतः ? आत्मशब्दात् , भोक्तुश्च भोगोपकरणात्परत्वोपपत्तेः । महत्त्वं चास्य स्वामित्वादुपपन्नम् । अथवा — ‘ मनो महान्मतिर्ब्रह्मा पूर्बुद्धिः ख्यातिरीश्वरः । प्रज्ञा संविच्चितिश्चैव स्मृतिश्च परिपठ्यते ’ इति स्मृतेः , ‘ यो ब्रह्माणं विदधाति पूर्वं यो वै वेदांश्च प्रहिणोति तस्मै ’ (श्वे. उ. ६ । १८) इति च श्रुतेः , या प्रथमजस्य हिरण्यगर्भस्य बुद्धिः , सा सर्वासां बुद्धीनां परमा प्रतिष्ठा ; सेह महानात्मेत्युच्यते । सा च पूर्वत्र बुद्धिग्रहणेनैव गृहीता सती हिरुगिहोपदिश्यते , तस्या अप्यस्मदीयाभ्यो बुद्धिभ्यः परत्वोपपत्तेः । एतस्मिंस्तु पक्षे परमात्मविषयेणैव परेण पुरुषग्रहणेन रथिन आत्मनो ग्रहणं द्रष्टव्यम् , परमार्थतः परमात्मविज्ञानात्मनोर्भेदाभावात् । तदेवं शरीरमेवैकं परिशिष्यते तेषु । इतरा णीन्द्रियादीनि प्रकृतान्येव परमपददिदर्शयिषया समनुक्रामन्परिशिष्यमाणेनेहान्त्येनाव्यक्तशब्देन परिशिष्यमाणं प्रकृतं शरीरं दर्शयतीति गम्यते । शरी रेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिविषयवेदनासंयुक्तस्य ह्यविद्यावतो भोक्तुः शरीरादीनां रथादिरूपककल्पनया संसारमोक्षगतिनिरूपणेन प्रत्यगात्मब्रह्मावगतिरिह विवक्षिता । तथा च ‘ एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽत्मा न प्रकाशते । दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । १२) इति वैष्णवस्य परमपदस्य दुरवगमत्वमुक्त्वा तदवगमार्थं योगं दर्शयति — ‘ यच्छेद्वाङ्मनसी प्राज्ञस्तद्यच्छेज्ज्ञान आत्मनि । ज्ञानमात्मनि महति नियच्छेत्तद्यच्छेच्छान्त आत्मनि ’ (क. उ. १ । ३ । १३) इति । एतदुक्तं भवति — वाचं मनसि संयच्छेत् वागादिबाह्येन्द्रियव्यापारमुत्सृज्य मनोमात्रेणावतिष्ठेत । मनोऽपि विषयविकल्पाभिमुखं विकल्पदोषदर्शनेन ज्ञानशब्दोदितायां बुद्धावध्यवसायस्वभावायां धारयेत् तामपि बुद्धिं महत्यात्मनि भोक्तरि अग्र्यायां वा बुद्धौ सूक्ष्मतापादनेन नियच्छेत् महान्तं त्वात्मानं शान्त आत्मनि प्रकरणवति परस्मिन्पुरुषे परस्यां काष्ठायां प्रतिष्ठापयेदिति । तदेवं पूर्वापरालोचनायां नास्त्यत्र परपरिकल्पितस्य प्रधानस्यावकाशः ॥ १ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ānumānika mapi anumānanirūpitamapi pradhānam , ekeṣāṃ śākhināṃ śabdavadupalabhyate ; kāṭhake hi paṭhyate — ‘ mahataḥ paramavyaktamavyaktātpuruṣaḥ paraḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 11) iti ; tatra ya eva yannāmāno yatkramāśca mahadavyaktapuruṣāḥ smṛtiprasiddhāḥ , ta eveha pratyabhijñāyante | tatrāvyaktamiti smṛtiprasiddheḥ , śabdādi hīnatvācca na vyaktamavyaktamiti vyutpattisambhavāt , smṛtiprasiddhaṃ pradhānamabhidhīyate | atastasya śabdavattvādaśabdatvamanupapannam | tadeva ca jagataḥ kāraṇaṃ śrutismṛtinyāyaprasiddhibhya iti cet , naita devam — na hye tatkāṭhakavākyaṃ smṛtiprasiddhayormahadavyaktayorastitvaparam | na hyatra yādṛśaṃ smṛtiprasiddhaṃ svatantraṃ kāraṇaṃ triguṇaṃ pradhānam , tādṛśaṃ pratyabhijñāyate | śabdamātraṃ hyatrāvyaktamiti pratyabhijñāyate | sa ca śabdaḥ — na vyaktamavyaktamiti — yaugikatvāt anyasminnapi sūkṣme sudurlakṣye ca prayujyate | na cā yaṃ kasmiṃścidrūḍhaḥ | yā tu pradhānavādināṃ rūḍhiḥ , sā teṣāmeva pāribhāṣikī satī na vedārthanirūpaṇe kāraṇabhāvaṃ pratipadyate | na ca krama mātrasāmānyātsamānārthapratipattirbhavati , asati tadrūpapratyabhijñāne | na hya śvasthāne gāṃ paśyannaśvo'yamityamūḍho'dhyavasyati | prakaraṇa nirūpaṇāyāṃ cātra na paraparikalpitaṃ pradhānaṃ pratīyate , śarīrarūpakavinyastagṛhīteḥ | śarīraṃ hyatra ratharūpakavinyastamavyaktaśabdena parigṛhyate | kutaḥ ? prakaraṇāt pariśeṣācca | tathā hya nantarātīto grantha ātma śarīrādīnāṃ rathirathādirūpakakḷptiṃ darśayati — ‘ ātmānaṃ rathinaṃ viddhi śarīraṃ rathameva tu | buddhiṃ tu sārathiṃ viddhi manaḥ pragrahameva ca ||’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 3) ‘ indriyāṇi hayānāhurviṣayāṃsteṣu gocarān | ātmendriyamanoyuktaṃ bhoktetyāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 4) iti | tai ścendriyādibhirasaṃyataiḥ saṃsāramadhigacchati , saṃyataistvadhvanaḥ pāraṃ tadviṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padamāpnoti iti darśayitvā , kiṃ tadadhvanaḥ pāraṃ viṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padamityasyāmākāṅkṣāyām , tebhya eva prakṛtebhyaḥ indriyādibhyaḥ paratvena paramātmānamadhvanaḥ pāraṃ viṣṇoḥ paramaṃ padaṃ darśayati — ‘ indriyebhyaḥ parā hyarthā arthebhyaśca paraṃ manaḥ | manasastu parā buddhirbuddherātmā mahānparaḥ ||’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 10) ‘ mahataḥ paramavyaktamavyaktātpuruṣaḥ paraḥ | puruṣānna paraṃ kiñcitsā kāṣṭhā sā parā gatiḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 11) iti ; tatra ya e vendriyādayaḥ pūrvasyāṃ ratharūpakakalpanāyāmaśvādibhāvena prakṛtāḥ , ta eveha parigṛhyante , prakṛtahānāprakṛtaprakriyāparihārāya | tatra indriya manobuddhayastāvatpūrvatra iha ca samānaśabdā eva | arthā stu ye śabdādayo viṣayā indriyahayagocaratvena nirdiṣṭāḥ , teṣāṃ cendriyebhyaḥ paratvam , indriyāṇāṃ grahatvaṃ viṣayāṇāmatigrahatvam iti śrutiprasiddheḥ | viṣayebhyaśca manasaḥ paratvam , manomūlatvādviṣayendriyavyavahārasya | manasastu parā buddhiḥ — buddhiṃ hyāruhya bhogyajātaṃ bhoktāramupasarpati | buddherātmā mahānparaḥ — yaḥ , saḥ ‘ ātmānaṃ rathinaṃ viddhi ’ iti rathitvenopakṣiptaḥ | kutaḥ ? ātmaśabdāt , bhoktuśca bhogopakaraṇātparatvopapatteḥ | mahattvaṃ cāsya svāmitvādupapannam | athavā — ‘ mano mahānmatirbrahmā pūrbuddhiḥ khyātirīśvaraḥ | prajñā saṃviccitiścaiva smṛtiśca paripaṭhyate ’ iti smṛteḥ , ‘ yo brahmāṇaṃ vidadhāti pūrvaṃ yo vai vedāṃśca prahiṇoti tasmai ’ (śve. u. 6 | 18) iti ca śruteḥ , yā prathamajasya hiraṇyagarbhasya buddhiḥ , sā sarvāsāṃ buddhīnāṃ paramā pratiṣṭhā ; seha mahānātmetyucyate | sā ca pūrvatra buddhigrahaṇenaiva gṛhītā satī hirugihopadiśyate , tasyā apyasmadīyābhyo buddhibhyaḥ paratvopapatteḥ | etasmiṃstu pakṣe paramātmaviṣayeṇaiva pareṇa puruṣagrahaṇena rathina ātmano grahaṇaṃ draṣṭavyam , paramārthataḥ paramātmavijñānātmanorbhedābhāvāt | tadevaṃ śarīramevaikaṃ pariśiṣyate teṣu | itarā ṇīndriyādīni prakṛtānyeva paramapadadidarśayiṣayā samanukrāmanpariśiṣyamāṇenehāntyenāvyaktaśabdena pariśiṣyamāṇaṃ prakṛtaṃ śarīraṃ darśayatīti gamyate | śarī rendriyamanobuddhiviṣayavedanāsaṃyuktasya hyavidyāvato bhoktuḥ śarīrādīnāṃ rathādirūpakakalpanayā saṃsāramokṣagatinirūpaṇena pratyagātmabrahmāvagatiriha vivakṣitā | tathā ca ‘ eṣa sarveṣu bhūteṣu gūḍho'tmā na prakāśate | dṛśyate tvagryayā buddhyā sūkṣmayā sūkṣmadarśibhiḥ ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 12) iti vaiṣṇavasya paramapadasya duravagamatvamuktvā tadavagamārthaṃ yogaṃ darśayati — ‘ yacchedvāṅmanasī prājñastadyacchejjñāna ātmani | jñānamātmani mahati niyacchettadyacchecchānta ātmani ’ (ka. u. 1 | 3 | 13) iti | etaduktaṃ bhavati — vācaṃ manasi saṃyacchet vāgādibāhyendriyavyāpāramutsṛjya manomātreṇāvatiṣṭheta | mano'pi viṣayavikalpābhimukhaṃ vikalpadoṣadarśanena jñānaśabdoditāyāṃ buddhāvadhyavasāyasvabhāvāyāṃ dhārayet tāmapi buddhiṃ mahatyātmani bhoktari agryāyāṃ vā buddhau sūkṣmatāpādanena niyacchet mahāntaṃ tvātmānaṃ śānta ātmani prakaraṇavati parasminpuruṣe parasyāṃ kāṣṭhāyāṃ pratiṣṭhāpayediti | tadevaṃ pūrvāparālocanāyāṃ nāstyatra paraparikalpitasya pradhānasyāvakāśaḥ || 1 ||

records.literal_translation: One may argue that the inferentially established Pradhana is also heard as scriptural in some schools, citing the Katha text "beyond the great is the unmanifest; beyond the unmanifest is the Person"; the terms great, unmanifest, and person then seem recognizable in their Smriti sense, so "unmanifest" would denote Pradhana and the claim of non-scripturality would fail. The reply: no. That Katha passage does not teach the independent tri-guna Pradhana known from Smriti. Only the bare word "unmanifest" is recognized, and as a yaugika term it can apply elsewhere to what is subtle and hard to grasp; it is not fixed here by Vedic usage to Sakhya's technical meaning. Sequence alone does not establish same meaning without real identity of referent. In this context, shaped by the chariot allegory, "unmanifest" is taken as body in its allegorical placement, established by context and residual indication. The surrounding passage explicitly lays out self, body, intellect, mind, senses, and objects through the chariot image, then ascends beyond them toward the supreme goal. In that ascent, the previously introduced terms are retained to avoid dropping the topic and importing another process; thus "objects", "mind", "intellect", "great self", and "unmanifest" are interpreted within that same chain. The teaching intention is inner-self/Brahman realization through analysis of bondage and release, not establishment of an alien Pradhana doctrine.
records.technical_translation: Major anti-pradhana exegesis of Katha 1.3: Shankara rejects lexical borrowing from Smriti technical usage and enforces prakarana + parisesa + ekavakyata constraints. Avyakta is interpreted intra-contextually within the ratha-rupaka progression toward parama-pada, preserving Vedantic intent and blocking Sankhya reification.

records.source_text: आनुमानिकमप्येकेषामिति चेन्न शरीररूपकविन्यस्तगृहीतेर्दर्शयति च ॥ १ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ānumānikamapyekeṣāmiti cenna śarīrarūpakavinyastagṛhīterdarśayati ca || 1 ||
records.transliteration: ānumānikamapyekeṣāmiti cenna śarīrarūpakavinyastagṛhīterdarśayati ca || 1 ||
records.literal_translation: If it be said that for some people the inferential entity is scripturally taught, no: because what is taken up is arranged in the body-metaphor, and this is also shown. (1)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.1 denies that Katha's avyakta validates Sankhya Pradhana; the referent is fixed by ratha-rupaka contextual uptake.
