records.source_text: प्रकृतिश्च प्रतिज्ञादृष्टान्तानुपरोधात् ॥ २३ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: prakṛtiśca pratijñādṛṣṭāntānuparodhāt || 23 ||
records.transliteration: prakṛtiśca pratijñādṛṣṭāntānuparodhāt || 23 ||
records.literal_translation: And Brahman is the material cause, because otherwise proposition and example would be obstructed. (23)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.23: Brahman must be read as material cause as well, else key scriptural proposition and illustrative reasoning fail.

records.source_text: अभिध्योपदेशश्चात्मनः कर्तृत्वप्रकृतित्वे गमयति — ‘ सोऽकामयत बहु स्यां प्रजायेय ’ (तै. उ. २ । ६ । १) इति , ‘ तदैक्षत बहु स्यां प्रजायेय ’ (छा. उ. ६ । २ । ३) इति च । तत्राभिध्यानपूर्विकायाः स्वातन्त्र्यप्रवृत्तेः कर्तेति गम्यते । बहु स्यामिति प्रत्यगात्मविषयत्वाद्बहुभवनाभिध्यानस्य प्रकृतिरित्यपि गम्यते ॥ २४ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: abhidhyopadeśaścātmanaḥ kartṛtvaprakṛtitve gamayati — ‘ so'kāmayata bahu syāṃ prajāyeya ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) iti , ‘ tadaikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeya ’ (chā. u. 6 | 2 | 3) iti ca | tatrābhidhyānapūrvikāyāḥ svātantryapravṛtteḥ karteti gamyate | bahu syāmiti pratyagātmaviṣayatvādbahubhavanābhidhyānasya prakṛtirityapi gamyate || 24 ||
records.transliteration: abhidhyopadeśaścātmanaḥ kartṛtvaprakṛtitve gamayati — ‘ so'kāmayata bahu syāṃ prajāyeya ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) iti , ‘ tadaikṣata bahu syāṃ prajāyeya ’ (chā. u. 6 | 2 | 3) iti ca | tatrābhidhyānapūrvikāyāḥ svātantryapravṛtteḥ karteti gamyate | bahu syāmiti pratyagātmaviṣayatvādbahubhavanābhidhyānasya prakṛtirityapi gamyate || 24 ||
records.literal_translation: Instruction about deliberate willing also indicates that the Self is both agent and material basis: statements like 'May I become many, may I be born.' Deliberative initiative reveals agency; and since the same reality becomes the plurality, material causality is also implied.
records.technical_translation: Abhidhya-upadesa supports dual causal status: volitional autonomy marks nimitta-kartṛtva, while self-manifestation into multiplicity indicates upadana identity.

records.source_text: प्रकृतित्वस्याय मभ्युच्चयः । इतश्च प्रकृतिर्ब्रह्म , यत्कारणं साक्षाद्ब्रह्मैव कारणमुपादाय उभौ प्रभवप्रलयावाम्नायेते — ‘ सर्वाणि ह वा इमानि भूतान्याकाशादेव समुत्पद्यन्त आकाशं प्रत्यस्तं यन्ति ’ (छा. उ. १ । ९ । १) इति । यद्धि यस्मात्प्रभवति , यस्मिंश्च प्रलीयते तत्तस्योपादानं प्रसिद्धम् , यथा व्रीहियवादीनां पृथिवी । ‘ साक्षात् ’ इति च — उपादानान्तरानुपादानं दर्शयति ‘ आकाशादेव ’ इति । प्रत्यस्तमयश्च नोपादानादन्यत्र कार्यस्य दृष्टः ॥ २५ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: prakṛtitvasyāya mabhyuccayaḥ | itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ sākṣādbrahmaiva kāraṇamupādāya ubhau prabhavapralayāvāmnāyete — ‘ sarvāṇi ha vā imāni bhūtānyākāśādeva samutpadyanta ākāśaṃ pratyastaṃ yanti ’ (chā. u. 1 | 9 | 1) iti | yaddhi yasmātprabhavati , yasmiṃśca pralīyate tattasyopādānaṃ prasiddham , yathā vrīhiyavādīnāṃ pṛthivī | ‘ sākṣāt ’ iti ca — upādānāntarānupādānaṃ darśayati ‘ ākāśādeva ’ iti | pratyastamayaśca nopādānādanyatra kāryasya dṛṣṭaḥ || 25 ||
records.transliteration: prakṛtitvasyāya mabhyuccayaḥ | itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ sākṣādbrahmaiva kāraṇamupādāya ubhau prabhavapralayāvāmnāyete — ‘ sarvāṇi ha vā imāni bhūtānyākāśādeva samutpadyanta ākāśaṃ pratyastaṃ yanti ’ (chā. u. 1 | 9 | 1) iti | yaddhi yasmātprabhavati , yasmiṃśca pralīyate tattasyopādānaṃ prasiddham , yathā vrīhiyavādīnāṃ pṛthivī | ‘ sākṣāt ’ iti ca — upādānāntarānupādānaṃ darśayati ‘ ākāśādeva ’ iti | pratyastamayaśca nopādānādanyatra kāryasya dṛṣṭaḥ || 25 ||
records.literal_translation: This is an additional proof of Brahman's material causality: scripture directly states both emergence and dissolution with Brahman itself as locus, as in passages saying all beings arise from and return to that very ground.
records.technical_translation: Abhyuccaya argument: direct two-way scriptural predication (origin and reabsorption) centered in Brahman implies upadana identity rather than external substrate dependence.

records.source_text: इतश्च प्रकृतिर्ब्रह्म , यत्कारणं ब्रह्मप्रक्रियायाम् ‘ तदात्मानं स्वयमकुरुत ’ (तै. उ. २ । ७ । १) इत्यात्मनः कर्मत्वं कर्तृत्वं च दर्शयति ; आत्मानमिति कर्मत्वम् , स्वयमकुरुतेति कर्तृत्वम् । कथं पुनः पूर्वसिद्धस्य सतः कर्तृत्वेन व्यवस्थितस्य क्रियमाणत्वं शक्यं सम्पादयितुम् ? परिणामा दिति ब्रूमः — पूर्वसिद्धोऽपि हि सन्नात्मा विशेषेण विकारात्मना परिणमयामा सात्मान मिति । विकारात्मना च परिणामो मृदाद्यासु प्रकृतिषूपलब्धः । स्वयमिति च विशेषणान्निमित्तान्तरानपेक्षत्वमपि प्रतीयते । ‘ परिणामात् ’ इति वा पृथक्सूत्रम् । तस्यैषोऽर्थः — इतश्च प्रकृतिर्ब्रह्म , यत्कारणं ब्रह्मण एव विकारात्मना परिणामः सामानाधिकरण्येनाम्नायते ‘ सच्च त्यच्चाभवत् । निरुक्तं चानिरुक्तं च ’ (तै. उ. २ । ६ । १) इत्यादिनेति ॥ २६ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahmaprakriyāyām ‘ tadātmānaṃ svayamakuruta ’ (tai. u. 2 | 7 | 1) ityātmanaḥ karmatvaṃ kartṛtvaṃ ca darśayati ; ātmānamiti karmatvam , svayamakuruteti kartṛtvam | kathaṃ punaḥ pūrvasiddhasya sataḥ kartṛtvena vyavasthitasya kriyamāṇatvaṃ śakyaṃ sampādayitum ? pariṇāmā diti brūmaḥ — pūrvasiddho'pi hi sannātmā viśeṣeṇa vikārātmanā pariṇamayāmā sātmāna miti | vikārātmanā ca pariṇāmo mṛdādyāsu prakṛtiṣūpalabdhaḥ | svayamiti ca viśeṣaṇānnimittāntarānapekṣatvamapi pratīyate | ‘ pariṇāmāt ’ iti vā pṛthaksūtram | tasyaiṣo'rthaḥ — itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahmaṇa eva vikārātmanā pariṇāmaḥ sāmānādhikaraṇyenāmnāyate ‘ sacca tyaccābhavat | niruktaṃ cāniruktaṃ ca ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) ityādineti || 26 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahmaprakriyāyām ‘ tadātmānaṃ svayamakuruta ’ (tai. u. 2 | 7 | 1) ityātmanaḥ karmatvaṃ kartṛtvaṃ ca darśayati ; ātmānamiti karmatvam , svayamakuruteti kartṛtvam | kathaṃ punaḥ pūrvasiddhasya sataḥ kartṛtvena vyavasthitasya kriyamāṇatvaṃ śakyaṃ sampādayitum ? pariṇāmā diti brūmaḥ — pūrvasiddho'pi hi sannātmā viśeṣeṇa vikārātmanā pariṇamayāmā sātmāna miti | vikārātmanā ca pariṇāmo mṛdādyāsu prakṛtiṣūpalabdhaḥ | svayamiti ca viśeṣaṇānnimittāntarānapekṣatvamapi pratīyate | ‘ pariṇāmāt ’ iti vā pṛthaksūtram | tasyaiṣo'rthaḥ — itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahmaṇa eva vikārātmanā pariṇāmaḥ sāmānādhikaraṇyenāmnāyate ‘ sacca tyaccābhavat | niruktaṃ cāniruktaṃ ca ’ (tai. u. 2 | 6 | 1) ityādineti || 26 ||
records.literal_translation: Further, in Brahman-process passages such as 'it made itself,' scripture shows both object-status and agent-status with respect to the self: itself as what is made, and made as agency. This is understood as self-referential causal teaching, not as two independent entities.
records.technical_translation: Atmakrti argument: karmata and kartṛta in a single self-referential construction supports self-transformation framing and hence upadana-nimitta unity in Brahman.

records.source_text: आत्मकृतेः परिणामात् ॥ २६ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ātmakṛteḥ pariṇāmāt || 26 ||
records.transliteration: ātmakṛteḥ pariṇāmāt || 26 ||
records.literal_translation: Because of self-making, through transformation. (26)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.26: self-referential creation statements indicate transformative self-causation, supporting Brahman as material cause.

records.source_text: इतश्च प्रकृतिर्ब्रह्म , यत्कारणं ब्रह्म योनिरित्यपि पठ्यते वेदान्तेषु — ‘ कर्तारमीशं पुरुषं ब्रह्मयोनिम् ’ (मु. उ. ३ । १ । ३) इति ‘ यद्भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति धीराः ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ६) इति च । योनिशब्दश्च प्रकृतिवचनः समधिगतो लोके — ‘ पृथिवी योनिरोषधिवनस्पतीनाम् ’ इति । स्त्रीयोने रप्यस्त्येवावयवद्वारेण गर्भं प्रत्युपादानकारणत्वम् । क्वचि त्स्थानवचनोऽपि योनिशब्दो दृष्टः ‘ योनिष्ट इन्द्र निषदे अकारि ’ (ऋ. सं. १ । १०४ । १) इति । वाक्यशेषात्त्वत्र प्रकृतिवचनता परिगृह्यते — ‘ यथोर्णनाभिः सृजते गृह्णते च ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ७) इत्येवंजातीयकात् । तदेवं प्रकृतित्वं ब्रह्मणः प्रसिद्धम् । यत्पुन रिदमुक्तम् , ईक्षापूर्वकं कर्तृत्वं निमित्तकारणेष्वेव कुलालादिषु लोके दृष्टम् , नोपादानेष्वित्यादि ; तत्प्रत्युच्यते — न लोकवदिह भवितव्यम् । न ह्य यमनुमानगम्योऽर्थः । शब्दगम्यत्वात्त्वस्यार्थस्य यथाशब्दमिह भवितव्यम् । शब्दश्चेक्षितुरीश्वरस्य प्रकृतित्वं प्रतिपादयतीत्यवोचाम । पुन श्चैतत्सर्वं विस्तरेण प्रतिवक्ष्यामः ॥ २७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahma yonirityapi paṭhyate vedānteṣu — ‘ kartāramīśaṃ puruṣaṃ brahmayonim ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 3) iti ‘ yadbhūtayoniṃ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 6) iti ca | yoniśabdaśca prakṛtivacanaḥ samadhigato loke — ‘ pṛthivī yoniroṣadhivanaspatīnām ’ iti | strīyone rapyastyevāvayavadvāreṇa garbhaṃ pratyupādānakāraṇatvam | kvaci tsthānavacano'pi yoniśabdo dṛṣṭaḥ ‘ yoniṣṭa indra niṣade akāri ’ (ṛ. saṃ. 1 | 104 | 1) iti | vākyaśeṣāttvatra prakṛtivacanatā parigṛhyate — ‘ yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 7) ityevaṃjātīyakāt | tadevaṃ prakṛtitvaṃ brahmaṇaḥ prasiddham | yatpuna ridamuktam , īkṣāpūrvakaṃ kartṛtvaṃ nimittakāraṇeṣveva kulālādiṣu loke dṛṣṭam , nopādāneṣvityādi ; tatpratyucyate — na lokavadiha bhavitavyam | na hya yamanumānagamyo'rthaḥ | śabdagamyatvāttvasyārthasya yathāśabdamiha bhavitavyam | śabdaścekṣiturīśvarasya prakṛtitvaṃ pratipādayatītyavocāma | puna ścaitatsarvaṃ vistareṇa prativakṣyāmaḥ || 27 ||
records.transliteration: itaśca prakṛtirbrahma , yatkāraṇaṃ brahma yonirityapi paṭhyate vedānteṣu — ‘ kartāramīśaṃ puruṣaṃ brahmayonim ’ (mu. u. 3 | 1 | 3) iti ‘ yadbhūtayoniṃ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 6) iti ca | yoniśabdaśca prakṛtivacanaḥ samadhigato loke — ‘ pṛthivī yoniroṣadhivanaspatīnām ’ iti | strīyone rapyastyevāvayavadvāreṇa garbhaṃ pratyupādānakāraṇatvam | kvaci tsthānavacano'pi yoniśabdo dṛṣṭaḥ ‘ yoniṣṭa indra niṣade akāri ’ (ṛ. saṃ. 1 | 104 | 1) iti | vākyaśeṣāttvatra prakṛtivacanatā parigṛhyate — ‘ yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 7) ityevaṃjātīyakāt | tadevaṃ prakṛtitvaṃ brahmaṇaḥ prasiddham | yatpuna ridamuktam , īkṣāpūrvakaṃ kartṛtvaṃ nimittakāraṇeṣveva kulālādiṣu loke dṛṣṭam , nopādāneṣvityādi ; tatpratyucyate — na lokavadiha bhavitavyam | na hya yamanumānagamyo'rthaḥ | śabdagamyatvāttvasyārthasya yathāśabdamiha bhavitavyam | śabdaścekṣiturīśvarasya prakṛtitvaṃ pratipādayatītyavocāma | puna ścaitatsarvaṃ vistareṇa prativakṣyāmaḥ || 27 ||
records.literal_translation: Further, Brahman is material cause because Vedanta also sings of Brahman as source or womb (yoni), in passages like 'the Lord, the maker, the person, Brahma-yoni' and 'the wise behold the source of beings.' The word yoni denotes generative source and material basis.
records.technical_translation: Yoni-shabda evidence: scriptural attribution of yoni to Brahman carries source-substrate force, reinforcing upadana status beyond merely efficient lordship.

records.source_text: योनिश्च हि गीयते ॥ २७ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: yoniśca hi gīyate || 27 ||
records.transliteration: yoniśca hi gīyate || 27 ||
records.literal_translation: For indeed source (yoni) is sung of it. (27)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.27: Brahman's designation as yoni in shruti supports material-causal interpretation.

records.source_text: ‘ ईक्षते र्नाशब्दम् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । ५) इत्यारभ्य प्रधानकारणवादः सूत्रैरेव पुनः पुनराशङ्क्य निराकृतः — तस्य हि पक्षस्योपोद्बलकानि कानिचिल्लिङ्गाभासानि वेदान्तेष्वापातेन मन्दमतीन्प्रति भान्तीति । स च कार्यकारणानन्यत्वाभ्युपगमात्प्रत्यासन्नो वेदान्तवादस्य देवलप्रभृतिभिश्च कैश्चिद्धर्मसूत्रकारैः स्वग्रन्थेष्वाश्रितः । तेन तत्प्रतिषेधे यत्नोऽतीव कृतः , नाण्वादिकारणवादप्रतिषेधे । तेऽपि तु ब्रह्मकारणवादपक्षस्य प्रतिपक्षत्वात्प्रतिषेद्धव्याः । तेषा मप्युपोद्बलकं वैदिकं किञ्चिल्लिङ्गमापातेन मन्दमतीन्प्रति भायादिति । अतः प्रधानमल्लनिबर्हणन्यायेनातिदिशति — एतेन प्रधानकारणवादप्रतिषेधन्यायकलापेन सर्वेऽण्वादिकारणवादा अपि प्रतिषिद्धतया व्याख्याता वेदितव्याः । तेषामपि प्रधानवदशब्दत्वाच्छब्दविरोधित्वाच्चेति । व्याख्याता व्याख्याता इति पदाभ्यासोऽध्यायपरिसमाप्तिं द्योतयति ॥ २८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: ‘ īkṣate rnāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) ityārabhya pradhānakāraṇavādaḥ sūtraireva punaḥ punarāśaṅkya nirākṛtaḥ — tasya hi pakṣasyopodbalakāni kānicilliṅgābhāsāni vedānteṣvāpātena mandamatīnprati bhāntīti | sa ca kāryakāraṇānanyatvābhyupagamātpratyāsanno vedāntavādasya devalaprabhṛtibhiśca kaiściddharmasūtrakāraiḥ svagrantheṣvāśritaḥ | tena tatpratiṣedhe yatno'tīva kṛtaḥ , nāṇvādikāraṇavādapratiṣedhe | te'pi tu brahmakāraṇavādapakṣasya pratipakṣatvātpratiṣeddhavyāḥ | teṣā mapyupodbalakaṃ vaidikaṃ kiñcilliṅgamāpātena mandamatīnprati bhāyāditi | ataḥ pradhānamallanibarhaṇanyāyenātidiśati — etena pradhānakāraṇavādapratiṣedhanyāyakalāpena sarve'ṇvādikāraṇavādā api pratiṣiddhatayā vyākhyātā veditavyāḥ | teṣāmapi pradhānavadaśabdatvācchabdavirodhitvācceti | vyākhyātā vyākhyātā iti padābhyāso'dhyāyaparisamāptiṃ dyotayati || 28 ||
records.transliteration: ‘ īkṣate rnāśabdam ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 5) ityārabhya pradhānakāraṇavādaḥ sūtraireva punaḥ punarāśaṅkya nirākṛtaḥ — tasya hi pakṣasyopodbalakāni kānicilliṅgābhāsāni vedānteṣvāpātena mandamatīnprati bhāntīti | sa ca kāryakāraṇānanyatvābhyupagamātpratyāsanno vedāntavādasya devalaprabhṛtibhiśca kaiściddharmasūtrakāraiḥ svagrantheṣvāśritaḥ | tena tatpratiṣedhe yatno'tīva kṛtaḥ , nāṇvādikāraṇavādapratiṣedhe | te'pi tu brahmakāraṇavādapakṣasya pratipakṣatvātpratiṣeddhavyāḥ | teṣā mapyupodbalakaṃ vaidikaṃ kiñcilliṅgamāpātena mandamatīnprati bhāyāditi | ataḥ pradhānamallanibarhaṇanyāyenātidiśati — etena pradhānakāraṇavādapratiṣedhanyāyakalāpena sarve'ṇvādikāraṇavādā api pratiṣiddhatayā vyākhyātā veditavyāḥ | teṣāmapi pradhānavadaśabdatvācchabdavirodhitvācceti | vyākhyātā vyākhyātā iti padābhyāso'dhyāyaparisamāptiṃ dyotayati || 28 ||
records.literal_translation: From 'Ikshater na asabdam' onward, the doctrine of pradhana as cause has been repeatedly raised and refuted, because certain seeming indicators in Vedanta can mislead less discriminating readers at first glance. Once cause-effect non-difference and Brahman's complete causal status are established, those residual semblances are exhausted by the same reasoning.
records.technical_translation: Meta-conclusion block: prior anti-pradhana refutations are reaffirmed as globally reusable. Apparent linga-abhasas are contextually defeasible once Brahman's upadana-nimitta unity and karya-karana-ananyatva are secured.

records.source_text: एतेन सर्वे व्याख्याता व्याख्याताः ॥ २८ ॥
records.transliteration_iast: etena sarve vyākhyātā vyākhyātāḥ || 28 ||
records.transliteration: etena sarve vyākhyātā vyākhyātāḥ || 28 ||
records.literal_translation: By this, all such cases are explained; indeed they are explained. (28)
records.technical_translation: Sutra 1.4.28: the established reasoning is sufficient to interpret all remaining similar passages and objections.
